# Executive Summary

This report estimates and forecasts **modern contraceptive prevalence rate (mCPR)** trajectories using a **hierarchical Bayesian logistic growth model**.

**Model setup**

a) Country trajectories follow a logistic growth shape (slow start, rapid growth, then plateau).
b) Each region has a fixed ceiling \(K_{r(i)}\), where \(0 < K_{r(i)} < 1\), and countries inherit the ceiling of their analytic region.
c) Each country has its own inflection timing \(t_{0,i}\), the point of fastest growth.
d) Countries share information through partial pooling, improving stability for sparse series.
e) Speed varies at the country level (\(s_i\)), with region-anchored priors, so country heterogeneity is expressed through timing and growth speed while the ceiling stays region-fixed to address \(K\)-\(s\) identifiability.

**Decision framing (as of 2026)**

a) **Scale-up**: high expected growth with substantial room to grow.
b) **Build / monitor**: mixed profile (moderate growth and/or moderate room).
c) **Maintain / equity**: low growth and/or near saturation, with focus on equity and access gaps.

# Theoretical Framework

## Logistic growth model

For country \(i\) at time \(t\), expected mCPR is:

\[
p_i(t) = \frac{K_{r(i)}}{1 + \exp\!\left(-\frac{t - t_{0,i}}{s_i}\right)}
\]

where:
a) \(K_{r(i)} \in (0,1)\) is the long-run ceiling (saturation level) for country \(i\)'s region \(r(i)\),
b) \(t_{0,i}\) is the inflection time (the year when growth is fastest),
c) \(s_i > 0\) is the country-specific speed parameter.

A key decision quantity is the instantaneous growth rate:

\[
\frac{d p_i(t)}{dt}
= \frac{K_{r(i)}}{s_i}
\cdot
\frac{
\exp\!\left(-\frac{t - t_{0,i}}{s_i}\right)
}{
\left[1+\exp\!\left(-\frac{t - t_{0,i}}{s_i}\right)\right]^2
}
\]

This peaks near \(t=t_{0,i}\) and shrinks as the curve saturates.

# Setup and Data Preparation

```{r libraries}
# Load all packages used for data prep, Bayesian modeling, diagnostics, and plotting.
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(brms)
library(ggplot2)
library(minpack.lm)
library(posterior)
library(ggrepel)
library(readxl)

# Use development settings by default. Switch `final_run` to TRUE only when
# you want the longer production-quality sampler settings.
final_run <- FALSE
run_profile_label <- if (final_run) "final" else "dev"

main_fit_iter <- if (final_run) 6000 else 3000
main_fit_warmup <- if (final_run) 3000 else 1500
main_fit_control <- if (final_run) {
  list(adapt_delta = 0.995, max_treedepth = 15)
} else {
  list(adapt_delta = 0.99, max_treedepth = 12)
}

holdout_fit_iter <- if (final_run) 4000 else 2000
holdout_fit_warmup <- if (final_run) 2000 else 1000
holdout_fit_control <- main_fit_control

appendix_fixeds_iter <- if (final_run) 3000 else 2000
appendix_fixeds_warmup <- if (final_run) 1500 else 1000

# Copied project snapshots can produce tiny timestamp differences between
# inputs and cached fits even when they come from the same run. Allow a short
# grace window before forcing an expensive Stan recompile.
cache_mtime_tolerance_secs <- 10

# Keep refits manual by default so report edits or copied-file timestamps do not
# trigger Stan compilation on a new machine unless we explicitly want it.
force_model_refit <- FALSE
force_validation_refit <- FALSE

cat("Run profile:", run_profile_label, "\n")
cat("Main fit iterations / warmup:", main_fit_iter, "/", main_fit_warmup, "\n")
```

```{r helpers}
# Convenience helper for inverse-logit transform.
inv_logit <- function(x) 1 / (1 + exp(-x))
ci_lower <- 0.025
ci_upper <- 0.975
ci_label <- "95%"

# Safely evaluate country-by-draw logistic trajectories at a given calendar year.
# Using an explicit column-wise helper avoids silent alignment issues when these
# matrices are subset and reused deep in the SSA goal sections.
logistic_draws_at_year <- function(K_mat, t0_mat, s_mat, year_value) {
  out <- vapply(
    seq_len(ncol(K_mat)),
    function(j) {
      K_mat[, j] / (1 + exp(-(((year_value - 2000) - t0_mat[, j]) / s_mat[, j])))
    },
    numeric(nrow(K_mat))
  )
  if (is.null(dim(out))) {
    out <- matrix(out, ncol = 1)
  }
  colnames(out) <- colnames(K_mat)
  out
}
```

```{r load-data}
# Read model input data and construct binomial outcomes used by brms.
mcpr_model_data <- read.csv("mcpr_data.csv")

# Simple survey count by country, then enforce minimum panel depth.
country_survey_counts <- mcpr_model_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(country), !is.na(year), !is.na(mCPR)) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(
    n_surveys = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_surveys), country)

knitr::kable(
  country_survey_counts,
  caption = "Survey count by country (raw input, before filtering)"
)

eligible_countries <- country_survey_counts %>%
  filter(n_surveys >= 2) %>%
  select(country)

cat("Countries in raw input:", n_distinct(mcpr_model_data$country), "\n")
cat("Countries with >=2 surveys kept for analysis:", nrow(eligible_countries), "\n")

# Filter out countries with fewer than 2 surveys for the entire downstream analysis.
mcpr_model_data <- mcpr_model_data %>%
  semi_join(eligible_countries, by = "country")

# Stable region code ordering used throughout priors, model matrix terms, and plots.
# The report now uses eight analytic regions: the original five non-SSA regions,
# plus West Africa, Central Africa, and East/Southern Africa.
region_label_lookup <- tibble::tribble(
  ~region, ~region_prior,
  "Central Africa", "CAF",
  "East Asia and Pacific", "EAP",
  "East/Southern Africa", "ESA",
  "Europe and Central Asia", "ECA",
  "Latin America and Carribbean", "LAC",
  "Middle East and North Africa", "MENA",
  "South Asia", "SA",
  "West Africa", "WAF"
)

region_prior_levels <- region_label_lookup$region_prior

# A consistent palette keeps region-level visuals aligned across the report.
region_palette <- c(
  "Central Africa" = "#8C564B",
  "East Asia and Pacific" = "#1B9E77",
  "East/Southern Africa" = "#4C78A8",
  "Europe and Central Asia" = "#7570B3",
  "Latin America and Carribbean" = "#D95F02",
  "Middle East and North Africa" = "#E7298A",
  "South Asia" = "#66A61E",
  "West Africa" = "#E6AB02"
)

# Map each country to one of eight analytic regions using a fixed lookup.
# This mapping is intentionally explicit so regional summaries are reproducible.
region_lookup <- c(
  # East Asia and Pacific
  "Cambodia" = "East Asia and Pacific",
  "Indonesia" = "East Asia and Pacific",
  "Philippines" = "East Asia and Pacific",
  "Timor-Leste" = "East Asia and Pacific",
  "Vietnam" = "East Asia and Pacific",
  # Europe and Central Asia
  "Albania" = "Europe and Central Asia",
  "Armenia" = "Europe and Central Asia",
  "Kazakhstan" = "Europe and Central Asia",
  "Kyrgyz Republic" = "Europe and Central Asia",
  "Tajikistan" = "Europe and Central Asia",
  "Turkey" = "Europe and Central Asia",
  # Latin America and Carribbean
  "Bolivia" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Brazil" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Colombia" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Dominican Republic" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Guatemala" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Haiti" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Honduras" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Nicaragua" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  "Peru" = "Latin America and Carribbean",
  # Middle East and North Africa
  "Egypt" = "Middle East and North Africa",
  "Jordan" = "Middle East and North Africa",
  "Morocco" = "Middle East and North Africa",
  "Yemen" = "Middle East and North Africa",
  # South Asia
  "Bangladesh" = "South Asia",
  "India" = "South Asia",
  "Maldives" = "South Asia",
  "Nepal" = "South Asia",
  "Pakistan" = "South Asia",
  # West Africa
  "Benin" = "West Africa",
  "Burkina Faso" = "West Africa",
  "Cote d'Ivoire" = "West Africa",
  "Gambia" = "West Africa",
  "Ghana" = "West Africa",
  "Guinea" = "West Africa",
  "Liberia" = "West Africa",
  "Mali" = "West Africa",
  "Niger" = "West Africa",
  "Nigeria" = "West Africa",
  "Senegal" = "West Africa",
  "Sierra Leone" = "West Africa",
  "Togo" = "West Africa",
  # Central Africa
  "Cameroon" = "Central Africa",
  "Chad" = "Central Africa",
  "Congo" = "Central Africa",
  "Congo Democratic Republic" = "Central Africa",
  "Gabon" = "Central Africa",
  # East/Southern Africa
  "Angola" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Burundi" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Comoros" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Ethiopia" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Kenya" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Lesotho" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Madagascar" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Malawi" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Mozambique" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Namibia" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Rwanda" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "South Africa" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Tanzania" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Uganda" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Zambia" = "East/Southern Africa",
  "Zimbabwe" = "East/Southern Africa"
)

mcpr_logistic_data <- mcpr_model_data %>%
  mutate(
    row_id      = dplyr::row_number(),   # stable row identifier
    region      = unname(region_lookup[country]), # analytic region classification
    year_center = year - 2000,
    y           = round(mCPR * n_women),  # successes
    n           = round(n_women)          # trials
  ) %>%
  left_join(region_label_lookup, by = "region") %>%
  mutate(
    region_prior = factor(
      region_prior,
      levels = region_prior_levels
    )
  )

# Validate region mapping coverage; stop if any country was left unmapped.
unmapped_countries <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(is.na(region)) %>%
  distinct(country) %>%
  arrange(country) %>%
  pull(country)

if (length(unmapped_countries) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Unmapped countries in region_lookup: ",
    paste(unmapped_countries, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Validate region code creation for the modeling matrix.
if (any(is.na(mcpr_logistic_data$region_prior))) {
  stop("Missing region_prior codes after region mapping; check region_lookup labels.")
}

# Show which DHS countries fall into each analytic region used downstream.
dhs_region_membership <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(region, country) %>%
  arrange(region, country) %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    countries = paste(country, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(
  dhs_region_membership,
  caption = "DHS analytic regions and countries included in the report"
)

glimpse(mcpr_logistic_data)
```

**Denominator definition used throughout this report:**  
`mCPR` is computed from DHS IR files as modern method use among **all women age 15-49** (based on `v313`), not limited to currently married women. This matters when comparing to published DHS figures, which are often reported for currently married women.

# Exploratory Global Shape and Implied Growth (Observed DHS Data)

These are descriptive (pre-model) summaries from observed DHS country-survey points.

```{r exploratory-global-shape}
# Build descriptive yearly global means and a smooth trajectory for visual diagnostics.
global_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_mcpr = mean(mCPR, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year)

global_shape <- global_obs %>%
  mutate(
    mean_mcpr_smooth = as.numeric(stats::predict(
      loess(mean_mcpr ~ year, data = global_obs, span = 0.35),
      newdata = year
    )),
    implied_growth = (lead(mean_mcpr_smooth) - lag(mean_mcpr_smooth)) / (lead(year) - lag(year))
  )

ggplot(global_obs, aes(x = year, y = mean_mcpr)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.35, se = TRUE, linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Exploratory global DHS mCPR shape",
    subtitle = "Points are observed DHS yearly means; curve is a LOESS smoother",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean observed mCPR"
  )

ggplot(global_shape, aes(x = year, y = implied_growth)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Exploratory implied DHS growth rate over time",
    subtitle = "Numerical derivative of the smoothed global DHS mean trajectory",
    x = "Year",
    y = "d mCPR per year"
  )
```

## Exploratory country-level raw trajectories

```{r exploratory-country-raw}
# Select DHS countries with at least 5 surveys, then visualize the 20 deepest panels.
explore_countries <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(n_surveys = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_surveys >= 5) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_surveys), country) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(country)

country_raw <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(country %in% explore_countries) %>%
  arrange(country, year)

ggplot(country_raw, aes(x = year, y = mCPR, group = country)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 1.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ country, ncol = 5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  labs(
    title = "Exploratory raw DHS mCPR trajectories by country",
    subtitle = "Top 20 DHS countries with at least 5 surveys; points are raw observed mCPR",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Observed mCPR"
  )

# Show the remaining DHS countries in a compact regional view so all countries are visible.
remaining_country_raw <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(!country %in% explore_countries) %>%
  arrange(region, country, year)

if (nrow(remaining_country_raw) > 0) {
  ggplot(remaining_country_raw, aes(x = year, y = mCPR, group = country)) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.18, linewidth = 0.45, color = "#4C78A8") +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 0.7, color = "#4C78A8") +
    facet_wrap(~ region, ncol = 3) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    labs(
      title = "Remaining DHS countries: raw observed trajectories",
      subtitle = "All DHS countries outside the top-20 panel, overlaid within region facets",
      x = "Year",
      y = "Observed mCPR"
    )
}
```

# Optional: DHS Data-Informed Ceiling Checks (not used as a predictor)


In DHS-based logistic curves, the ceiling \(K_i\) can be hard to pin down if a country has few surveys or never reaches a plateau. A quick per-country nonlinear fit can give a **rough sense** of plausible ceilings.  

**Important:** In the alternative final model, ceilings are **fixed by region** using WB-informed medians.  
So the DHS-only ceiling estimates here are **diagnostic only**: they help check whether the fixed regional anchors still look broadly compatible with the DHS data.

```{r mle-ceiling-diagnostic}
# Fit simple DHS country-by-country logistic MLEs to inspect plausible K values (diagnostic only).
country_K_mle <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  filter(n() >= 5) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    dat <- .x
    fit <- tryCatch(
      nlsLM(
        mCPR ~ K / (1 + exp(-(year_center - t0) / s)),
        data  = dat,
        start = list(K = max(dat$mCPR) * 1.2, t0 = 0, s = 6),
        lower = c(K = 0.01, t0 = -60, s = 0.5),
        upper = c(K = 0.99, t0 =  60, s = 50),
        control = nls.lm.control(maxiter = 300)
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    tibble(K_hat_mle = if (!is.null(fit)) coef(fit)[["K"]] else NA_real_)
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

summary(country_K_mle$K_hat_mle)
```

# Optional: Per-country logistic diagnostics for speed \(s\) from DHS data (not used in model)

To understand plausible transition speeds from the raw data, we fit separate nonlinear logistic curves by country and summarize the implied \(s\) distribution.

Filter used for stability:
a) at least 5 surveys in a country;
b) observed mCPR range at least 0.08.

These fits are **diagnostic only** and are not used as priors mechanically; they help calibrate prior location/scale choices.

```{r mle-country-speed-diagnostic}
# Fit country-level logistic MLEs and summarize s distribution (diagnostic only).
country_logistic_mle <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  filter(n() >= 5, diff(range(mCPR, na.rm = TRUE)) >= 0.08) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    dat <- .x
    fit <- tryCatch(
      nlsLM(
        mCPR ~ K / (1 + exp(-(year_center - t0) / s)),
        data  = dat,
        start = list(
          K  = min(0.95, max(dat$mCPR, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25),
          t0 = median(dat$year_center, na.rm = TRUE),
          s  = 10
        ),
        lower = c(K = 0.03, t0 = -50, s = 1.5),
        upper = c(K = 0.99, t0 =  50, s = 35),
        control = nls.lm.control(maxiter = 300)
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(fit)) {
      return(tibble(K_hat = NA_real_, t0_hat = NA_real_, s_hat = NA_real_, n_obs = nrow(dat)))
    }

    co <- coef(fit)
    tibble(
      K_hat  = unname(co[["K"]]),
      t0_hat = unname(co[["t0"]]),
      s_hat  = unname(co[["s"]]),
      n_obs  = nrow(dat)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

speed_diag <- country_logistic_mle %>%
  filter(is.finite(s_hat))

summary(speed_diag$s_hat)
quantile(speed_diag$s_hat, probs = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE)
nrow(speed_diag)

ggplot(speed_diag, aes(x = s_hat)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Per-country logistic MLE speed distribution (diagnostic)",
    subtitle = "Countries with >=5 surveys and mCPR range >= 0.08",
    x = "Estimated speed s (years)",
    y = "Country count"
  )
```

# WB-based prior calibration for \(K\) and \(s\) (1985+)

We use external World Bank modern-method mCPR data (`wb_surveys.xlsx`) to build empirical prior anchors for
country ceilings (\(K\)) and regional speeds (\(s\)).

Steps:
a) subset to years 1985 and later;
b) convert percentages to proportions (`value / 100`);
c) count non-missing surveys by country;
d) fit per-country bounded nonlinear least-squares logistic curves for countries with at least 5 non-missing survey years;
e) classify each fitted country into program regions using country code;
f) inspect regional parameter distributions (\(K, t_0, s\)) to inform prior centering and spread;
g) calibrate region-level priors for \(K\) and fixed regional values for \(s\), then transform internally where needed.

Before the Bayesian model, we fit a simple logistic S-curve separately to each WB country series as a pragmatic calibration step. The goal is not to treat these country fits as the final model; it is to get reasonable empirical ranges for the three core quantities:
a) \(K\), the long-run ceiling;
b) \(t_0\), the inflection year (fastest growth point);
c) \(s\), the speed parameter in years.

The starting values and bounds in `nlsLM(...)` were chosen to keep the optimizer in a plausible part of the parameter space:
a) `K` starts at `1.2 x` the observed maximum, capped at `0.95`, because the ceiling should usually be somewhat above the highest observed value but still below the impossible boundary of 1.
b) `t0` starts at the median observed year (on the centered year scale), which is a neutral midpoint when we do not know whether the country is early or late in transition.
c) `s` starts at `10`, which is a moderate transition speed and gives the optimizer a stable default rather than an unrealistically steep or flat initial curve.
d) `K` is bounded to `[0.02, 0.99]` to avoid impossible or boundary-hugging ceilings.
e) `t0` is bounded to `[-60, 60]` on the centered scale, which corresponds to calendar years 1940 to 2060. This is intentionally wider than the observed window because the inferred inflection year can fall before or after the survey years.
f) `s` is bounded to `[0.5, 50]` years to rule out near-vertical jumps and essentially flat curves that are not useful for prior calibration.
g) `maxiter = 400` gives the nonlinear solver enough room to converge on difficult series without letting a single country run indefinitely.

```{r wb-prior-calibration}
# Read WB extract (wide format: one row per country and one column per year).
wb_raw <- readxl::read_excel("wb_surveys.xlsx", sheet = 1, skip = 3)

# Explicit WB country-code mapping keeps prior calibration restricted to country series
# and standardizes French workbook labels into English names used in this report.
wb_region_lookup <- bind_rows(
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("AUS", "KHM", "CHN", "PRK", "IDN", "JPN", "LAO", "MYS", "MNG", "MMR", "PHL", "THA", "TLS", "VNM"),
    wb_region = "East Asia and Pacific"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("ALB", "ARM", "DEU", "ESP", "EST", "FIN", "FRA", "GEO", "KAZ", "KGZ", "MDA", "RUS", "SRB", "TJK", "TUR", "GBR"),
    wb_region = "Europe and Central Asia"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("BOL", "COL", "CRI", "CUB", "DOM", "ECU", "GTM", "GUY", "HTI", "HND", "MEX", "NIC", "PRY", "PER", "TTO"),
    wb_region = "Latin America and Carribbean"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("DZA", "EGY", "IRN", "IRQ", "JOR", "KWT", "MAR", "OMN", "PSE", "YEM"),
    wb_region = "Middle East and North Africa"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("AFG", "BGD", "IND", "MDV", "NPL", "PAK", "LKA"),
    wb_region = "South Asia"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("BEN", "BFA", "CIV", "GMB", "GHA", "GIN", "GNB", "LBR", "MLI", "MRT", "NER", "NGA", "SEN", "SLE", "TGO"),
    wb_region = "West Africa"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("CAF", "CMR", "TCD", "COD", "COG", "GAB", "STP"),
    wb_region = "Central Africa"
  ),
  tibble::tibble(
    `Country Code` = c("AGO", "BDI", "COM", "ETH", "KEN", "LSO", "MDG", "MWI", "MOZ", "NAM", "RWA", "SDN", "SWZ", "TZA", "UGA", "ZAF", "ZMB", "ZWE"),
    wb_region = "East/Southern Africa"
  )
)

wb_country_name_lookup <- tibble::tribble(
  ~`Country Code`, ~country_name_english,
  "AFG", "Afghanistan",
  "AGO", "Angola",
  "ALB", "Albania",
  "ARM", "Armenia",
  "AUS", "Australia",
  "BDI", "Burundi",
  "BEN", "Benin",
  "BFA", "Burkina Faso",
  "BGD", "Bangladesh",
  "BOL", "Bolivia",
  "CAF", "Central African Republic",
  "CHN", "China",
  "CIV", "Cote d'Ivoire",
  "CMR", "Cameroon",
  "COG", "Congo, Rep.",
  "COD", "Congo, Dem. Rep.",
  "COL", "Colombia",
  "COM", "Comoros",
  "CRI", "Costa Rica",
  "CUB", "Cuba",
  "DEU", "Germany",
  "DOM", "Dominican Republic",
  "DZA", "Algeria",
  "ECU", "Ecuador",
  "EGY", "Egypt, Arab Rep.",
  "ESP", "Spain",
  "EST", "Estonia",
  "ETH", "Ethiopia",
  "FIN", "Finland",
  "FRA", "France",
  "GBR", "United Kingdom",
  "GEO", "Georgia",
  "GHA", "Ghana",
  "GIN", "Guinea",
  "GMB", "Gambia, The",
  "GNB", "Guinea-Bissau",
  "GTM", "Guatemala",
  "GUY", "Guyana",
  "HTI", "Haiti",
  "HND", "Honduras",
  "IDN", "Indonesia",
  "IND", "India",
  "IRN", "Iran, Islamic Rep.",
  "IRQ", "Iraq",
  "JOR", "Jordan",
  "JPN", "Japan",
  "KAZ", "Kazakhstan",
  "KEN", "Kenya",
  "KGZ", "Kyrgyz Republic",
  "KHM", "Cambodia",
  "KOR", "Korea, Rep.",
  "KWT", "Kuwait",
  "LAO", "Lao PDR",
  "LBR", "Liberia",
  "LSO", "Lesotho",
  "LKA", "Sri Lanka",
  "MAR", "Morocco",
  "MDA", "Moldova",
  "MDG", "Madagascar",
  "MDV", "Maldives",
  "MEX", "Mexico",
  "MLI", "Mali",
  "MMR", "Myanmar",
  "MNG", "Mongolia",
  "MOZ", "Mozambique",
  "MRT", "Mauritania",
  "MWI", "Malawi",
  "MYS", "Malaysia",
  "NAM", "Namibia",
  "NER", "Niger",
  "NGA", "Nigeria",
  "NIC", "Nicaragua",
  "NPL", "Nepal",
  "OMN", "Oman",
  "PAK", "Pakistan",
  "PER", "Peru",
  "PHL", "Philippines",
  "PRK", "Korea, Dem. People's Rep.",
  "PRY", "Paraguay",
  "PSE", "West Bank and Gaza",
  "RUS", "Russian Federation",
  "RWA", "Rwanda",
  "SEN", "Senegal",
  "SLE", "Sierra Leone",
  "SRB", "Serbia",
  "SSD", "South Sudan",
  "STP", "Sao Tome and Principe",
  "SDN", "Sudan",
  "SWZ", "Eswatini",
  "TCD", "Chad",
  "THA", "Thailand",
  "TJK", "Tajikistan",
  "TIM", "Timor-Leste",
  "TLS", "Timor-Leste",
  "TGO", "Togo",
  "TTO", "Trinidad and Tobago",
  "TUR", "Turkey",
  "TZA", "Tanzania",
  "UGA", "Uganda",
  "VNM", "Vietnam",
  "YEM", "Yemen, Rep.",
  "ZAF", "South Africa",
  "ZMB", "Zambia",
  "ZWE", "Zimbabwe"
)

wb_region_levels <- region_label_lookup$region

# Identify year columns like 1960, 1961, ...
year_cols <- names(wb_raw)[grepl("^\\d{4}$", names(wb_raw))]

# Reshape to long format, keep years >= 1985, and convert percentages to proportions.
wb_long <- wb_raw %>%
  select(`Country Name`, `Country Code`, `Indicator Name`, `Indicator Code`, all_of(year_cols)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(year_cols),
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "mcpr_pct"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(year),
    mcpr_pct = as.numeric(mcpr_pct),
    mcpr_prop = mcpr_pct / 100
  ) %>%
  left_join(wb_country_name_lookup, by = "Country Code") %>%
  mutate(
    `Country Name` = dplyr::coalesce(country_name_english, `Country Name`)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    year >= 1985,
    !is.na(`Country Name`),
    !is.na(`Country Code`),
    !is.na(mcpr_prop),
    mcpr_prop >= 0, mcpr_prop <= 1
  ) %>%
  select(-country_name_english)

# Restrict calibration to explicitly mapped country series used in the eight analytic regions.
wb_long_countries <- wb_long %>%
  left_join(wb_region_lookup, by = "Country Code") %>%
  filter(!is.na(wb_region)) %>%
  mutate(
    wb_region = factor(wb_region, levels = wb_region_levels)
  )

# Validation check: how many non-missing WB survey years exist by country.
wb_survey_counts <- wb_long_countries %>%
  group_by(`Country Name`, `Country Code`) %>%
  summarise(
    n_surveys = n_distinct(year),
    first_year = min(year, na.rm = TRUE),
    last_year = max(year, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_surveys), `Country Name`)

n_countries_total_wb <- nrow(wb_survey_counts)
n_countries_5plus_wb <- sum(wb_survey_counts$n_surveys >= 5)

cat("WB countries with >=1 non-missing survey year (1985+):", n_countries_total_wb, "\n")
cat("WB countries with >=5 non-missing survey years (1985+):", n_countries_5plus_wb, "\n")

# Show a quick top-20 table so WB calibration coverage can be inspected in the report.
knitr::kable(
  wb_survey_counts %>% slice_head(n = 20),
  caption = "WB validation check: top 20 mapped countries by number of non-missing surveys (1985+)"
)

# List the WB calibration countries included in each planning region.
wb_region_country_membership <- wb_long_countries %>%
  distinct(wb_region, `Country Name`) %>%
  arrange(wb_region, `Country Name`) %>%
  group_by(wb_region) %>%
  summarise(
    countries = paste(`Country Name`, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(
  wb_region_country_membership,
  caption = "WB prior-calibration regions and mapped countries"
)

# Fit country-level bounded nonlinear least-squares logistic curves
# (K, t0, and s all estimated freely) for countries with >=5 surveys.
wb_country_mle <- wb_long_countries %>%
  # Keep wb_region as a grouping key so the fitted country-level parameters
  # retain their region assignment for downstream regional summaries.
  group_by(`Country Name`, `Country Code`, wb_region) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(year) >= 5) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    dat <- .x %>%
      arrange(year) %>%
      mutate(year_center = year - 2000)

    fit <- tryCatch(
      nlsLM(
        mcpr_prop ~ K / (1 + exp(-(year_center - t0) / s)),
        data = dat,
        start = list(
          # Start K slightly above the observed maximum, start t0 at the time midpoint,
          # and start s at a moderate 10-year transition speed.
          K = min(0.95, max(dat$mcpr_prop, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.20),
          t0 = stats::median(dat$year_center, na.rm = TRUE),
          s = 10
        ),
        # Bounds keep the fit in a plausible range while allowing inflection timing
        # to fall outside the observed survey window if the data imply that.
        lower = c(K = 0.02, t0 = -60, s = 0.5),
        upper = c(K = 0.99, t0 =  60, s = 50),
        control = nls.lm.control(maxiter = 400)
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(fit)) {
      return(tibble(K_hat = NA_real_, t0_hat = NA_real_, s_hat = NA_real_, n_obs = nrow(dat)))
    }

    co <- coef(fit)
    tibble(
      K_hat = unname(co[["K"]]),
      t0_hat = unname(co[["t0"]]),
      s_hat = unname(co[["s"]]),
      n_obs = nrow(dat)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(is.finite(K_hat), K_hat > 0, K_hat < 1)

# Surface any codes that were expected but not translated, so the mapping stays auditable.
wb_untranslated <- wb_long_countries %>%
  distinct(`Country Code`, `Country Name`) %>%
  anti_join(wb_country_name_lookup %>% select(`Country Code`), by = "Country Code") %>%
  arrange(`Country Code`)

if (nrow(wb_untranslated) > 0) {
  knitr::kable(
    wb_untranslated,
    caption = "WB mapped country codes still using workbook names"
  )
}

# Remove clear boundary-hit ceilings (typically weakly identified at optimizer cap)
wb_k_for_prior <- wb_country_mle %>%
  filter(K_hat <= 0.98, K_hat >= 0.02)

# Keep the full fitted K distribution for visualization diagnostics.
# This aligns plotting with the "WB per-country MLE ceiling distribution (K)" figure.
wb_k_for_plot <- wb_country_mle %>%
  filter(is.finite(K_hat), K_hat > 0, K_hat < 1)

# Historical support for the inflection year. `t0` is peak growth, not method
# invention, so we allow some lead time after the introduction of modern methods
# but disallow implausibly early inflection years.
t0_year_min <- 1966
t0_year_max <- 2035
t0_lower <- t0_year_min - 2000
t0_upper <- t0_year_max - 2000
t0_span <- t0_upper - t0_lower
t0_bound_eps <- 0.5

# Also keep bounded-but-free estimates for t0 and s to summarize all parameter distributions.
wb_t0_for_prior <- wb_country_mle %>%
  filter(is.finite(t0_hat), t0_hat >= t0_lower, t0_hat <= t0_upper)

wb_s_for_prior <- wb_country_mle %>%
  filter(is.finite(s_hat), s_hat > 0.5)

# Summaries for all three parameters from WB country-level MLEs.
summary(wb_country_mle$K_hat)
quantile(wb_country_mle$K_hat, probs = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE)
nrow(wb_country_mle)
nrow(wb_k_for_prior)
summary(wb_t0_for_prior$t0_hat)
quantile(wb_t0_for_prior$t0_hat, probs = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE)
summary(wb_s_for_prior$s_hat)
quantile(wb_s_for_prior$s_hat, probs = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE)

# Region-level K summaries are the key diagnostic for K prior calibration.
wb_region_k_summary <- wb_country_mle %>%
  group_by(wb_region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries = n(),
    mean_K = mean(K_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_K = median(K_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    p25_K = quantile(K_hat, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    p75_K = quantile(K_hat, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(wb_region)

knitr::kable(
  wb_region_k_summary %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "WB MLE K distribution by region (used to sanity-check K prior calibration)"
)

# Inspect raw WB mCPR values by region (not fitted coefficients) to contextualize prior anchors.
# This uses all non-missing 1985+ WB observations in proportion scale.
wb_raw_region <- wb_long_countries

wb_raw_region_summary <- wb_raw_region %>%
  group_by(wb_region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_observations = n(),
    n_countries = n_distinct(`Country Code`),
    mean_mcpr = mean(mcpr_prop, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_mcpr = median(mcpr_prop, na.rm = TRUE),
    p25_mcpr = quantile(mcpr_prop, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    p75_mcpr = quantile(mcpr_prop, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(wb_region)

knitr::kable(
  wb_raw_region_summary %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Raw WB mCPR distribution by region (1985+, proportion scale)"
)

ggplot(
  wb_raw_region,
  aes(x = factor(wb_region, levels = wb_region_levels), y = mcpr_prop, fill = wb_region)
) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.60, outlier.alpha = 0.12, width = 0.65) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.10, alpha = 0.07, size = 0.8, color = "black") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Raw WB mCPR values by region",
    subtitle = "All non-missing observations (1985+), before country-level MLE fitting",
    x = "Region",
    y = "mCPR (proportion)"
  )

ggplot(
  wb_country_mle,
  aes(x = factor(wb_region, levels = wb_region_levels), y = K_hat, fill = wb_region)
) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.55, outlier.alpha = 0.35, width = 0.65) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.45, size = 1.3, color = "black") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "WB per-country MLE ceiling (K) by region",
    subtitle = "Diagnostic check for how regional distributions influence K prior choices",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Estimated K (proportion)"
  )

ggplot(wb_country_mle, aes(x = K_hat)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.98, linetype = "dashed", color = "firebrick", alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "WB per-country MLE ceiling distribution (K)",
    subtitle = "Countries with at least 5 non-missing survey years (1985+); dashed line marks prior-calibration cutoff",
    x = "Estimated K (proportion)",
    y = "Country count"
  )

# Visualize all MLE parameter distributions (K, t0 in calendar year, and s in years).
wb_param_long <- bind_rows(
  # Use full K MLEs so this panel is consistent with the WB per-country K plot above.
  wb_k_for_plot %>%
    transmute(parameter = "K (ceiling)", value = K_hat),
  wb_t0_for_prior %>%
    transmute(parameter = "t0 (calendar year)", value = 2000 + t0_hat),
  # Keep upper-bound s estimates here because the final-model fixed regional speeds
  # are now calibrated to the same raw WB MLE distribution shown in the report.
  wb_s_for_prior %>%
    transmute(parameter = "s (speed, years)", value = s_hat)
)

ggplot(wb_param_long, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  geom_vline(
    data = tibble(parameter = "K (ceiling)", value = 0.98),
    aes(xintercept = value),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "firebrick",
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ parameter, scales = "free_x", ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "WB MLE parameter distributions (countries with >=5 surveys, 1985+)",
    subtitle = "K uses the bounded prior-calibration subset; s retains upper-bound hits so the displayed distribution matches the final-model speed calibration",
    x = "Value",
    y = "Country count"
  )

# Companion regional view across all fitted coefficients (K, t0, s).
wb_param_region_long <- wb_country_mle %>%
  transmute(
    wb_region,
    `K (ceiling)` = K_hat,
    `t0 (calendar year)` = 2000 + t0_hat,
    `s (speed, years)` = s_hat
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`K (ceiling)`, `t0 (calendar year)`, `s (speed, years)`),
    names_to = "parameter",
    values_to = "value"
  )

ggplot(
  wb_param_region_long,
  aes(x = factor(wb_region, levels = wb_region_levels), y = value, fill = wb_region)
) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.alpha = 0.3, width = 0.65) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~parameter, scales = "free_x", ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "WB country-level MLE coefficient distributions by region",
    subtitle = "Used as a regional diagnostic when setting informative priors",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Coefficient value"
  )

# Region-level fixed ceilings anchored to regional WB MLE summaries.
# These values enter the model directly on the interpretable K scale.
wb_region_prior_inputs <- wb_country_mle %>%
  group_by(wb_region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries = n(),
    mean_K = mean(K_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_K = median(K_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    p25_K = quantile(K_hat, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    p75_K = quantile(K_hat, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    region_label_lookup %>% rename(wb_region = region),
    by = "wb_region"
  ) %>%
  arrange(region_prior)

wb_region_k_fixed <- wb_region_prior_inputs %>%
  mutate(
    K_region_fixed = pmin(pmax(median_K, 0.05), 0.98),
    K_lo95_display = pmin(pmax(p25_K, 0.05), 0.98),
    K_hi95_display = pmin(pmax(p75_K, 0.05), 0.98)
  ) %>%
  arrange(wb_region)

knitr::kable(
  wb_region_k_fixed %>%
    select(wb_region, region_prior, n_countries, K_region_fixed, median_K, p25_K, p75_K) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Region-level fixed ceiling settings derived from WB regional MLE summaries"
)

# Region-level speed priors from WB country-level MLE speed estimates.
# The model estimates country-level speeds, so these regional summaries anchor the
# log-speed baselines rather than entering as fixed constants.
wb_region_s_prior_spec <- wb_country_mle %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(s_hat),
    s_hat > 0.5
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    region_label_lookup %>% rename(wb_region = region),
    by = "wb_region"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(region_prior)) %>%
  group_by(region_prior) %>%
  summarise(
    n_regionspeed = n(),
    n_eq50 = sum(abs(s_hat - 50) < 1e-8, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_eq50 = mean(abs(s_hat - 50) < 1e-8, na.rm = TRUE),
    s_region_median = median(s_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    s_region_p25 = quantile(s_hat, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    s_region_p75 = quantile(s_hat, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Ensure all analytic regions have speed priors; fall back to an 8-year center only if sparse/missing.
speed_region_template <- tibble(region_prior = region_prior_levels)
wb_region_s_prior_spec <- speed_region_template %>%
  left_join(wb_region_s_prior_spec, by = "region_prior") %>%
  mutate(
    s_prior_center = if_else(is.na(s_region_median), 8, s_region_median),
    s_region_p25 = if_else(is.na(s_region_p25), s_prior_center / exp(0.5), s_region_p25),
    s_region_p75 = if_else(is.na(s_region_p75), s_prior_center * exp(0.5), s_region_p75),
    prior_center_logs = log(pmax(s_prior_center, 0.5)),
    prior_sd_raw = (log(pmax(s_region_p75, 0.5)) - log(pmax(s_region_p25, 0.5))) / 1.349,
    prior_sd_logs = pmax(0.20, pmin(1.25, prior_sd_raw)),
    prior_lo95_s = exp(prior_center_logs - 2 * prior_sd_logs),
    prior_hi95_s = exp(prior_center_logs + 2 * prior_sd_logs),
    coef_name = paste0("region_prior", region_prior)
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    region_label_lookup %>% rename(wb_region = region),
    by = "region_prior"
  ) %>%
  arrange(wb_region)

knitr::kable(
  wb_region_s_prior_spec %>%
    select(wb_region, region_prior, n_regionspeed, n_eq50, prop_eq50, s_prior_center, s_region_p25, s_region_p75, prior_lo95_s, prior_hi95_s) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Region-level speed prior settings derived from WB regional MLE summaries"
)

# Add fixed regional K values to the DHS modeling data used by brms.
mcpr_logistic_data <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  left_join(
    wb_region_k_fixed %>% select(region_prior, K_region_fixed),
    by = "region_prior"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    region_prior = factor(as.character(region_prior), levels = region_prior_levels)
  )

if (any(is.na(mcpr_logistic_data$K_region_fixed))) {
  stop("Missing K_region_fixed for one or more country rows; check region mapping.")
}

# Optional calibration for t0 prior using WB MLE timing distribution.
t0_fallback_mean <- -2.5
t0_fallback_sd   <- 4

if (nrow(wb_t0_for_prior) >= 15) {
  t0_prior_mean <- stats::median(wb_t0_for_prior$t0_hat, na.rm = TRUE)
  t0_prior_sd <- stats::sd(wb_t0_for_prior$t0_hat, na.rm = TRUE)
  t0_prior_sd <- max(2.0, min(10.0, t0_prior_sd))
} else {
  t0_prior_mean <- t0_fallback_mean
  t0_prior_sd   <- t0_fallback_sd
}

t0_prior_mean <- pmax(t0_lower + t0_bound_eps, pmin(t0_upper - t0_bound_eps, t0_prior_mean))

# Region-level t0 priors: center/sd by WB regional MLE timing summaries.
# This gives each region its own inflection-year baseline while retaining
# country-level random effects around those regional baselines.
wb_region_t0_prior_spec <- wb_country_mle %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(t0_hat),
    t0_hat >= -60,
    t0_hat <= 60
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    region_label_lookup %>% rename(wb_region = region),
    by = "wb_region"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(region_prior)) %>%
  group_by(region_prior) %>%
  summarise(
    n_region_t0 = n(),
    t0_region_median = median(t0_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    t0_region_p25 = quantile(t0_hat, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    t0_region_p75 = quantile(t0_hat, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

t0_region_template <- tibble(region_prior = region_prior_levels)
wb_region_t0_prior_spec <- t0_region_template %>%
  left_join(wb_region_t0_prior_spec, by = "region_prior") %>%
  mutate(
    prior_center_t0 = if_else(is.na(t0_region_median), t0_prior_mean, t0_region_median),
    prior_center_t0 = pmax(t0_lower + t0_bound_eps, pmin(t0_upper - t0_bound_eps, prior_center_t0)),
    prior_q25_t0 = if_else(is.na(t0_region_p25), prior_center_t0 - t0_prior_sd, t0_region_p25),
    prior_q75_t0 = if_else(is.na(t0_region_p75), prior_center_t0 + t0_prior_sd, t0_region_p75),
    prior_q25_t0 = pmax(t0_lower + t0_bound_eps, pmin(prior_center_t0 - 0.10, prior_q25_t0)),
    prior_q75_t0 = pmin(t0_upper - t0_bound_eps, pmax(prior_center_t0 + 0.10, prior_q75_t0)),
    prior_center_frac = (prior_center_t0 - t0_lower) / t0_span,
    prior_q25_frac = (prior_q25_t0 - t0_lower) / t0_span,
    prior_q75_frac = (prior_q75_t0 - t0_lower) / t0_span,
    prior_center_t0_raw = qlogis(prior_center_frac),
    prior_sd_t0_raw = (qlogis(prior_q75_frac) - qlogis(prior_q25_frac)) / 1.349,
    prior_sd_t0_raw = if_else(is.finite(prior_sd_t0_raw), prior_sd_t0_raw, 1.25),
    prior_sd_t0_raw = pmax(0.35, pmin(2.50, prior_sd_t0_raw)),
    prior_center_year = 2000 + prior_center_t0,
    prior_lo95_year = 2000 + (t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(prior_center_t0_raw - 2 * prior_sd_t0_raw)),
    prior_hi95_year = 2000 + (t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(prior_center_t0_raw + 2 * prior_sd_t0_raw)),
    coef_name = paste0("region_prior", region_prior)
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    region_label_lookup %>% rename(wb_region = region),
    by = "region_prior"
  ) %>%
  arrange(wb_region)

knitr::kable(
  wb_region_t0_prior_spec %>%
    select(wb_region, region_prior, n_region_t0, prior_center_year, prior_lo95_year, prior_hi95_year) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Region-level bounded t0 prior settings derived from WB MLE timing summaries"
)

cat("K MLE rows used for calibration:", nrow(wb_k_for_prior), "\n")
cat("Region-level fixed K settings configured for regions:", paste(sort(wb_region_k_fixed$region_prior), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Fixed regional K values:\n")
print(
  wb_region_k_fixed %>%
    select(region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
    mutate(K_region_fixed = round(K_region_fixed, 3))
)
cat("Region-level speed priors configured for regions:", paste(sort(wb_region_s_prior_spec$region_prior), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("t0 MLE rows used for prior calibration:", nrow(wb_t0_for_prior), "\n")
cat("Bounded t0 support (calendar years):", t0_year_min, "to", t0_year_max, "\n")
cat("Global fallback t0 prior mean (year_center scale):", round(t0_prior_mean, 3), "\n")
cat("Global fallback t0 prior sd:", round(t0_prior_sd, 3), "\n")
cat("Implied fallback t0 prior center year:", round(2000 + t0_prior_mean, 1), "\n")
cat("Region-level t0 priors configured for regions:", paste(sort(wb_region_t0_prior_spec$region_prior), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

# Model Specification (alternative final model)

We model survey counts as:

\[
y_{it} \sim \mathrm{Binomial}\!\left(n_{it},\, p_i(t)\right), \qquad
p_i(t)=K_{r(i)}\times \operatorname{logit}^{-1}\!\left(\frac{t-t_{0,i}}{s_i}\right).
\]

In `brms` nonlinear form:
a) \(K_{r(i)} = K^{\text{fixed}}_{r(i)}\), a region-level ceiling taken directly from WB regional median MLE values,
b) \(t_{0,i} = t0_{\min} + (t0_{\max} - t0_{\min})\operatorname{logit}^{-1}(\eta_{t0,i})\), with \(t0_{\min}=1966\) and \(t0_{\max}=2035\) on the calendar-year scale,
c) \(s_i = \exp(\eta_{s,i})\), so speed stays positive while varying by country.

The final-model structure is:
a) `K_region_fixed` enters directly in the nonlinear mean as a region-specific constant.
b) Internal timing term (`t0raw` in code) follows `0 + region_prior + (1 | country)` and is transformed to the bounded inflection-year window 1966 to 2035.
c) Internal log-speed term (`logs` in code) follows `0 + region_prior + (1 | country)`, so regions anchor the typical speed level and countries deviate around that baseline.

Why this structure:
a) It keeps ceilings anchored to regional WB evidence, which removes one major source of \(K\)-\(s\) confounding.
b) It allows country-specific speed trajectories, which is the main quantity of interest in this alternative specification.
c) It preserves the bounded inflection-year strategy so implausibly early timing is ruled out by construction.

```{r model-spec-optionc}
# Alternative final model: region-fixed K, bounded country-varying t0, country-varying s.
logistic_final_model <- bf(
  y | trials(n) ~ K_region_fixed *
    inv_logit((year_center - (-34 + 69 * inv_logit(t0raw))) / exp(logs)),
  t0raw ~ 0 + region_prior + (1 | country),
  logs  ~ 0 + region_prior + (1 | country),
  nl = TRUE
)
```

# Priors (proper, interpretable, stabilizing)

Priors encode plausible parameter ranges before seeing the data, regularize weakly identified pieces, and reduce overfitting in sparse-country settings.

## How the transforms work

In this model, some parameters are estimated on unconstrained scales and then transformed back to interpretable scales:

\[
K_i = K^{\text{fixed}}_{r(i)}, \qquad
t_{0,i} = t0_{\min} + (t0_{\max} - t0_{\min})\operatorname{logit}^{-1}(\eta_{t0,i}), \qquad
s_i = \exp(\eta_{s,i}), \qquad
t = \text{year} - 2000.
\]

So:

a) the ceiling is not estimated inside the Bayesian fit; it is a fixed regional input on the interpretable \(0\) to \(1\) scale.
b) the internal timing term lives on \((-\infty,\infty)\), but \(t_{0,i}\) is constrained to the historically plausible window 1966 to 2035.
c) the internal speed term lives on \((-\infty,\infty)\), and exponentiating it ensures \(s_i > 0\).

## Prior choices and interpretation

### How to read priors

a) In `normal(a, b)`, `a` is the prior mean and `b` is the prior standard deviation.
b) A quick approximation is that about 95% of prior mass is in `a +/- 2b`.
c) `year_center = year - 2000`, so any `t0` value can be mapped to calendar year via `2000 + t0`.
d) `K_region_fixed` is not estimated; it is fixed by region from WB MLE medians.

### Ceiling level \(K_{r(i)}\)

The ceiling is fixed by region:
\[
K_{r(i)} = K^{\text{fixed}}_{r(i)}.
\]

Interpretation:
a) each region's fixed ceiling is the WB regional median of the country-level logistic MLE ceilings;
b) this collapses ceiling uncertainty to a regional anchor so country-level heterogeneity is expressed through timing and speed rather than through \(K_i\);
c) the WB calibration table above shows the region-specific median, p25, and p75 used to justify these fixed values.

### Inflection timing \(t_{0,i}\)

Region-specific timing prior on the internal bounded-timing scale:
\[
\eta_{t0,r} \sim \operatorname{Normal}(\mu^{\text{raw}}_{t0,r},\,\sigma^{\text{raw}}_{t0,r}),
\qquad
t0_r = t0_{\min} + (t0_{\max} - t0_{\min})\operatorname{logit}^{-1}(\eta_{t0,r}),
\quad r \text{ indexes the eight analytic regions.}
\]

Interpretation:
a) each region gets its own timing center, calibrated from WB country-level MLE medians and then mapped into the bounded 1966 to 2035 calendar-year window;
b) each region gets its own spread, calibrated from the WB regional IQR after the same bounded transform;
c) this allows earlier or later regional transition timing without permitting implausibly early inflection years.

Country random-effect spread:
\[
\sigma_{t0} \sim \operatorname{Exponential}(0.5).
\]

Mean prior spread is \(1/0.5=2\) on the internal bounded-timing scale, with heavier country deviations still possible but penalized.

### Country speed \(s_i\)

Region-specific speed prior on the log-speed scale:
\[
\eta_{s,r} \sim \operatorname{Normal}(\mu_{\log s,r},\,\sigma_{\log s,r}),
\qquad
s_r = \exp(\eta_{s,r}),
\quad r \text{ indexes the eight analytic regions.}
\]

Interpretation:
a) each region gets its own log-speed center, calibrated from the WB regional median of country-level \(s\) estimates;
b) each region gets its own spread, calibrated from the WB IQR of \(\log(s)\);
c) countries are then allowed to deviate around that regional speed level through the country random effect.

Country random-effect spread:
\[
\sigma_{\log s} \sim \operatorname{Exponential}(1).
\]

This is moderate shrinkage on the log-speed scale: it keeps country speeds from drifting too far unless the DHS data strongly support it.

```{r priors}
# Build priors after WB-based calibration objects are computed above.
s_region_priors_internal <- do.call(
  c,
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(wb_region_s_prior_spec)), function(i) {
    prior_string(
      sprintf(
        "normal(%.6f, %.6f)",
        wb_region_s_prior_spec$prior_center_logs[i],
        wb_region_s_prior_spec$prior_sd_logs[i]
      ),
      nlpar = "logs",
      class = "b",
      coef = wb_region_s_prior_spec$coef_name[i]
    )
  })
)

t0_region_priors_internal <- do.call(
  c,
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(wb_region_t0_prior_spec)), function(i) {
    prior_string(
      sprintf(
        "normal(%.6f, %.6f)",
        wb_region_t0_prior_spec$prior_center_t0_raw[i],
        wb_region_t0_prior_spec$prior_sd_t0_raw[i]
      ),
      nlpar = "t0raw",
      class = "b",
      coef = wb_region_t0_prior_spec$coef_name[i]
    )
  })
)

priors_fullhier <- c(
  s_region_priors_internal,
  t0_region_priors_internal,
  prior(exponential(1),     nlpar = "logs",  class = "sd", group = "country"),
  prior(exponential(0.5),   nlpar = "t0raw", class = "sd", group = "country")
)

# Sanity check: confirm parameter names match the priors above
get_prior(
  formula = logistic_final_model,
  data    = mcpr_logistic_data,
  family  = binomial(link = "identity")
)
```

# Model Fitting

We save/load the fitted model from disk so knitting is reproducible.  
If `mcpr_data.csv` changes, the code below now forces a refit automatically instead of silently reusing an outdated cached model.

Technical note:
a) In this nonlinear `brms` setup, posterior prediction can fail with `predictor_fe(...)` if `newdata` has predictor type or level mismatches (especially factor levels for `region_prior`) or missing `K_region_fixed`.
b) Posterior-prediction sections therefore build `newdata` from `mcpr_logistic_data` profiles and check required predictors before calling `posterior_epred()`.
c) If the error persists after code changes, remove cached fit files and refit so the stored model terms and current data pipeline are aligned.
d) This report defaults to a development sampler profile; switch `final_run <- TRUE` in the libraries chunk for the longer final sampler settings.

```{r fit-model}
# Read cached fit when the underlying model inputs still match, unless a manual
# refit is requested. Report-text edits should not force a Stan rebuild.
fit_path <- paste0("fit_logistic_final_model_regionFixedK_countryS_boundedT0_", run_profile_label, ".rds")
input_data_path <- "mcpr_data.csv"
wb_input_path <- "wb_surveys.xlsx"

cache_is_current <- function(cache_path, dependency_paths) {
  if (!file.exists(cache_path)) return(FALSE)
  cache_time <- as.numeric(file.info(cache_path)[["mtime"]])
  dep_times <- as.numeric(file.info(dependency_paths)[["mtime"]])
  dep_times <- dep_times[!is.na(dep_times)]
  if (length(dep_times) == 0) return(TRUE)
  all((cache_time + cache_mtime_tolerance_secs) >= dep_times)
}

if (!force_model_refit && cache_is_current(fit_path, c(input_data_path, wb_input_path))) {
  fit_logistic <- readRDS(fit_path)
} else {
  fit_logistic <- brm(
    formula = logistic_final_model,
    data    = mcpr_logistic_data,
    family  = binomial(link = "identity"),
    prior   = priors_fullhier,
    chains  = 4,
    cores   = 4,
    iter    = main_fit_iter,
    warmup  = main_fit_warmup,
    seed    = 123,
    control = main_fit_control
  )
  saveRDS(fit_logistic, fit_path)
}

summary(fit_logistic)
```

# Diagnostics

## Convergence diagnostics

Diagnostic criteria:
a) **Rhat** near 1.00 (typically <= 1.01).
b) **Effective sample sizes (ESS)** reasonably large (hundreds+).
c) Trace plots should show stable mixing without trends or sticking.

```{r diagnostics-convergence}
# Check chain mixing, divergences, and tree depth behavior.
plot(fit_logistic)

nuts <- nuts_params(fit_logistic)
divs <- sum(nuts$Parameter == "divergent__" & nuts$Value == 1)
max_td <- max(nuts[nuts$Parameter == "treedepth__", "Value"])
cat("Divergences:", divs, "\n")
cat("Max treedepth observed:", max_td, "\n")
```

# Posterior population-level parameter distributions

This section summarizes what the *population-level* model implies.

Diagnostic criteria:
a) region-level inflection-year baselines should be plausible;
b) regional fixed ceiling anchors should align with the WB calibration;
c) posterior region-level speed baselines should remain consistent with the WB-informed prior scale.

```{r global-shape}
# Extract posterior draws for region-level t0 and speed baselines, and summarize fixed region-level K inputs.
draws <- posterior::as_draws_df(fit_logistic)

t0_region_meta <- wb_region_t0_prior_spec %>%
  transmute(
    region_prior,
    region = wb_region,
    draw_name = paste0("b_t0raw_", coef_name)
  ) %>%
  filter(draw_name %in% names(draws))

t0_region_posterior <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(t0_region_meta)), function(i) {
    tibble(
      region_prior = t0_region_meta$region_prior[i],
      region = t0_region_meta$region[i],
      t0_year = 2000 + (t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(draws[[t0_region_meta$draw_name[i]]]))
    )
  })
) 

# Fixed regional K values used as constants in the nonlinear mean.
k_region_summary <- wb_region_k_fixed %>%
  transmute(
    region_prior = factor(region_prior, levels = region_prior_levels),
    region = factor(wb_region, levels = region_label_lookup$region),
    n_countries,
    K_fixed = K_region_fixed,
    K_p25 = p25_K,
    K_p75 = p75_K
  ) %>%
  arrange(region)

knitr::kable(
  k_region_summary %>%
    select(region, region_prior, n_countries, K_fixed, K_p25, K_p75) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Region-level fixed ceiling settings used by the alternative final model"
)

speed_region_meta <- wb_region_s_prior_spec %>%
  transmute(
    region_prior,
    region = wb_region,
    draw_name = paste0("b_logs_", coef_name)
  ) %>%
  filter(draw_name %in% names(draws))

speed_region_posterior <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(speed_region_meta)), function(i) {
    tibble(
      region_prior = speed_region_meta$region_prior[i],
      region = speed_region_meta$region[i],
      s_years = exp(draws[[speed_region_meta$draw_name[i]]])
    )
  })
)

ggplot(t0_region_posterior, aes(x = t0_year, color = region, fill = region)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.40, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(
    data = t0_region_posterior %>%
      group_by(region) %>%
      summarise(v = median(t0_year), .groups = "drop"),
    aes(xintercept = v),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.6,
    color = "grey25",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = region_palette) +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior of Region-level Inflection-Year Baselines (t0)",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines mark posterior medians by region",
    x = "Inflection year",
    y = "Density"
  )

ggplot(k_region_summary, aes(x = region, y = K_fixed, fill = region)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = K_p25, ymax = K_p75),
    width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey15", alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = region_palette) +
  labs(
    title = "Region-level fixed ceiling values (K)",
    subtitle = "Bars are WB regional medians; whiskers show WB regional IQR (p25 to p75)",
    x = "Region",
    y = "K (ceiling)"
  )

ggplot(speed_region_posterior, aes(x = s_years, color = region, fill = region)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.40, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(
    data = speed_region_posterior %>%
      group_by(region) %>%
      summarise(v = median(s_years), .groups = "drop"),
    aes(xintercept = v),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.6,
    color = "grey25",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = region_palette) +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior of region-level speed baselines (s)",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines mark posterior medians by region",
    x = "Speed s (years)",
    y = "Density"
  )
```

# Prior-to-posterior update checks

These diagnostics show how the likelihood updates key prior assumptions after seeing the data.

```{r prior-posterior-update}
# Compare prior and posterior distributions for key model components.
set.seed(20260225)
draws <- posterior::as_draws_df(fit_logistic)
n_draws_update <- nrow(draws)

# Robustly locate key group-SD parameter names in posterior draws.
sd_logs_name <- names(draws)[grepl("^sd_country__logs_Intercept$", names(draws))]
if (length(sd_logs_name) == 0) {
  sd_logs_name <- names(draws)[grepl("^sd_country__logs", names(draws))]
}
if (length(sd_logs_name) == 0) {
  stop("Could not find posterior draws for sd_country__logs.")
}
sd_logs_name <- sd_logs_name[1]

sd_t0_name <- names(draws)[grepl("^sd_country__t0raw_Intercept$", names(draws))]
if (length(sd_t0_name) == 0) {
  sd_t0_name <- names(draws)[grepl("^sd_country__t0raw", names(draws))]
}
if (length(sd_t0_name) == 0) {
  stop("Could not find posterior draws for sd_country__t0raw.")
}
sd_t0_name <- sd_t0_name[1]

core_update <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    parameter = "sd country log s",
    source = "Prior",
    value = rexp(n_draws_update, rate = 1)
  ),
  tibble(
    parameter = "sd country log s",
    source = "Posterior",
    value = draws[[sd_logs_name]]
  ),
  tibble(
    parameter = "sd country t0",
    source = "Prior",
    value = rexp(n_draws_update, rate = 0.5)
  ),
  tibble(
    parameter = "sd country t0",
    source = "Posterior",
    value = draws[[sd_t0_name]]
  )
)

ggplot(core_update, aes(x = value, color = source, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.20, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ parameter, scales = "free", ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#7F8C8D", "Posterior" = "#1F77B4")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#BDC3C7", "Posterior" = "#5DADE2")) +
  labs(
    title = "Prior vs posterior updates for core final-model parameters",
    subtitle = "This model updates country-level timing and speed variation; K is fixed by region",
    x = "Parameter value",
    y = "Density"
  )

# For region-level t0 coefficients, show prior/posterior on calendar-year scale.
t0_update_meta <- wb_region_t0_prior_spec %>%
  transmute(
    region_prior,
    region = wb_region,
    draw_name = paste0("b_t0raw_", coef_name),
    prior_mean_raw = prior_center_t0_raw,
    prior_sd_raw = prior_sd_t0_raw
  ) %>%
  filter(draw_name %in% names(draws))

t0_update_draws <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(t0_update_meta)), function(i) {
    tibble(
      region_prior = t0_update_meta$region_prior[i],
      region = t0_update_meta$region[i],
      source = "Prior",
      t0_year = 2000 + (
        t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(
          rnorm(
            n_draws_update,
            mean = t0_update_meta$prior_mean_raw[i],
            sd = t0_update_meta$prior_sd_raw[i]
          )
        )
      )
    )
  }),
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(t0_update_meta)), function(i) {
    tibble(
      region_prior = t0_update_meta$region_prior[i],
      region = t0_update_meta$region[i],
      source = "Posterior",
      t0_year = 2000 + (t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(draws[[t0_update_meta$draw_name[i]]]))
    )
  })
)

ggplot(t0_update_draws, aes(x = t0_year, color = source, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.20, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ factor(region, levels = region_label_lookup$region), ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#7F8C8D", "Posterior" = "#2E8B57")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#BDC3C7", "Posterior" = "#7CCBA2")) +
  labs(
    title = "Prior vs posterior update for regional t0 baselines",
    subtitle = "Displayed as inflection year for interpretability",
    x = "Inflection year",
    y = "Density"
  )

# For region-level speed coefficients, show prior/posterior on the interpretable s scale.
s_update_meta <- wb_region_s_prior_spec %>%
  transmute(
    region_prior,
    region = wb_region,
    draw_name = paste0("b_logs_", coef_name),
    prior_mean = prior_center_logs,
    prior_sd = prior_sd_logs
  ) %>%
  filter(draw_name %in% names(draws))

s_update_draws <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(s_update_meta)), function(i) {
    tibble(
      region_prior = s_update_meta$region_prior[i],
      region = s_update_meta$region[i],
      source = "Prior",
      s_years = exp(rnorm(n_draws_update, mean = s_update_meta$prior_mean[i], sd = s_update_meta$prior_sd[i]))
    )
  }),
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(s_update_meta)), function(i) {
    tibble(
      region_prior = s_update_meta$region_prior[i],
      region = s_update_meta$region[i],
      source = "Posterior",
      s_years = exp(draws[[s_update_meta$draw_name[i]]])
    )
  })
)

ggplot(s_update_draws, aes(x = s_years, color = source, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.20, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ factor(region, levels = region_label_lookup$region), ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#7F8C8D", "Posterior" = "#C0392B")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Prior" = "#BDC3C7", "Posterior" = "#F1948A")) +
  labs(
    title = "Prior vs posterior update for regional speed baselines",
    subtitle = "Displayed on the interpretable speed scale in years",
    x = "Speed s (years)",
    y = "Density"
  )
```

# Fitted values and residual diagnostics

`posterior_epred()` returns expected counts:
\[
\mathbb{E}[y_{it}\mid \text{data}] = n_{it} p_{it}.
\]
We compute:
a) \(\hat y\): fitted counts,
b) \(\hat p = \hat y / n\): fitted probabilities.

Diagnostic criteria:
a) observed vs fitted probabilities should lie close to the diagonal;
b) Pearson residuals should be centered near 0 without strong structure.

```{r fitted-and-residuals}
# Compute fitted probabilities and residual diagnostics on observed data.
ep_obs <- posterior_epred(fit_logistic, newdata = mcpr_logistic_data, re_formula = NULL)

y_hat_vec <- colMeans(ep_obs)
p_hat_vec <- y_hat_vec / mcpr_logistic_data$n

mcpr_logistic_data <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  mutate(
    p_hat = p_hat_vec,
    y_hat = y_hat_vec,
    prob_error = mCPR - p_hat,
    p_hat_clipped = pmax(pmin(p_hat, 0.999), 0.001),
    pearson_resid = (y - y_hat) / sqrt(n * p_hat_clipped * (1 - p_hat_clipped))
  )

summary(mcpr_logistic_data$p_hat)
summary(mcpr_logistic_data$pearson_resid)
summary(abs(mcpr_logistic_data$prob_error))
```

```{r residual-plots}
# Visual diagnostics: observed-vs-fitted, residual patterns, and absolute error distribution.
ggplot(mcpr_logistic_data, aes(x = p_hat, y = mCPR, color = country)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs Fitted mCPR (Probability scale)",
    subtitle = "We want points to broadly cluster around the diagonal line",
    x = expression("Fitted probability (" * hat(p) * ")"),
    y = "Observed mCPR"
  )

ggplot(mcpr_logistic_data, aes(x = p_hat, y = pearson_resid, color = country)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Pearson Residuals vs Fitted Probability",
    subtitle = "We want residuals centered near 0 with no strong structure",
    x = expression("Fitted probability (" * hat(p) * ")"),
    y = "Pearson residual"
  )

ggplot(mcpr_logistic_data, aes(x = abs(prob_error), fill = country)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Absolute Probability Error Distribution",
    subtitle = "Typical errors should be small; long tails may suggest outliers/misfit",
    x = "|Observed - Fitted|",
    y = "Count"
  )
```

# Posterior predictive checks

Because the fitted model is binomial, a useful diagnostic is whether replicated datasets from the posterior look like the observed DHS data on the same probability scale.

Diagnostic criteria:
a) observed summary statistics should sit inside the posterior predictive reference range;
b) observed mCPR values should broadly fall inside pointwise posterior predictive intervals;
c) the overall shape of the observed probability distribution should resemble replicated draws.

```{r posterior-predictive-checks}
# Draw replicated outcomes from the posterior predictive distribution and convert them
# back to the observed probability scale so they are directly comparable to DHS mCPR.
set.seed(20260307)
n_ppc_draws <- 200
yrep_obs <- posterior_predict(
  fit_logistic,
  newdata = mcpr_logistic_data,
  re_formula = NULL,
  ndraws = n_ppc_draws
)

if (is.null(dim(yrep_obs))) {
  yrep_obs <- matrix(yrep_obs, nrow = 1)
}

p_rep <- sweep(yrep_obs, 2, mcpr_logistic_data$n, "/")
obs_p <- mcpr_logistic_data$mCPR

ppc_stat_summary <- tibble(
  statistic = c("Mean mCPR", "SD mCPR", "Median mCPR", "Min mCPR", "Max mCPR"),
  observed = c(
    mean(obs_p, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(obs_p, na.rm = TRUE),
    median(obs_p, na.rm = TRUE),
    min(obs_p, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(obs_p, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  pp_median = c(
    median(rowMeans(p_rep, na.rm = TRUE)),
    median(apply(p_rep, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)),
    median(apply(p_rep, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)),
    median(apply(p_rep, 1, min, na.rm = TRUE)),
    median(apply(p_rep, 1, max, na.rm = TRUE))
  ),
  pp_p05 = c(
    quantile(rowMeans(p_rep, na.rm = TRUE), ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE), ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE), ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, min, na.rm = TRUE), ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, max, na.rm = TRUE), ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  pp_p95 = c(
    quantile(rowMeans(p_rep, na.rm = TRUE), ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE), ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE), ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, min, na.rm = TRUE), ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
    quantile(apply(p_rep, 1, max, na.rm = TRUE), ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    observed_inside_pp95 = observed >= pp_p05 & observed <= pp_p95
  )

knitr::kable(
  ppc_stat_summary %>%
    select(statistic, observed, pp_median, observed_inside_pp95) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Posterior predictive summary checks on the DHS probability scale"
)

ppc_interval_df <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    year,
    region,
    obs_p = mCPR,
    pp_mean = colMeans(p_rep, na.rm = TRUE),
    pp_lo = apply(p_rep, 2, quantile, probs = ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    pp_hi = apply(p_rep, 2, quantile, probs = ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(inside_pp95 = obs_p >= pp_lo & obs_p <= pp_hi)

ppc_pointwise_coverage <- mean(ppc_interval_df$inside_pp95, na.rm = TRUE)
cat(
  "Pointwise 95% posterior predictive coverage on the observed probability scale:",
  round(100 * ppc_pointwise_coverage, 1), "%\n"
)
```

```{r posterior-predictive-plots}
# Visualize replicated probability distributions and pointwise predictive intervals.
ppc_draw_ids <- seq_len(min(30, nrow(p_rep)))

ppc_density_df <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    source = "Observed",
    draw = "Observed",
    value = obs_p
  ),
  bind_rows(lapply(ppc_draw_ids, function(i) {
    tibble(
      source = "Replicated",
      draw = paste0("Draw ", i),
      value = p_rep[i, ]
    )
  }))
)

ggplot() +
  geom_density(
    data = ppc_density_df %>% filter(source == "Replicated"),
    aes(x = value, group = draw),
    color = "grey65",
    linewidth = 0.45,
    alpha = 0.35
  ) +
  geom_density(
    data = ppc_density_df %>% filter(source == "Observed"),
    aes(x = value),
    color = "#C0392B",
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior predictive density overlay",
    subtitle = "Grey lines are replicated DHS datasets from the posterior; red line is observed mCPR",
    x = "mCPR (probability scale)",
    y = "Density"
  )

ggplot(ppc_interval_df, aes(x = obs_p, y = pp_mean, color = inside_pp95)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pp_lo, ymax = pp_hi), alpha = 0.22, width = 0) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 1.8) +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "#2E8B57", "FALSE" = "#C0392B"),
    labels = c("FALSE" = "Outside 95% interval", "TRUE" = "Inside 95% interval")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Pointwise posterior predictive intervals",
    subtitle = "Each point shows observed vs posterior predictive mean; vertical bars are 95% predictive intervals",
    x = "Observed mCPR",
    y = "Posterior predictive mean mCPR",
    color = NULL
  )
```

# Residual shape check

The binomial model does **not** assume Gaussian errors. This section therefore uses Pearson residual shape as a heuristic check only. Strong skew, heavy tails, or large QQ-plot departures would suggest remaining misfit in the mean structure.

```{r residual-shape-check}
# Summarize Pearson residual shape and compare it to a normal reference only as
# a heuristic diagnostic, not as a formal model assumption.
resid_df <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(is.finite(pearson_resid)) %>%
  transmute(pearson_resid)

resid_mean <- mean(resid_df$pearson_resid, na.rm = TRUE)
resid_sd <- sd(resid_df$pearson_resid, na.rm = TRUE)
resid_centered <- resid_df$pearson_resid - resid_mean

resid_shape_summary <- tibble(
  mean_resid = resid_mean,
  sd_resid = resid_sd,
  skewness = mean(resid_centered^3, na.rm = TRUE) / (resid_sd^3),
  excess_kurtosis = mean(resid_centered^4, na.rm = TRUE) / (resid_sd^4) - 3
)

knitr::kable(
  resid_shape_summary %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Pearson residual shape summary"
)

qq_probs <- ppoints(nrow(resid_df))
qq_theoretical <- qnorm(qq_probs)
qq_sample <- sort(resid_df$pearson_resid)
qq_slope <- IQR(qq_sample, na.rm = TRUE) / IQR(qq_theoretical, na.rm = TRUE)
qq_intercept <- median(qq_sample, na.rm = TRUE) - qq_slope * median(qq_theoretical, na.rm = TRUE)
qq_df <- tibble(theoretical = qq_theoretical, sample = qq_sample)
```

```{r residual-shape-plots}
ggplot(resid_df, aes(x = pearson_resid)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, fill = "#4C78A8", alpha = 0.65) +
  stat_function(
    fun = dnorm,
    args = list(mean = resid_mean, sd = resid_sd),
    color = "#C0392B",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Pearson residual distribution",
    subtitle = "Red curve is a normal reference using the empirical residual mean and SD",
    x = "Pearson residual",
    y = "Density"
  )

ggplot(qq_df, aes(x = theoretical, y = sample)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.4, color = "#2C3E50") +
  geom_abline(intercept = qq_intercept, slope = qq_slope, color = "#C0392B", linewidth = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Pearson residual QQ plot",
    subtitle = "Systematic departures from the line indicate asymmetric or heavy-tailed residual structure",
    x = "Normal-theory quantiles",
    y = "Observed Pearson residual quantiles"
  )
```

# Residual serial-correlation check

If the logistic mean structure has captured most of the time dependence, within-country residuals should show limited lagged correlation after ordering observations by year.

Diagnostic criteria:
a) lag-1 residual scatter should be centered near the origin without a strong linear trend;
b) average lag correlation should be modest rather than persistently large across lags.

```{r residual-serial-correlation}
# Check whether residuals are still serially correlated within country over time.
resid_serial_df <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  arrange(country, year) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  mutate(
    lag1_resid = lag(pearson_resid),
    lag_gap_years = year - lag(year)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

lag_summary <- bind_rows(lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  lag_k <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
    arrange(country, year) %>%
    group_by(country) %>%
    summarise(
      n_pairs = sum(is.finite(pearson_resid) & is.finite(dplyr::lag(pearson_resid, k))),
      lag_corr = if (n_pairs >= 2) {
        cor(pearson_resid, dplyr::lag(pearson_resid, k), use = "complete.obs")
      } else {
        NA_real_
      },
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(lag_corr), n_pairs > 0)

  tibble(
    lag = k,
    n_countries = nrow(lag_k),
    weighted_mean_corr = if (nrow(lag_k) > 0) weighted.mean(lag_k$lag_corr, lag_k$n_pairs) else NA_real_,
    median_country_corr = if (nrow(lag_k) > 0) median(lag_k$lag_corr, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  )
}))

lag1_country_corr <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  arrange(country, year) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs = sum(is.finite(pearson_resid) & is.finite(lag(pearson_resid))),
    lag1_corr = if (n_pairs >= 2) cor(pearson_resid, lag(pearson_resid), use = "complete.obs") else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(lag1_corr))

knitr::kable(
  lag_summary %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Within-country residual lag correlation summary"
)
```

```{r residual-serial-correlation-plots}
ggplot(
  resid_serial_df %>% filter(is.finite(lag1_resid), is.finite(pearson_resid)),
  aes(x = lag1_resid, y = pearson_resid)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, size = 1.6, color = "#4C78A8") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Lag-1 Pearson residual dependence within country",
    subtitle = "Each point compares a country's current residual with its previous survey residual",
    x = "Lag-1 Pearson residual",
    y = "Current Pearson residual"
  )

ggplot(lag_summary, aes(x = lag, y = weighted_mean_corr)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, color = "#2C3E50") +
  geom_point(size = 2.2, color = "#2C3E50") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = lag_summary$lag) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Average residual autocorrelation by lag",
    subtitle = "Weighted mean of within-country Pearson residual correlations",
    x = "Lag (surveys apart)",
    y = "Weighted mean residual correlation"
  )
```

# Validation: 10-country holdout on last observation (2010-prioritized)

To test out-of-sample performance, we run a small holdout exercise:

1. Select 10 countries (prioritizing countries with an observation around 2010).  
2. Mask each selected country's **last** observed mCPR point.  
3. Refit the same hierarchical model on remaining data.  
4. Compare model projection at the held-out year against the actual held-out value.

```{r validation-holdout-setup}
# Fix seed so selected holdout countries are reproducible.
set.seed(2026)

# Summarize each country's panel depth and whether it has data near 2010.
country_last_info <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(
    n_obs = n(),
    last_year = max(year, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_obs_around_2010 = any(year >= 2008 & year <= 2012),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_obs >= 4) %>%
  arrange(desc(has_obs_around_2010), abs(last_year - 2010), desc(n_obs), country)

# Select 10 holdout countries, prioritizing those with observations around 2010.
holdout_countries <- country_last_info %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  pull(country)

# For each selected country, hold out its last observed survey point.
holdout_rows <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(country %in% holdout_countries) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(country_last_info %>% select(country, n_obs, last_year, has_obs_around_2010),
            by = "country") %>%
  arrange(country)

# Training data are all rows except held-out row_ids.
train_holdout_data <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  anti_join(holdout_rows %>% select(row_id), by = "row_id")

# Print holdout design table for transparency.
holdout_rows %>%
  select(country, n_obs, has_obs_around_2010, year, mCPR, n_women) %>%
  arrange(desc(has_obs_around_2010), abs(year - 2010), country)
```

```{r validation-holdout-fit}
# Cache the validation fit, but only invalidate it when the underlying inputs or
# the main fitted model change, unless a manual validation refit is requested.
validation_fit_path <- paste0(
  "fit_logistic_holdout10_lastobs_final_model_regionFixedK_countryS_boundedT0_",
  run_profile_label,
  ".rds"
)
validation_dependencies <- c(input_data_path, wb_input_path, fit_path)

if (!force_validation_refit && cache_is_current(validation_fit_path, validation_dependencies)) {
  fit_logistic_holdout <- readRDS(validation_fit_path)
} else {
  # Refit the same model object on reduced (holdout-masked) training data.
  fit_logistic_holdout <- update(
    fit_logistic,
    newdata = train_holdout_data,
    recompile = FALSE,
    chains = 4,
    cores = 4,
    iter = holdout_fit_iter,
    warmup = holdout_fit_warmup,
    seed = 2026,
    control = holdout_fit_control
  )
  saveRDS(fit_logistic_holdout, validation_fit_path)
}

# Display holdout-fit summary diagnostics.
summary(fit_logistic_holdout)
```

```{r validation-holdout-evaluate}
# Predict held-out rows on the count scale and convert to probability scale.
ep_holdout <- posterior_epred(
  fit_logistic_holdout,
  newdata = holdout_rows,
  re_formula = NULL
)

p_holdout <- sweep(ep_holdout, 2, holdout_rows$n, FUN = "/")
qcol <- function(x, p) apply(x, 2, quantile, probs = p)

# Build per-country holdout comparison table: predicted mean/interval vs actual.
holdout_eval <- holdout_rows %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    holdout_year = year,
    actual_mcpr = mCPR,
    pred_mean = colMeans(p_holdout),
    pred_p05 = qcol(p_holdout, ci_lower),
    pred_p95 = qcol(p_holdout, ci_upper)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    error = pred_mean - actual_mcpr,
    abs_error = abs(error),
    covered_95 = actual_mcpr >= pred_p05 & actual_mcpr <= pred_p95
  ) %>%
  arrange(abs_error)

# Aggregate holdout metrics to summarize forecast quality.
validation_metrics <- holdout_eval %>%
  summarise(
    n_holdout = n(),
    MAE = mean(abs_error),
    RMSE = sqrt(mean(error^2)),
    mean_error = mean(error),
    coverage_95 = mean(covered_95)
  )

knitr::kable(
  validation_metrics %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Summary metrics for the 10-country holdout validation"
)

knitr::kable(
  holdout_eval %>%
```
