```{r validation-holdout-evaluate}
    select(country, holdout_year, actual_mcpr, pred_mean, error, abs_error, covered_95) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Country-level holdout validation results"
)
```

```{r validation-holdout-plots}
# Plot 1: predicted vs actual for held-out points with 95% intervals.
ggplot(holdout_eval, aes(x = pred_mean, y = actual_mcpr, label = country)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = pred_p05, xmax = pred_p95), height = 0, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.6, color = "#2b8cbe") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf) +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "10-country holdout validation: projected vs actual last observation",
    subtitle = "Horizontal bars are 95% posterior intervals at the held-out year",
    x = "Projected mCPR at held-out year (posterior mean)",
    y = "Actual held-out mCPR"
  )

# Build country-specific training trajectories for faceted holdout visualization.
train_plot <- train_holdout_data %>%
  filter(country %in% holdout_eval$country) %>%
  select(country, year, mCPR)

# Plot 2: each holdout country with train points + predicted held-out CI + actual held-out.
ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = train_plot,
    aes(x = year, y = mCPR, group = country),
    color = "grey40", alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = train_plot,
    aes(x = year, y = mCPR),
    color = "grey20", alpha = 0.7, size = 1.4
  ) +
  geom_linerange(
    data = holdout_eval,
    aes(x = holdout_year, ymin = pred_p05, ymax = pred_p95),
    color = "#2b8cbe", linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = holdout_eval,
    aes(x = holdout_year, y = pred_mean),
    color = "#2b8cbe", size = 2.4
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = holdout_eval,
    aes(x = holdout_year, y = actual_mcpr),
    color = "#d7301f", size = 2.6
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ country, ncol = 5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = "Country-level holdout check (masked last point per country)",
    subtitle = "Grey = training observations, blue = projection (95% CI), red = held-out actual",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR"
  )
```

# Posterior decision quantities (as-of 2026)

We evaluate each country at **year 2026** (even if the latest survey is earlier).  
For each country we compute posterior distributions for:
a) the effective ceiling assigned to that country, \(K_{r(i)}\),
b) \(p_i(2026)\),
c) headroom \(K_{r(i)} - p_i(2026)\),
d) relative saturation \(p_i(2026)/K_{r(i)}\),
e) growth rate \(\left.\frac{dp_i(t)}{dt}\right|_{t=2026}\).

```{r posterior-country-parameters}
# Construct posterior country-level effective K, t0, and s under the alternative final model.
nd_country <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(country, region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
  arrange(country) %>%
  mutate(
    country = as.character(country),
    region_prior = as.character(region_prior),
    year_center = 0,
    n = 1,
    y = 0
  )

countries <- nd_country$country

logs_draws <- posterior_linpred(
  fit_logistic, nlpar = "logs", newdata = nd_country,
  re_formula = NULL, transform = FALSE, summary = FALSE
)
s_draws <- exp(logs_draws)

t0_raw_draws <- posterior_linpred(
  fit_logistic, nlpar = "t0raw", newdata = nd_country,
  re_formula = NULL, transform = FALSE, summary = FALSE
)
t0_draws <- t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(t0_raw_draws)

n_draws <- nrow(s_draws)
n_countries <- ncol(s_draws)

# Region-fixed K matrix aligned to country columns.
K_draws <- matrix(
  rep(nd_country$K_region_fixed, each = n_draws),
  nrow = n_draws,
  ncol = n_countries
)
```

```{r posterior-quantities-2026}
# Evaluate posterior mCPR, growth, and headroom at decision year 2026.
t_dec_year <- 2026
t_dec <- t_dec_year - 2000

t_mat <- matrix(t_dec, nrow = n_draws, ncol = n_countries)
s_mat <- s_draws

z_mat <- exp(-(t_mat - t0_draws) / s_mat)
p_mat <- K_draws / (1 + z_mat)
dpdt_mat <- (K_draws / s_mat) * z_mat / (1 + z_mat)^2
d2pdt2_mat <- (K_draws / (s_mat^2)) * z_mat * (z_mat - 1) / (1 + z_mat)^3

t_goal_base_year <- 2025
t_goal_base <- t_goal_base_year - 2000
t_goal_base_mat <- matrix(t_goal_base, nrow = n_draws, ncol = n_countries)
z_goal_base_mat <- exp(-(t_goal_base_mat - t0_draws) / s_mat)
p_goal_base_mat <- K_draws / (1 + z_goal_base_mat)
dpdt_goal_base_mat <- (K_draws / s_mat) * z_goal_base_mat / (1 + z_goal_base_mat)^2

headroom_mat <- K_draws - p_mat
rellevel_mat <- p_mat / K_draws

qcol <- function(x, p) apply(x, 2, quantile, probs = p)

country_summary <- tibble(
  country = countries,
  decision_year = t_dec_year,
  K_mean = colMeans(K_draws),
  K_p05  = qcol(K_draws, ci_lower),
  K_p95  = qcol(K_draws, ci_upper),
  t0_mean = colMeans(t0_draws),
  t0_p05  = qcol(t0_draws, ci_lower),
  t0_p95  = qcol(t0_draws, ci_upper),
  s_mean = colMeans(s_draws),
  s_p05  = qcol(s_draws, ci_lower),
  s_p95  = qcol(s_draws, ci_upper),
  p_mean = colMeans(p_mat),
  p_p05  = qcol(p_mat, ci_lower),
  p_p95  = qcol(p_mat, ci_upper),
  head_mean = colMeans(headroom_mat),
  head_p05  = qcol(headroom_mat, ci_lower),
  head_p95  = qcol(headroom_mat, ci_upper),
  rel_mean = colMeans(rellevel_mat),
  rel_p05  = qcol(rellevel_mat, ci_lower),
  rel_p95  = qcol(rellevel_mat, ci_upper),
  dpdt_mean = colMeans(dpdt_mat),
  dpdt_p05  = qcol(dpdt_mat, ci_lower),
  dpdt_p95  = qcol(dpdt_mat, ci_upper),
  d2pdt2_mean = colMeans(d2pdt2_mat),
  d2pdt2_p05 = qcol(d2pdt2_mat, ci_lower),
  d2pdt2_p95 = qcol(d2pdt2_mat, ci_upper)
) %>%
  mutate(
    t0_year_mean = round(2000 + t0_mean),
    years_since_t0_2026 = decision_year - (2000 + t0_mean),
    accel_status_2026 = case_when(
      d2pdt2_p05 > 0 ~ "Accelerating",
      d2pdt2_p95 < 0 ~ "Decelerating",
      TRUE ~ "Near peak / uncertain"
    )
  )

summary(country_summary$K_mean)
```

```{r region-posterior-intervals}
# Build region-level posterior intervals for key parameters under the final model.
# Use only region levels that are actually present in the fitted DHS data.
nd_region_param <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
  filter(!is.na(region_prior), !is.na(K_region_fixed)) %>%
  arrange(region_prior) %>%
  mutate(
    region_prior = as.character(region_prior),
    country = as.character(mcpr_logistic_data$country[1]),
    year_center = 0,
    n = 1,
    y = 0
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    region_label_lookup,
    by = "region_prior"
  )

if (nrow(nd_region_param) == 0) {
  stop("No observed regions found for region-level posterior interval plotting.")
}

# K is fixed by region in the final model, so repeat constants across posterior draws.
logs_region_draws <- posterior_linpred(
  fit_logistic,
  nlpar = "logs",
  newdata = nd_region_param,
  re_formula = NA,
  transform = FALSE,
  summary = FALSE
)
s_region_draws <- exp(logs_region_draws)

K_region_draws <- matrix(
  rep(nd_region_param$K_region_fixed, each = nrow(s_region_draws)),
  nrow = nrow(s_region_draws),
  ncol = ncol(s_region_draws)
)

t0_raw_region_draws <- posterior_linpred(
  fit_logistic,
  nlpar = "t0raw",
  newdata = nd_region_param,
  re_formula = NA,
  transform = FALSE,
  summary = FALSE
)
t0_mat_region <- t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(t0_raw_region_draws)
t_mat_region <- matrix(2026 - 2000, nrow = nrow(s_region_draws), ncol = ncol(s_region_draws))
p_region_draws <- K_region_draws / (1 + exp(-(t_mat_region - t0_mat_region) / s_region_draws))

qcol <- function(x, p) apply(x, 2, quantile, probs = p)

region_interval_summary <- tibble(
  region_prior = nd_region_param$region_prior,
  region = nd_region_param$region,
  K_p50 = apply(K_region_draws, 2, median),
  K_p05 = qcol(K_region_draws, ci_lower),
  K_p95 = qcol(K_region_draws, ci_upper),
  t0_year_p50 = 2000 + apply(t0_mat_region, 2, median),
  t0_year_p05 = 2000 + qcol(t0_mat_region, ci_lower),
  t0_year_p95 = 2000 + qcol(t0_mat_region, ci_upper),
  s_p50 = apply(s_region_draws, 2, median),
  s_p05 = qcol(s_region_draws, ci_lower),
  s_p95 = qcol(s_region_draws, ci_upper),
  p2026_p50 = apply(p_region_draws, 2, median),
  p2026_p05 = qcol(p_region_draws, ci_lower),
  p2026_p95 = qcol(p_region_draws, ci_upper)
)

knitr::kable(
  region_interval_summary %>%
    arrange(region) %>%
    transmute(
      region,
      region_prior,
      K = K_p50,
      t0_year = t0_year_p50,
      s_years = s_p50,
      p2026 = p2026_p50
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Region-level posterior summaries (posterior medians)"
)

region_interval_long <- bind_rows(
  region_interval_summary %>%
    transmute(region, region_prior, parameter = "K (ceiling)", p50 = K_p50, p05 = K_p05, p95 = K_p95),
  region_interval_summary %>%
    transmute(region, region_prior, parameter = "t0 (inflection year)", p50 = t0_year_p50, p05 = t0_year_p05, p95 = t0_year_p95),
  region_interval_summary %>%
    transmute(region, region_prior, parameter = "s (speed, years)", p50 = s_p50, p05 = s_p05, p95 = s_p95),
  region_interval_summary %>%
    transmute(region, region_prior, parameter = "p(2026)", p50 = p2026_p50, p05 = p2026_p05, p95 = p2026_p95)
)

plot_region_interval <- function(df_plot, plot_title, plot_subtitle, y_label) {
  ggplot(
    df_plot,
    aes(
      x = factor(region, levels = region_label_lookup$region),
      y = p50,
      ymin = p05,
      ymax = p95,
      color = region
    )
  ) +
    geom_pointrange(linewidth = 0.9) +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "none") +
    scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      subtitle = plot_subtitle,
      x = "Region",
      y = y_label
    )
}

plot_region_interval(
  region_interval_long %>% filter(parameter == "K (ceiling)"),
  "Region-level posterior intervals: K",
  "Points are medians; bars are 95% credible intervals",
  "K (ceiling)"
)

plot_region_interval(
  region_interval_long %>% filter(parameter == "t0 (inflection year)"),
  "Region-level posterior intervals: t0",
  "Points are medians; bars are 95% credible intervals",
  "Inflection year"
)

plot_region_interval(
  region_interval_long %>% filter(parameter == "s (speed, years)"),
  "Region-level posterior intervals: s",
  "Points are medians; bars are 95% credible intervals",
  "Speed s (years)"
)

plot_region_interval(
  region_interval_long %>% filter(parameter == "p(2026)"),
  "Region-level posterior intervals: p(2026)",
  "Points are medians; bars are 95% credible intervals",
  "mCPR at 2026"
)
```

# Investment phase classification (rank-based leverage, as-of 2026)

Leverage is high when:
a) growth at 2026 is high; and
b) room-to-grow is high.

Classification steps:

a) Compute two room-to-grow signals: absolute headroom \(K_i - p_i(2026)\) and relative room \(1 - p_i(2026)/K_i\).
b) Convert growth and room quantities into percentile ranks from 0 to 1.
c) Build two leverage scores: `leverage_head` from growth rank x absolute-headroom rank, and `leverage_rel` from growth rank x relative-room rank.
d) Combine them into a final score: `leverage = 0.4 * leverage_head + 0.6 * leverage_rel`.
e) Assign phases from the top and bottom leverage groups.

```{r classify-phases-rank}
# Build rank-based leverage scores and assign countries into three investment phases.
pct_scaleup  <- 0.20
pct_maintain <- 0.20

df <- country_summary %>%
  mutate(
    room_abs = head_mean,
    room_rel = 1 - rel_mean,
    r_growth = dplyr::percent_rank(dpdt_mean),
    r_head   = dplyr::percent_rank(room_abs),
    r_room   = dplyr::percent_rank(room_rel),
    leverage_head = r_growth * r_head,
    leverage_rel  = r_growth * r_room,
    leverage = 0.4 * leverage_head + 0.6 * leverage_rel
  )

n_total <- nrow(df)
n_scaleup  <- max(5, round(pct_scaleup  * n_total))
n_maintain <- max(5, round(pct_maintain * n_total))

scaleup_set <- df %>% arrange(desc(leverage)) %>% slice_head(n = n_scaleup) %>% pull(country)
maintain_set <- df %>% arrange(leverage) %>% slice_head(n = n_maintain) %>% pull(country)

investment_table <- df %>%
  mutate(
    phase = case_when(
      country %in% scaleup_set  ~ "Scale-up",
      country %in% maintain_set ~ "Maintain / equity",
      TRUE                      ~ "Build / monitor"
    ),
    phase_priority = case_when(
      phase == "Scale-up"          ~ 1L,
      phase == "Build / monitor"   ~ 2L,
      phase == "Maintain / equity" ~ 3L,
      TRUE                         ~ 9L
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(phase_priority, desc(leverage), desc(room_rel), desc(dpdt_mean))

investment_table %>% count(phase, phase_priority) %>% arrange(phase_priority)
```

```{r classify-phases-lastobs-headroom}
# Build a parallel investment table using headroom measured from each country's
# latest observed DHS mCPR instead of the posterior mean p(2026).
latest_country_obs_for_table <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(mCPR), !is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    last_obs_year = year,
    last_obs_mcpr = mCPR
  )

df_lastobs_headroom <- country_summary %>%
  left_join(latest_country_obs_for_table, by = "country") %>%
  mutate(
    head_lastobs_mean = K_mean - last_obs_mcpr,
    rel_lastobs_mean = last_obs_mcpr / K_mean,
    room_rel_lastobs = 1 - rel_lastobs_mean,
    r_growth = dplyr::percent_rank(dpdt_mean),
    r_head_lastobs = dplyr::percent_rank(head_lastobs_mean),
    r_room_lastobs = dplyr::percent_rank(room_rel_lastobs),
    leverage_head_lastobs = r_growth * r_head_lastobs,
    leverage_rel_lastobs = r_growth * r_room_lastobs,
    leverage_lastobs = 0.4 * leverage_head_lastobs + 0.6 * leverage_rel_lastobs
  )

n_total_lastobs <- nrow(df_lastobs_headroom)
n_scaleup_lastobs <- max(5, round(pct_scaleup * n_total_lastobs))
n_maintain_lastobs <- max(5, round(pct_maintain * n_total_lastobs))

scaleup_lastobs_set <- df_lastobs_headroom %>%
  arrange(desc(leverage_lastobs)) %>%
  slice_head(n = n_scaleup_lastobs) %>%
  pull(country)

maintain_lastobs_set <- df_lastobs_headroom %>%
  arrange(leverage_lastobs) %>%
  slice_head(n = n_maintain_lastobs) %>%
  pull(country)

investment_table_lastobs <- df_lastobs_headroom %>%
  mutate(
    phase_lastobs = case_when(
      country %in% scaleup_lastobs_set ~ "Scale-up",
      country %in% maintain_lastobs_set ~ "Maintain / equity",
      TRUE ~ "Build / monitor"
    ),
    phase_priority_lastobs = case_when(
      phase_lastobs == "Scale-up" ~ 1L,
      phase_lastobs == "Build / monitor" ~ 2L,
      phase_lastobs == "Maintain / equity" ~ 3L,
      TRUE ~ 9L
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(phase_priority_lastobs, desc(leverage_lastobs), desc(room_rel_lastobs), desc(dpdt_mean))

investment_table_lastobs %>%
  count(phase_lastobs, phase_priority_lastobs) %>%
  arrange(phase_priority_lastobs)
```

# Results

## Country investment table (as-of 2026)

How to interpret the columns:
All quantities below are conditional on the final-model structure (country-level \(K\), region-fixed \(s\)).

a) `country`: country name.
b) `decision_year`: decision horizon (fixed at 2026).
c) `phase`: investment phase label.
d) `leverage`: final composite leverage score used for ranking.
e) `leverage_head`: leverage from growth x absolute headroom rank.
f) `leverage_rel`: leverage from growth x relative room-to-grow rank.
g) `p_mean`: posterior mean \(p_i(2026)\), expected mCPR at 2026.
h) `K_mean`: posterior mean ceiling \(K_i\).
i) `s_mean`: fixed regional speed \(s_{r(i)}\) used for the country's region (WB median anchor).
j) `head_mean`: absolute headroom \(K_i - p_i(2026)\).
k) `rel_mean`: relative saturation \(p_i(2026)/K_i\).
l) `room_rel`: relative room-to-grow \(1 - p_i(2026)/K_i\).
m) `dpdt_mean`: growth rate at 2026.
n) `t0_year_mean`: posterior mean inflection year.

```{r investment-table}
# Print a compact table of phase assignments and key posterior summaries.
knitr::kable(
  investment_table %>%
    select(country, decision_year, phase, leverage, leverage_head, leverage_rel,
           p_mean, K_mean, s_mean, head_mean, rel_mean, room_rel, dpdt_mean, t0_year_mean) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Country Investment Phase Summary (as-of 2026; selected columns)",
  align   = "l"
)
```

## Alternative country investment table using latest observed mCPR headroom

This companion table keeps the same ranking logic, but redefines absolute and relative room-to-grow using the latest observed DHS mCPR instead of the model-implied \(p_i(2026)\).

How to interpret the additional columns:

a) `last_obs_year`: latest observed DHS survey year for that country.
b) `last_obs_mcpr`: observed mCPR in that latest DHS survey.
c) `head_lastobs_mean`: absolute headroom \(K_i - \text{last observed mCPR}\).
d) `rel_lastobs_mean`: observed saturation relative to the ceiling, \(\text{last observed mCPR} / K_i\).
e) `room_rel_lastobs`: relative room-to-grow, \(1 - \text{last observed mCPR} / K_i\).
f) `leverage_lastobs`: composite leverage score under this alternate headroom definition.
g) `phase_lastobs`: phase assignment under this alternate headroom definition.

```{r investment-table-lastobs}
# Print an alternate investment table using the latest observed mCPR as the
# headroom reference instead of p(2026).
knitr::kable(
  investment_table_lastobs %>%
    select(
      country, decision_year, last_obs_year, last_obs_mcpr,
      phase_lastobs, leverage_lastobs, leverage_head_lastobs, leverage_rel_lastobs,
      K_mean, head_lastobs_mean, rel_lastobs_mean, room_rel_lastobs,
      dpdt_mean, t0_year_mean
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Alternative Country Investment Phase Summary using K - last observed mCPR",
  align = "l"
)
```

## S-curve stage classification from the latest observed DHS survey

This table classifies each country into one of three logistic S-curve stages using its latest observed DHS mCPR together with the fitted ceiling, inflection timing, and growth profile.

Stage rule used within each posterior draw:

a) `Stage 1: initial` if the latest observed survey is still before the inferred inflection point and observed saturation is below 50% of the fitted ceiling.
b) `Stage 3: plateau` if the latest observed survey is after the inferred inflection point, observed saturation is at least 80% of the fitted ceiling, and current growth has fallen to at most 50% of peak growth.
c) `Stage 2: diffusion` otherwise.

The final table reports the modal stage and the posterior probability assigned to each stage.

```{r s-curve-stage-table}
# Classify each country into logistic S-curve stages using the latest observed
# DHS survey, relative saturation, inflection timing, and growth versus peak growth.
latest_country_obs_stage <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(mCPR), !is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    region,
    latest_obs_year = year,
    latest_obs_mcpr = mCPR
  )

latest_obs_year_vec <- latest_country_obs_stage$latest_obs_year[match(countries, latest_country_obs_stage$country)]
latest_obs_mcpr_vec <- latest_country_obs_stage$latest_obs_mcpr[match(countries, latest_country_obs_stage$country)]
latest_obs_region_vec <- latest_country_obs_stage$region[match(countries, latest_country_obs_stage$country)]

latest_obs_year_mat <- matrix(
  rep(latest_obs_year_vec, each = n_draws),
  nrow = n_draws,
  ncol = n_countries
)
latest_obs_mcpr_mat <- matrix(
  rep(latest_obs_mcpr_vec, each = n_draws),
  nrow = n_draws,
  ncol = n_countries
)

latest_t_mat <- latest_obs_year_mat - 2000
latest_z_mat <- exp(-(latest_t_mat - t0_draws) / s_draws)
dpdt_lastobs_mat <- (K_draws / s_draws) * latest_z_mat / (1 + latest_z_mat)^2
peak_growth_mat <- K_draws / (4 * s_draws)
growth_ratio_lastobs_mat <- dpdt_lastobs_mat / peak_growth_mat
rel_sat_lastobs_mat <- latest_obs_mcpr_mat / K_draws
years_since_t0_lastobs_mat <- latest_obs_year_mat - (2000 + t0_draws)

stage1_mat <- years_since_t0_lastobs_mat < 0 & rel_sat_lastobs_mat < 0.50
stage3_mat <- years_since_t0_lastobs_mat >= 0 &
  rel_sat_lastobs_mat >= 0.80 &
  growth_ratio_lastobs_mat <= 0.50
stage2_mat <- !(stage1_mat | stage3_mat)

s_curve_stage_table <- tibble(
  country = countries,
  region = latest_obs_region_vec,
  latest_obs_year = latest_obs_year_vec,
  latest_obs_mcpr = latest_obs_mcpr_vec,
  last_obs_rel_sat_mean = colMeans(rel_sat_lastobs_mat),
  last_obs_rel_sat_p05 = apply(rel_sat_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  last_obs_rel_sat_p95 = apply(rel_sat_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
  years_since_t0_lastobs_mean = colMeans(years_since_t0_lastobs_mat),
  years_since_t0_lastobs_p05 = apply(years_since_t0_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  years_since_t0_lastobs_p95 = apply(years_since_t0_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
  growth_ratio_to_peak_mean = colMeans(growth_ratio_lastobs_mat),
  growth_ratio_to_peak_p05 = apply(growth_ratio_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  growth_ratio_to_peak_p95 = apply(growth_ratio_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
  prob_stage1_initial = colMeans(stage1_mat),
  prob_stage2_diffusion = colMeans(stage2_mat),
  prob_stage3_plateau = colMeans(stage3_mat)
) %>%
  mutate(
    s_curve_stage = case_when(
      prob_stage1_initial >= prob_stage2_diffusion & prob_stage1_initial >= prob_stage3_plateau ~ "Stage 1: initial",
      prob_stage3_plateau >= prob_stage1_initial & prob_stage3_plateau >= prob_stage2_diffusion ~ "Stage 3: plateau",
      TRUE ~ "Stage 2: diffusion"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(s_curve_stage, levels = c("Stage 1: initial", "Stage 2: diffusion", "Stage 3: plateau")),
    desc(pmax(prob_stage1_initial, prob_stage2_diffusion, prob_stage3_plateau)),
    country
  )

knitr::kable(
  s_curve_stage_table %>%
    select(
      country, region, latest_obs_year, latest_obs_mcpr,
      last_obs_rel_sat_mean, years_since_t0_lastobs_mean, growth_ratio_to_peak_mean,
      prob_stage1_initial, prob_stage2_diffusion, prob_stage3_plateau, s_curve_stage
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Country S-curve stage classification from the latest observed DHS survey"
)
```

### S-curve stage classification using p(2026)

This companion table applies the same standardized-position rule, but it evaluates each country at the model-implied `p(2026)` rather than at the latest observed mCPR.

Stage rule used within each posterior draw:

a) `Stage 1: initial` if \(x_i(2026) < -1.5\).
b) `Stage 2: diffusion` if \(-1.5 \le x_i(2026) \le 1.5\).
c) `Stage 3: plateau` if \(x_i(2026) > 1.5\).

The final table reports the modal stage and the posterior probability assigned to each stage at the 2026 decision horizon.

```{r s-curve-stage-2026-table}
# Apply the same standardized-position S-curve rules at p(2026) instead of
# the latest observed mCPR point.
years_since_t0_2026_mat <- t_dec_year - (2000 + t0_draws)
growth_ratio_2026_mat <- dpdt_mat / peak_growth_mat
rel_sat_2026_mat <- p_mat / K_draws
stage_position_2026_mat <- years_since_t0_2026_mat / s_draws

stage1_2026_mat <- stage_position_2026_mat < -1.5
stage3_2026_mat <- stage_position_2026_mat > 1.5
stage2_2026_mat <- !(stage1_2026_mat | stage3_2026_mat)

s_curve_stage_2026_table <- tibble(
  country = countries,
  region = latest_obs_region_vec,
  decision_year = t_dec_year,
  p_2026_mean = colMeans(p_mat),
  stage_position_2026_mean = colMeans(stage_position_2026_mat),
  rel_sat_2026_mean = colMeans(rel_sat_2026_mat),
  years_since_t0_2026_mean = colMeans(years_since_t0_2026_mat),
  growth_ratio_to_peak_2026_mean = colMeans(growth_ratio_2026_mat),
  prob_stage1_initial_2026 = colMeans(stage1_2026_mat),
  prob_stage2_diffusion_2026 = colMeans(stage2_2026_mat),
  prob_stage3_plateau_2026 = colMeans(stage3_2026_mat)
) %>%
  mutate(
    s_curve_stage_2026 = case_when(
      prob_stage1_initial_2026 >= prob_stage2_diffusion_2026 &
        prob_stage1_initial_2026 >= prob_stage3_plateau_2026 ~ "Stage 1: initial",
      prob_stage3_plateau_2026 >= prob_stage1_initial_2026 &
        prob_stage3_plateau_2026 >= prob_stage2_diffusion_2026 ~ "Stage 3: plateau",
      TRUE ~ "Stage 2: diffusion"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(s_curve_stage_2026, levels = c("Stage 1: initial", "Stage 2: diffusion", "Stage 3: plateau")),
    desc(pmax(prob_stage1_initial_2026, prob_stage2_diffusion_2026, prob_stage3_plateau_2026)),
    country
  )

knitr::kable(
  s_curve_stage_2026_table %>%
    select(
      country, region, decision_year, p_2026_mean,
      stage_position_2026_mean, rel_sat_2026_mean,
      years_since_t0_2026_mean, growth_ratio_to_peak_2026_mean,
      prob_stage1_initial_2026, prob_stage2_diffusion_2026, prob_stage3_plateau_2026,
      s_curve_stage_2026
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Country S-curve stage classification using p(2026)"
)
```

## Latest observed mCPR relative to posterior projection

This table compares each country's latest observed DHS mCPR with the model's posterior projection for that same survey year.

Interpretation:

a) `projection_gap` is `observed - projected`.
b) `status_latest_projection` is based on the 95% posterior projection interval for the latest observed survey year.
c) Countries above the upper bound are overperforming relative to the fitted trajectory; countries below the lower bound are underperforming.

```{r latest-observed-vs-projection}
# Compare the latest observed DHS value for each country against the model's
# posterior projection at that same survey year.
latest_country_rows <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(mCPR), !is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ep_latest <- posterior_epred(
  fit_logistic,
  newdata = latest_country_rows,
  re_formula = NULL
)

p_latest_draws <- sweep(ep_latest, 2, latest_country_rows$n, "/")

latest_projection_table <- tibble(
  country = latest_country_rows$country,
  region = latest_country_rows$region,
  latest_obs_year = latest_country_rows$year,
  latest_obs_mcpr = latest_country_rows$mCPR,
  projected_latest_mean = colMeans(p_latest_draws),
  projected_latest_p05 = apply(p_latest_draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  projected_latest_p95 = apply(p_latest_draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
) %>%
  mutate(
    projection_gap = latest_obs_mcpr - projected_latest_mean,
    abs_projection_gap = abs(projection_gap),
    status_latest_projection = case_when(
      latest_obs_mcpr > projected_latest_p95 ~ "Overperforming",
      latest_obs_mcpr < projected_latest_p05 ~ "Underperforming",
      TRUE ~ "Within 95% projection interval"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    desc(status_latest_projection == "Overperforming"),
    desc(status_latest_projection == "Underperforming"),
    desc(abs_projection_gap)
  )

knitr::kable(
  latest_projection_table %>%
    filter(status_latest_projection != "Within 95% projection interval") %>%
    select(
      country, region, latest_obs_year, latest_obs_mcpr,
      projected_latest_mean,
      projection_gap, status_latest_projection
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Countries over- and underperforming their posterior projection at the latest observed DHS survey"
)
```

## Years to reach 99% of the projected ceiling

The logistic curve approaches \(K_i\) asymptotically, so it never reaches the ceiling exactly in finite time. For planning purposes, this table therefore reports the year and waiting time to reach 99% of the projected ceiling.

Interpretation:

a) `target_99pct_K_mean` is the posterior mean of \(0.99 \times K_i\).
b) `year_reach_99pctK_mean` is the posterior mean calendar year when the country reaches 99% of its ceiling.
c) `years_from_latest_to_99pctK_mean` is the expected number of years from the latest observed DHS survey to that 99%-of-\(K\) point.
d) Countries already at or beyond that threshold are shown with zero remaining years.

```{r years-to-99pct-k}
# Summarize when each country reaches 99% of its projected ceiling.
target_rel_sat <- 0.99
target_logit <- qlogis(target_rel_sat)

t99_year_draws <- 2000 + t0_draws + s_draws * target_logit
K99_draws <- target_rel_sat * K_draws

latest_year_lookup <- latest_country_rows %>%
  transmute(country, latest_obs_year = year)

latest_year_vec <- latest_year_lookup$latest_obs_year[match(countries, latest_year_lookup$country)]
latest_year_mat <- matrix(
  rep(latest_year_vec, each = nrow(t99_year_draws)),
  nrow = nrow(t99_year_draws),
  ncol = ncol(t99_year_draws)
)

years_to_99_draws <- pmax(t99_year_draws - latest_year_mat, 0)

time_to_k_table <- tibble(
  country = countries,
  target_rel_sat = target_rel_sat,
  target_99pct_K_mean = colMeans(K99_draws),
  target_99pct_K_p05 = apply(K99_draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  target_99pct_K_p95 = apply(K99_draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
  year_reach_99pctK_mean = colMeans(t99_year_draws),
  year_reach_99pctK_p05 = apply(t99_year_draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  year_reach_99pctK_p95 = apply(t99_year_draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
  years_from_latest_to_99pctK_mean = colMeans(years_to_99_draws),
  years_from_latest_to_99pctK_p05 = apply(years_to_99_draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  years_from_latest_to_99pctK_p95 = apply(years_to_99_draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
) %>%
  left_join(latest_country_rows %>% transmute(country, region, latest_obs_year = year, latest_obs_mcpr = mCPR), by = "country") %>%
  mutate(
    status_99pctK = case_when(
      years_from_latest_to_99pctK_mean <= 0.25 ~ "Already near 99% of K",
      TRUE ~ "Below 99% of K"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(years_from_latest_to_99pctK_mean, country)

knitr::kable(
  time_to_k_table %>%
    select(
      country, region, latest_obs_year, latest_obs_mcpr,
      target_99pct_K_mean,
      year_reach_99pctK_mean,
      years_from_latest_to_99pctK_mean,
      status_99pctK
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Years from latest observed DHS survey to 99% of the projected ceiling"
)
```

## Acceleration versus deceleration at 2026

For the logistic S-curve, acceleration is captured by the second derivative \(\frac{d^2p_i(t)}{dt^2}\).

Interpretation:

a) `d2pdt2_mean > 0` means the country is still accelerating.
b) `d2pdt2_mean < 0` means the country is decelerating.
c) If the 95% credible interval crosses zero, the country is near peak growth or the sign is uncertain.
d) `years_since_t0_2026` provides the same intuition on the time scale: negative values are pre-inflection and positive values are post-inflection.

```{r acceleration-status-2026}
# Summarize which countries are accelerating or decelerating at the 2026 decision point.
acceleration_plot_data <- investment_table %>%
  select(
    country, phase, t0_year_mean, years_since_t0_2026,
    dpdt_mean, d2pdt2_mean, d2pdt2_p05, d2pdt2_p95, accel_status_2026
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(accel_status_2026, levels = c("Accelerating", "Near peak / uncertain", "Decelerating")),
    desc(abs(d2pdt2_mean)),
    country
  )

acceleration_table <- acceleration_plot_data %>%
  select(
    country, phase, t0_year_mean, years_since_t0_2026,
    dpdt_mean, d2pdt2_mean, accel_status_2026
  )

knitr::kable(
  acceleration_table %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5))),
  caption = "Country acceleration status at 2026 based on the second derivative of the fitted S-curve"
)
```

```{r acceleration-visual-2026}
# Visualize acceleration status using years since inflection and the posterior
# mean second derivative at the 2026 decision point.
accel_cols <- c(
  "Accelerating" = "#2DA44E",
  "Near peak / uncertain" = "#B58900",
  "Decelerating" = "#D73A49"
)

acceleration_labels <- acceleration_table %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(d2pdt2_mean))) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

ggplot(
  acceleration_plot_data,
  aes(x = years_since_t0_2026, y = d2pdt2_mean, color = accel_status_2026)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey55", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "#1D4ED8", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = d2pdt2_p05, ymax = d2pdt2_p95), alpha = 0.30, linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(size = 2.4, alpha = 0.90) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = acceleration_labels,
    aes(label = country),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.35,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = accel_cols) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Which countries are accelerating versus decelerating?",
    subtitle = "Positive second derivative = accelerating; negative second derivative = decelerating; vertical line marks the inflection point",
    x = "Years since inflection at 2026",
    y = "Second derivative at 2026 (d2p/dt2)"
  )
```

## Decision space: growth vs headroom (as-of 2026)

```{r decision-space}
# Visualize phase decision space using growth vs absolute headroom.
ggplot(investment_table, aes(x = dpdt_mean, y = head_mean, color = phase)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85, size = 2.5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.5)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Decision space (as-of 2026): Growth vs Headroom",
    subtitle = "Final leverage combines absolute and relative room-to-grow signals",
    x = "Growth rate at 2026 (dp/dt)",
    y = "Headroom at 2026 (K - p)"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())

# Companion view with posterior uncertainty bars on both axes.
ggplot(investment_table, aes(x = dpdt_mean, y = head_mean, color = phase)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = head_p05, ymax = head_p95), alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = dpdt_p05, xmax = dpdt_p95), alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85, size = 2.2) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.5)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Decision space with posterior uncertainty (as-of 2026)",
    subtitle = "Horizontal and vertical bars are 95% credible intervals",
    x = "Growth rate at 2026 (dp/dt)",
    y = "Headroom at 2026 (K - p)"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())
```

# Country-level posterior projections (all countries)

Interpretation:
a) Each panel shows the posterior mean mCPR trajectory for one country.
b) The panel label reports `2026 - t0`, the number of years between the 2026 decision point and the posterior mean inflection year.
c) The ochre horizontal band and line show the 95% credible interval and posterior mean for the country ceiling `K`.
d) The blue vertical band and line show the 95% credible interval and posterior mean for the inflection year `t0`.
e) The grey dotted line marks the last observed DHS survey year, and each panel projects 10 years beyond that point.

```{r country-curves-all}
# Plot posterior trajectories for all countries, split by region so each
# rendered figure stays readable and only shows a modest number of countries.
phase_order <- c("Scale-up", "Build / monitor", "Maintain / equity")

country_obs_window <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(
    first_obs_year = min(year, na.rm = TRUE),
    last_obs_year = max(year, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

country_region_lookup <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(country, region)

curve_guides <- investment_table %>%
  left_join(country_obs_window, by = "country") %>%
  left_join(country_region_lookup, by = "country") %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    region,
    phase,
    leverage,
    first_obs_year,
    last_obs_year,
    forecast_end_year = last_obs_year + 10,
    K_mean,
    K_p05,
    K_p95,
    t0_year_mean = 2000 + t0_mean,
    t0_year_p05 = 2000 + t0_p05,
    t0_year_p95 = 2000 + t0_p95,
    years_since_t0_2026 = 2026 - (2000 + t0_mean)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(K_mean), is.finite(K_p05), is.finite(K_p95),
    is.finite(t0_year_mean), is.finite(t0_year_p05), is.finite(t0_year_p95),
    is.finite(last_obs_year), is.finite(forecast_end_year)
  )

country_order <- curve_guides %>%
  mutate(region = factor(region, levels = region_label_lookup$region)) %>%
  mutate(phase = factor(phase, levels = phase_order)) %>%
  arrange(region, phase, desc(leverage), country) %>%
  pull(country)

curve_guides <- curve_guides %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(country, levels = country_order),
    country_label = paste0(
      as.character(country),
      "\n2026 - t0 = ",
      sprintf("%.1f", years_since_t0_2026),
      " years"
    )
  )

country_labeller <- setNames(
  curve_guides$country_label,
  as.character(curve_guides$country)
)

years <- seq(
  min(mcpr_logistic_data$year, na.rm = TRUE) - 2,
  max(curve_guides$forecast_end_year, na.rm = TRUE),
  by = 1
)

nd_grid <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(country, region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
  mutate(country = as.character(country)) %>%
  left_join(
    curve_guides %>%
      transmute(country = as.character(country), region, phase, first_obs_year, last_obs_year, forecast_end_year),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(year = years) %>%
  filter(
    year >= first_obs_year - 2,
    year <= forecast_end_year
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    region_prior = as.character(region_prior),
    year_center = year - 2000,
    n = 1,
    y = 0
  )

ep <- posterior_epred(fit_logistic, newdata = nd_grid, re_formula = NULL)

summ <- tibble(
  country = nd_grid$country,
  region = nd_grid$region,
  phase = nd_grid$phase,
  year = nd_grid$year,
  first_obs_year = nd_grid$first_obs_year,
  last_obs_year = nd_grid$last_obs_year,
  forecast_end_year = nd_grid$forecast_end_year,
  p_mean = colMeans(ep),
  p_lo = apply(ep, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  p_hi = apply(ep, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
) %>%
  mutate(
    segment = if_else(year <= last_obs_year, "historical", "forecast")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    curve_guides %>%
      transmute(country = as.character(country), country_factor = country),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(country = country_factor) %>%
  select(-country_factor)

obs_subset <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  left_join(
    curve_guides %>%
      transmute(country = as.character(country), phase, country_factor = country),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(country = country_factor) %>%
  filter(!is.na(country)) %>%
  select(country, region, phase, year, mCPR)

# Consistent color direction across phases.
phase_cols <- c(
  "Scale-up" = "#2F6FE4",
  "Build / monitor" = "#E85D5D",
  "Maintain / equity" = "#1E9B5B"
)

region_plot_order <- curve_guides %>%
  distinct(region) %>%
  mutate(region = factor(region, levels = region_label_lookup$region)) %>%
  arrange(region) %>%
  pull(region) %>%
  as.character()

make_region_country_plot <- function(region_name) {
  region_guides <- curve_guides %>%
    filter(region == region_name)

  region_summ <- summ %>%
    filter(region == region_name)

  region_obs <- obs_subset %>%
    filter(region == region_name)

  n_country_region <- nrow(region_guides)
  ncol_region <- dplyr::case_when(
    n_country_region <= 4 ~ 2L,
    n_country_region <= 9 ~ 3L,
    n_country_region <= 16 ~ 4L,
    TRUE ~ 5L
  )

  ggplot() +
    # Horizontal band = 95% CrI for K (country ceiling).
    geom_rect(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = K_p05, ymax = K_p95),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      fill = "#FDE68A",
      alpha = 0.07
    ) +
    # Vertical band = 95% CrI for inflection year.
    geom_rect(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(xmin = t0_year_p05, xmax = t0_year_p95, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      fill = "#BFDBFE",
      alpha = 0.06
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(xintercept = last_obs_year),
      linetype = "dotted",
      linewidth = 0.45,
      color = "grey40",
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "historical"),
      aes(x = year, ymin = p_lo, ymax = p_hi, fill = phase),
      alpha = 0.14
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, ymin = p_lo, ymax = p_hi, fill = phase),
      alpha = 0.30
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_lo, color = phase),
      linewidth = 0.45,
      alpha = 0.70,
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_hi, color = phase),
      linewidth = 0.45,
      alpha = 0.70,
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "historical"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_mean, color = phase),
      linewidth = 0.9,
      alpha = 0.90
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_mean, color = phase),
      linewidth = 1.15,
      alpha = 0.95
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(yintercept = K_mean),
      linetype = "dotdash",
      linewidth = 0.55,
      color = "#8A5A00",
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(xintercept = t0_year_mean),
      linetype = "dashed",
      linewidth = 0.55,
      color = "#1D4ED8",
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = region_obs,
      aes(x = year, y = mCPR),
      size = 2,
      alpha = 0.75,
      color = "#B00020"
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ country,
      ncol = ncol_region,
      scales = "free_x",
      labeller = labeller(country = country_labeller)
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = phase_cols) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = phase_cols) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 8.8) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7.4, lineheight = 0.95),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6.9),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6.9),
      plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8.2)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(region_name, ": Observed mCPR vs Posterior Projections"),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Each panel projects 10 years beyond the last DHS survey; darker ribbon and dashed bounds mark the 95% forecast CrI (n = ",
        n_country_region,
        " countries)"
      ),
      x = "Year",
      y = "mCPR",
      caption = "Strip labels report years since inflection at 2026. Grey dotted line = last observed DHS survey year. Blue = inflection year (with 95% CrI band). Ochre = K (with 95% CrI band)."
    )
}

purrr::walk(region_plot_order, function(region_name) {
  print(make_region_country_plot(region_name))
})
```

## Country-level growth rates over time

This section reports the model-implied annual change in mCPR, \(\frac{dp_i(t)}{dt}\), for each country across its observed DHS window and an additional 10-year projection window.

Interpretation:

a) `dpdt_mean` is the posterior mean annual change in mCPR at that calendar year.
b) `dpdt_p05` and `dpdt_p95` are the 95% credible interval bounds for that annual change.
c) `segment` marks whether a given year is inside the observed survey window or in the forward projection period.

```{r country-growth-rate-table}
# Build a full country-year table of model-implied growth rates using the
# analytic derivative of the logistic S-curve.
country_growth_meta <- curve_guides %>%
  transmute(
    country = as.character(country),
    region,
    phase,
    first_obs_year,
    last_obs_year,
    forecast_end_year,
    country_col = match(as.character(country), countries)
  )

country_growth_rate_table <- bind_rows(
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(country_growth_meta)), function(i) {
    meta_i <- country_growth_meta[i, ]
    year_vec <- seq(meta_i$first_obs_year, meta_i$forecast_end_year, by = 1)
    t_vec <- year_vec - 2000

    t_mat_i <- matrix(rep(t_vec, each = n_draws), nrow = n_draws)
    t0_i <- t0_draws[, meta_i$country_col]
    s_i <- s_draws[, meta_i$country_col]
    K_i <- K_draws[, meta_i$country_col]

    z_i <- exp(-sweep(sweep(t_mat_i, 1, t0_i, "-"), 1, s_i, "/"))
    dpdt_i <- sweep(z_i / (1 + z_i)^2, 1, K_i / s_i, "*")

    tibble(
      country = meta_i$country,
      region = meta_i$region,
      phase = meta_i$phase,
      year = year_vec,
      segment = if_else(year_vec <= meta_i$last_obs_year, "historical", "forecast"),
      dpdt_mean = colMeans(dpdt_i),
      dpdt_p05 = apply(dpdt_i, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
      dpdt_p95 = apply(dpdt_i, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
    )
  })
) %>%
  arrange(region, country, year)

# Use a paged table in the HTML report because the full country-year output is long.
rmarkdown::paged_table(
  country_growth_rate_table %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))
)
```

```{r country-growth-rate-plot}
# Plot a readable subset of countries so growth-rate trajectories can be scanned quickly.
pick_growth_plot <- function(df, phase_name, n_pick) {
  df %>%
    filter(phase == phase_name) %>%
    arrange(desc(leverage), country) %>%
    slice_head(n = n_pick)
}

growth_plot_countries <- bind_rows(
  pick_growth_plot(investment_table, "Scale-up", 4),
  pick_growth_plot(investment_table, "Build / monitor", 4),
  pick_growth_plot(investment_table, "Maintain / equity", 4)
) %>%
  distinct(country, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  left_join(
    curve_guides %>%
      transmute(country = as.character(country), last_obs_year),
    by = "country"
  )

growth_plot_data <- country_growth_rate_table %>%
  filter(country %in% growth_plot_countries$country) %>%
  left_join(
    growth_plot_countries %>%
      select(country, phase, leverage, last_obs_year, t0_year_mean),
    by = c("country", "phase")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = growth_plot_countries %>%
        arrange(factor(phase, levels = phase_order), desc(leverage), country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ggplot(growth_plot_data, aes(x = year, y = dpdt_mean, color = phase, fill = phase)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey55", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = last_obs_year),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.45,
    alpha = 0.85,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = dpdt_p05, ymax = dpdt_p95), alpha = 0.18, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.0) +
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = t0_year_mean),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "#1D4ED8",
    linewidth = 0.45,
    alpha = 0.85,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ country, ncol = 4, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_color_manual(values = phase_cols) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phase_cols) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Selected countries: model-implied mCPR growth rates over time",
    subtitle = "Lines show posterior mean annual change in mCPR; ribbons show 95% credible intervals; grey dashed line marks the last observed DHS year and blue dotted line marks the posterior mean inflection year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Annual change in mCPR (dp/dt)"
  )
```

# Sub-Saharan Africa: projected path to 40% mCPR

This section focuses only on the Sub-Saharan Africa analytic regions:
a) West Africa
b) Central Africa
c) East/Southern Africa

The goal is to answer four practical questions:
a) where each country is projected to be over the next 10 years;
b) when each country is projected to reach 40% mCPR;
c) how much annual growth would be needed to get there within 10 years from the latest observed survey;
d) whether that required growth is broadly consistent with both observed inter-survey growth and the model-implied instantaneous growth profile.

```{r ssa-target40-setup}
# Build Sub-Saharan Africa summary objects used by the plots and tables below.
ssa_regions <- c("West Africa", "Central Africa", "East/Southern Africa")
target_mcpr_40 <- 0.40

# Latest observed DHS value by SSA country.
ssa_latest_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions, !is.na(mCPR), !is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(country, region) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    region,
    last_obs_year = year,
    last_obs_mcpr = mCPR
  ) %>%
  arrange(region, country)

ssa_country_order_vec <- ssa_latest_obs$country
ssa_country_cols <- match(ssa_country_order_vec, countries)
if (any(is.na(ssa_country_cols))) {
  stop("Could not match one or more SSA countries to posterior country columns.")
}

# Observation-to-observation annualized growth on the observed DHS scale.
ssa_obs_growth_intervals <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions, !is.na(mCPR), !is.na(year)) %>%
  arrange(country, year) %>%
  group_by(country, region) %>%
  mutate(
    prev_year = lag(year),
    prev_mcpr = lag(mCPR),
    obs_growth_annual = (mCPR - prev_mcpr) / (year - prev_year),
    interval_mid_year = (year + prev_year) / 2
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(obs_growth_annual), is.finite(obs_growth_annual)) %>%
  ungroup()

ssa_obs_growth_summary <- ssa_obs_growth_intervals %>%
  group_by(country, region) %>%
  summarise(
    latest_obs_interval_growth = obs_growth_annual[which.max(year)],
    mean_obs_interval_growth = mean(obs_growth_annual, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_obs_interval_growth = max(obs_growth_annual, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_obs_interval_growth = min(obs_growth_annual, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_intervals = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Country-specific posterior year when mCPR first reaches 40%.
# If a country is already above 40% at the latest observed survey, we report the
# latest observed year as the forward-looking reach year. If K <= 40% in a draw,
# the target is unreachable in that draw.
ssa_last_obs_year_mat <- matrix(
  rep(ssa_latest_obs$last_obs_year, each = n_draws),
  nrow = n_draws,
  ncol = length(ssa_country_cols)
)
ssa_last_obs_mcpr_mat <- matrix(
  rep(ssa_latest_obs$last_obs_mcpr, each = n_draws),
  nrow = n_draws,
  ncol = length(ssa_country_cols)
)

ssa_K_draws <- K_draws[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE]
ssa_t0_draws <- t0_draws[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE]
ssa_s_draws <- s_draws[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE]

reach40_possible_mat <- ssa_K_draws > target_mcpr_40
target_ratio_mat <- pmin(target_mcpr_40 / ssa_K_draws, 0.999999)
t40_year_raw_mat <- 2000 + ssa_t0_draws + ssa_s_draws * qlogis(target_ratio_mat)

ssa_target40_year_mat <- ifelse(
  ssa_last_obs_mcpr_mat >= target_mcpr_40,
  ssa_last_obs_year_mat,
  ifelse(
    reach40_possible_mat,
    pmax(t40_year_raw_mat, ssa_last_obs_year_mat),
    NA_real_
  )
)

ssa_years_to_40_mat <- ssa_target40_year_mat - ssa_last_obs_year_mat
ssa_model_dpdt_lastobs_mat <- dpdt_lastobs_mat[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE]

ssa_target40_table <- tibble(
  country = ssa_latest_obs$country,
  region = ssa_latest_obs$region,
  last_obs_year = ssa_latest_obs$last_obs_year,
  last_obs_mcpr = ssa_latest_obs$last_obs_mcpr,
  prob_ever_reach_40 = colMeans(!is.na(ssa_target40_year_mat)),
  projected_year_reach_40 = apply(ssa_target40_year_mat, 2, function(x) if (all(is.na(x))) NA_real_ else mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)),
  projected_year_reach_40_p05 = apply(ssa_target40_year_mat, 2, function(x) if (all(is.na(x))) NA_real_ else quantile(x, ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE)),
  projected_year_reach_40_p95 = apply(ssa_target40_year_mat, 2, function(x) if (all(is.na(x))) NA_real_ else quantile(x, ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE)),
  projected_years_from_latest_to_40 = apply(ssa_years_to_40_mat, 2, function(x) if (all(is.na(x))) NA_real_ else mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)),
  required_growth_to_40_10y = pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_latest_obs$last_obs_mcpr, 0) / 10,
  model_dpdt_lastobs_mean = colMeans(ssa_model_dpdt_lastobs_mat),
  model_dpdt_lastobs_p05 = apply(ssa_model_dpdt_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  model_dpdt_lastobs_p95 = apply(ssa_model_dpdt_lastobs_mat, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_obs_growth_summary,
    by = c("country", "region")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    investment_table %>%
      transmute(
        country,
        investment_phase = phase,
        leverage,
        K_mean,
        t0_year_mean,
        years_since_t0_2026,
        dpdt_mean_2026 = dpdt_mean
      ),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    s_curve_stage_table %>%
      transmute(
        country,
        s_curve_stage,
        last_obs_rel_sat_mean,
        years_since_t0_lastobs_mean
      ),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    target40_status = case_when(
      last_obs_mcpr >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Already at or above 40%",
      prob_ever_reach_40 < 0.50 ~ "Not expected to reach 40%",
      TRUE ~ "Projected to reach 40%"
    ),
    required_growth_pp_per_year = 100 * required_growth_to_40_10y,
    latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year = 100 * latest_obs_interval_growth,
    max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year = 100 * max_obs_interval_growth,
    model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year = 100 * model_dpdt_lastobs_mean,
    model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year = 100 * dpdt_mean_2026
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(region, levels = ssa_regions),
    desc(target40_status == "Projected to reach 40%"),
    projected_year_reach_40,
    country
  )
```

## Sub-Saharan Africa: observed trajectories and 10-year projections

Interpretation:
a) red points are observed DHS mCPR values;
b) solid colored lines are posterior mean trajectories;
c) ribbons are 95% credible intervals;
d) the grey dotted line marks the latest observed DHS survey year;
e) the blue dashed line marks the posterior mean inflection year;
f) the grey horizontal line marks the 40% target;
g) the ochre line marks the fixed regional ceiling \(K\) used for that country.

```{r ssa-country-projections}
# Plot SSA country trajectories and 10-year projections, grouped by SSA subregion.
ssa_curve_guides <- curve_guides %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_target40_table %>%
      select(country, target40_status, projected_year_reach_40),
    by = c("country" = "country")
  )

ssa_country_labeller <- setNames(
  paste0(
    as.character(ssa_curve_guides$country),
    "\n40%: ",
    dplyr::case_when(
      ssa_curve_guides$target40_status == "Already at or above 40%" ~ "already reached",
      ssa_curve_guides$target40_status == "Not expected to reach 40%" ~ "not expected",
      TRUE ~ as.character(round(ssa_curve_guides$projected_year_reach_40))
    )
  ),
  as.character(ssa_curve_guides$country)
)

make_ssa_projection_plot <- function(region_name) {
  region_guides <- ssa_curve_guides %>%
    filter(region == region_name)

  region_summ <- summ %>%
    filter(region == region_name, country %in% region_guides$country)

  region_obs <- obs_subset %>%
    filter(region == region_name, country %in% region_guides$country)

  n_country_region <- nrow(region_guides)
  ncol_region <- dplyr::case_when(
    n_country_region <= 4 ~ 2L,
    n_country_region <= 9 ~ 3L,
    n_country_region <= 16 ~ 4L,
    TRUE ~ 5L
  )

  ggplot() +
    geom_hline(yintercept = target_mcpr_40, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey55", linewidth = 0.45) +
    geom_vline(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(xintercept = last_obs_year),
      linetype = "dotted",
      linewidth = 0.45,
      color = "grey40",
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "historical"),
      aes(x = year, ymin = p_lo, ymax = p_hi, fill = phase),
      alpha = 0.14
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, ymin = p_lo, ymax = p_hi, fill = phase),
      alpha = 0.26
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_lo, color = phase),
      linewidth = 0.42,
      alpha = 0.65,
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_hi, color = phase),
      linewidth = 0.42,
      alpha = 0.65,
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "historical"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_mean, color = phase),
      linewidth = 0.9,
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_summ %>% filter(segment == "forecast"),
      aes(x = year, y = p_mean, color = phase),
      linewidth = 1.1,
      alpha = 0.95
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(yintercept = K_mean),
      linetype = "dotdash",
      linewidth = 0.55,
      color = "#8A5A00",
      alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      data = region_guides,
      aes(xintercept = t0_year_mean),
      linetype = "dashed",
      linewidth = 0.5,
      color = "#1D4ED8",
      alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = region_obs,
      aes(x = year, y = mCPR),
      size = 2,
      alpha = 0.78,
      color = "#B00020"
    ) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~ country,
      ncol = ncol_region,
      scales = "free_x",
      labeller = labeller(country = ssa_country_labeller)
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = phase_cols) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = phase_cols) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7.4, lineheight = 0.95),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6.8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6.8),
      plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8.0)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(region_name, ": observed mCPR and projected next 10 years"),
      subtitle = paste0("Panels show posterior mean plus 95% credible interval; strip labels report the projected year the country reaches 40% mCPR where applicable (n = ", n_country_region, " countries)"),
      x = "Year",
      y = "mCPR",
      caption = "Grey dotted line = latest observed DHS year. Blue dashed line = posterior mean inflection year. Ochre line = fixed regional K. Grey dashed horizontal line = 40% target."
    )
}

purrr::walk(ssa_regions, function(region_name) {
  print(make_ssa_projection_plot(region_name))
})
```

## Sub-Saharan Africa: projected year each country reaches 40% mCPR

Interpretation:
a) points show the posterior mean projected year a country reaches 40% mCPR;
b) horizontal bars show 95% credible intervals;
c) countries already at or above 40% are shown at the latest observed survey year;
d) countries not expected to reach 40% under the current fitted trajectory are shown with an `x` marker and a text label.

```{r ssa-year-reach40-plot}
# Plot projected year to 40% mCPR for all SSA countries. Countries not
# expected to reach 40% are shown with an x-marker and label at the top of the plot.
ssa_year40_plot_cap <- max(ssa_target40_table$projected_year_reach_40_p95, na.rm = TRUE) + 2

ssa_year40_plot_data <- ssa_target40_table %>%
  mutate(
    year40_plot = dplyr::case_when(
      target40_status == "Not expected to reach 40%" ~ ssa_year40_plot_cap,
      TRUE ~ projected_year_reach_40
    ),
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_target40_table %>%
        arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), projected_year_reach_40, country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ggplot(ssa_year40_plot_data, aes(x = country, color = region)) +
  geom_pointrange(
    data = ssa_year40_plot_data %>% filter(target40_status != "Not expected to reach 40%"),
    aes(y = projected_year_reach_40, ymin = projected_year_reach_40_p05, ymax = projected_year_reach_40_p95),
    linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_year40_plot_data %>% filter(target40_status == "Not expected to reach 40%"),
    aes(y = year40_plot),
    shape = 4,
    size = 2.8,
    stroke = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = ssa_year40_plot_data %>% filter(target40_status == "Not expected to reach 40%"),
    aes(y = year40_plot + 0.6, label = "Not projected"),
    color = "grey25",
    size = 3,
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
  labs(
    title = "Sub-Saharan Africa: projected year each country reaches 40% mCPR",
    subtitle = "Points are posterior means and horizontal bars are 95% credible intervals; x-marks indicate countries not projected to reach 40%",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Projected year reaching 40% mCPR"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())
```

## Sub-Saharan Africa: required versus observed and model-implied growth

Interpretation:
a) the red horizontal line is the annual growth required to move from the latest observed mCPR to 40% within 10 years;
b) black points and lines are observed inter-survey annualized growth rates;
c) the colored curve and ribbon are the model-implied instantaneous annual growth rate and its 95% credible interval;
d) this comparison shows whether the 40% target is close to, or well above, the growth rates already observed and implied by the fitted trajectory.

```{r ssa-growth-comparison-plots}
# Compare observed interval growth, model-implied instantaneous growth, and the
# annual growth required to hit 40% mCPR in 10 years from the latest survey.
ssa_growth_plot_base <- country_growth_rate_table %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_target40_table %>%
      select(country, required_growth_pp_per_year, last_obs_year, t0_year_mean, target40_status),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dpdt_mean_pp = 100 * dpdt_mean,
    dpdt_p05_pp = 100 * dpdt_p05,
    dpdt_p95_pp = 100 * dpdt_p95
  )

ssa_obs_growth_plot <- ssa_obs_growth_intervals %>%
  mutate(obs_growth_pp = 100 * obs_growth_annual)

make_ssa_growth_plot <- function(region_name) {
  region_growth <- ssa_growth_plot_base %>%
    filter(region == region_name)

  region_obs_growth <- ssa_obs_growth_plot %>%
    filter(region == region_name)

  region_order <- ssa_target40_table %>%
    filter(region == region_name) %>%
    arrange(projected_year_reach_40, desc(required_growth_pp_per_year), country) %>%
    pull(country)

  n_country_region <- length(region_order)
  ncol_region <- dplyr::case_when(
    n_country_region <= 4 ~ 2L,
    n_country_region <= 9 ~ 3L,
    n_country_region <= 16 ~ 4L,
    TRUE ~ 5L
  )

  region_growth <- region_growth %>%
    mutate(country = factor(country, levels = region_order))

  region_obs_growth <- region_obs_growth %>%
    mutate(country = factor(country, levels = region_order))

  ggplot(region_growth, aes(x = year, y = dpdt_mean_pp, color = phase, fill = phase)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey55", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_hline(
      aes(yintercept = required_growth_pp_per_year),
      color = "#C0392B",
      linewidth = 0.55,
      linetype = "dotdash",
      alpha = 0.9,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      aes(xintercept = last_obs_year),
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "grey40",
      linewidth = 0.45,
      alpha = 0.85,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      aes(xintercept = t0_year_mean),
      linetype = "dotted",
      color = "#1D4ED8",
      linewidth = 0.45,
      alpha = 0.85,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = dpdt_p05_pp, ymax = dpdt_p95_pp), alpha = 0.18, color = NA) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.95) +
    geom_line(
      data = region_obs_growth,
      aes(x = interval_mid_year, y = obs_growth_pp, group = country),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "grey20",
      linewidth = 0.55,
      alpha = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = region_obs_growth,
      aes(x = interval_mid_year, y = obs_growth_pp),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "grey15",
      size = 1.5,
      alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ country, ncol = ncol_region, scales = "free_x") +
    scale_color_manual(values = phase_cols) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = phase_cols) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 9.5) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7.3)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(region_name, ": growth needed versus growth observed"),
      subtitle = "Red horizontal line = annual growth required to reach 40% in 10 years; black points/line = observed inter-survey annualized growth; colored curve = model-implied instantaneous annual growth",
      x = "Year",
      y = "Annual growth (percentage points per year)"
    )
}

purrr::walk(ssa_regions, function(region_name) {
  print(make_ssa_growth_plot(region_name))
})
```

## Sub-Saharan Africa summary table

This table combines the main planning quantities for each Sub-Saharan Africa country:
a) latest observed DHS mCPR;
b) fixed regional ceiling \(K\);
c) fitted inflection year and years since inflection at the latest observed survey;
d) investment phase and S-curve stage;
e) observed, required, and model-implied annual growth rates;
f) projected year reaching 40% mCPR.

```{r ssa-summary-table}
# Print a compact SSA planning table with the key quantities needed to judge the
# realism of a 40% mCPR target.
ssa_summary_table <- ssa_target40_table %>%
  select(
    country,
    region,
    last_obs_year,
    last_obs_mcpr,
    K_mean,
    t0_year_mean,
    years_since_t0_lastobs_mean,
    model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year,
    model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year,
    latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    required_growth_pp_per_year,
    investment_phase,
    s_curve_stage,
    projected_year_reach_40,
    target40_status
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), projected_year_reach_40, country)

knitr::kable(
  ssa_summary_table %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Sub-Saharan Africa summary: stage, growth, and projected year reaching 40% mCPR"
)
```

# Goals: Sub-Saharan Africa relative to 40% mCPR

This section reframes the SSA results around a single planning question: how close are the region and its countries to a 40% mCPR target, and how demanding would it be to reach that target within 10 years?

The goals section deliberately separates two decision lenses:
a) the **natural-trajectory lens**, which asks whether countries and the weighted SSA region reach 40% under the fitted logistic model as estimated;
b) the **intervention lens**, which asks what annual growth rate would be required from the model-implied `p(2026)` to reach 40% within 10 years even if the fitted `K` suggests the natural trajectory would plateau below that level.

For the regional target analysis, we do **not** fit a separate SSA logistic curve. Instead, we:
a) take the fitted country-level posterior trajectories from the final model;
b) aggregate them into an SSA regional trajectory using WPP women age 15-49 population weights;
c) carry the latest available WPP weights forward for post-2023 forecast years, because the current weights file was built from the WPP `Estimates` sheet.

This keeps the regional target analysis consistent with the fitted country model while still weighting countries by population size.

```{r ssa-goals-setup}
# Build a population-weighted SSA regional target summary by aggregating the
# fitted country posteriors using WPP women age 15-49 weights.
ssa_pop_weights <- read.csv("country_pop_weights_15_49.csv") %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions)

ssa_weight_countries <- ssa_target40_table %>%
  distinct(country, region)

ssa_pop_weights_model <- ssa_pop_weights %>%
  filter(country %in% ssa_weight_countries$country)

missing_ssa_weights <- setdiff(ssa_weight_countries$country, unique(ssa_pop_weights_model$country))
if (length(missing_ssa_weights) > 0) {
  stop("Missing SSA population weights for: ", paste(missing_ssa_weights, collapse = ", "))
}

latest_wpp_year <- max(ssa_pop_weights_model$Year, na.rm = TRUE)

ssa_weight_latest <- ssa_pop_weights_model %>%
  filter(Year == latest_wpp_year) %>%
  select(country, women_15_49)

ssa_goal_years <- seq(
  min(mcpr_logistic_data$year[mcpr_logistic_data$region %in% ssa_regions], na.rm = TRUE),
  2036,
  by = 1
)

ssa_nd_region <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  distinct(country, region, region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
  mutate(
    country = as.character(country),
    region_prior = as.character(region_prior)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(year = ssa_goal_years) %>%
  mutate(
    weight_year = pmin(year, latest_wpp_year),
    year_center = year - 2000,
    n = 1L,
    y = 0L
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_pop_weights_model %>%
      select(country, Year, women_15_49),
    by = c("country", "weight_year" = "Year")
  ) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  mutate(weight_country = women_15_49 / sum(women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

if (any(is.na(ssa_nd_region$weight_country))) {
  stop("SSA regional prediction grid contains missing population weights.")
}

ep_ssa_region_country <- posterior_epred(
  fit_logistic,
  newdata = ssa_nd_region,
  re_formula = NULL
)

ssa_region_draws <- sapply(ssa_goal_years, function(yy) {
  idx <- which(ssa_nd_region$year == yy)
  ep_ssa_region_country[, idx, drop = FALSE] %*% ssa_nd_region$weight_country[idx]
})

ssa_region_curve <- tibble(
  year = ssa_goal_years,
  p_mean = colMeans(ssa_region_draws),
  p_p05 = apply(ssa_region_draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  p_p95 = apply(ssa_region_draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
)

# Observed weighted SSA points use country-year DHS observations and the WPP
# women 15-49 weight for that same calendar year. These are descriptive only,
# because the country composition differs across survey years.
ssa_observed_weighted <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  inner_join(
    ssa_pop_weights_model %>%
      select(country, Year, women_15_49),
    by = c("country", "year" = "Year")
  ) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    observed_weighted_mcpr = weighted.mean(mCPR, women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_countries_obs = n_distinct(country),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ssa_region_p2026_draws <- as.numeric(ssa_region_draws[, match(2026, ssa_goal_years)])
ssa_region_p2025_draws <- as.numeric(ssa_region_draws[, match(2025, ssa_goal_years)])
ssa_region_required_growth_draws <- pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_region_p2026_draws, 0) / 10
ssa_region_required_growth_2025_draws <- pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_region_p2025_draws, 0) / 10

ssa_weight_vec_latest <- ssa_weight_latest$women_15_49[match(ssa_latest_obs$country, ssa_weight_latest$country)]
ssa_weight_vec_latest <- ssa_weight_vec_latest / sum(ssa_weight_vec_latest, na.rm = TRUE)

ssa_region_dpdt_2025_draws <- as.numeric(dpdt_goal_base_mat[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE] %*% ssa_weight_vec_latest)
ssa_region_dpdt_2026_draws <- as.numeric(dpdt_mat[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE] %*% ssa_weight_vec_latest)

ssa_region_year40_draws <- apply(ssa_region_draws, 1, function(x) {
  idx <- which(x >= target_mcpr_40)
  if (length(idx) == 0) NA_real_ else ssa_goal_years[min(idx)]
})

ssa_region_goals_summary <- tibble(
  geography = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
  weight_basis = paste0("Women 15-49 from WPP Estimates; ", latest_wpp_year, " weights carried forward after 2023"),
  last_obs_weighted_mean = weighted.mean(ssa_target40_table$last_obs_mcpr, ssa_weight_vec_latest, na.rm = TRUE),
  p2025_mean = mean(ssa_region_p2025_draws),
  p2025_p05 = quantile(ssa_region_p2025_draws, ci_lower),
  p2025_p95 = quantile(ssa_region_p2025_draws, ci_upper),
  p2026_mean = mean(ssa_region_p2026_draws),
  p2026_p05 = quantile(ssa_region_p2026_draws, ci_lower),
  p2026_p95 = quantile(ssa_region_p2026_draws, ci_upper),
  required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year =
    100 * pmax(target_mcpr_40 - weighted.mean(ssa_target40_table$last_obs_mcpr, ssa_weight_vec_latest, na.rm = TRUE), 0) / 10,
  required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(ssa_region_required_growth_2025_draws),
  required_growth_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(ssa_region_required_growth_draws),
  required_growth_pp_per_year_p05 = 100 * quantile(ssa_region_required_growth_draws, ci_lower),
  required_growth_pp_per_year_p95 = 100 * quantile(ssa_region_required_growth_draws, ci_upper),
  model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(ssa_region_dpdt_2025_draws),
  model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year_p05 = 100 * quantile(ssa_region_dpdt_2025_draws, ci_lower),
  model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year_p95 = 100 * quantile(ssa_region_dpdt_2025_draws, ci_upper),
  model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(ssa_region_dpdt_2026_draws),
  model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year_p05 = 100 * quantile(ssa_region_dpdt_2026_draws, ci_lower),
  model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year_p95 = 100 * quantile(ssa_region_dpdt_2026_draws, ci_upper),
  observed_latest_interval_growth_pp_per_year =
    weighted.mean(ssa_target40_table$latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year, ssa_weight_vec_latest, na.rm = TRUE),
  observed_max_interval_growth_pp_per_year =
    weighted.mean(ssa_target40_table$max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year, ssa_weight_vec_latest, na.rm = TRUE),
  projected_year_reach_40_p50 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_year40_draws))) NA_real_ else median(ssa_region_year40_draws, na.rm = TRUE),
  projected_year_reach_40 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_year40_draws))) NA_real_ else mean(ssa_region_year40_draws, na.rm = TRUE),
  projected_year_reach_40_p05 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_year40_draws))) NA_real_ else quantile(ssa_region_year40_draws, ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
  projected_year_reach_40_p95 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_year40_draws))) NA_real_ else quantile(ssa_region_year40_draws, ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
  prob_reach_40_by_2035 = mean(!is.na(ssa_region_year40_draws) & ssa_region_year40_draws <= 2035),
  prob_reach_40_by_2036 = mean(!is.na(ssa_region_year40_draws) & ssa_region_year40_draws <= 2036)
)

ssa_p2025_draws <- p_goal_base_mat[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE]

ssa_p2025_country_summary <- tibble(
  country = ssa_country_order_vec,
  p2025_mean = colMeans(ssa_p2025_draws),
  p2025_p05 = apply(ssa_p2025_draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  p2025_p95 = apply(ssa_p2025_draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
  prob_reach_40_by_2035 = colMeans(!is.na(ssa_target40_year_mat) & ssa_target40_year_mat <= 2035)
)

ssa_goals_country_table <- ssa_target40_table %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_p2025_country_summary,
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    investment_table %>%
      transmute(country, p2026_mean = p_mean, K_p05, K_p95),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    s_curve_stage_2026_table %>%
      transmute(country, s_curve_stage_2026),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_weight_latest %>%
      select(country, women_15_49),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weight_share_ssa = women_15_49 / sum(women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE),
    target_gap_pp = 100 * (target_mcpr_40 - last_obs_mcpr),
    target_gap_p2025_pp = 100 * (target_mcpr_40 - p2025_mean),
    target_gap_p2026_pp = 100 * (target_mcpr_40 - p2026_mean),
    projected_reach_40_by_2036 = !is.na(projected_year_reach_40) & projected_year_reach_40 <= 2036,
    crossing_label = case_when(
      last_obs_mcpr >= target_mcpr_40 ~ paste0("Already crossed (", last_obs_year, ")"),
      is.na(projected_year_reach_40_p50) ~ "Not projected",
      projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2035 ~ paste0("By 2035 (~", round(projected_year_reach_40_p50), ")"),
      TRUE ~ paste0("After 2035 (~", round(projected_year_reach_40_p50), ")")
    ),
    required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year = pmax(target_gap_pp, 0) / 10,
    required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year = pmax(target_gap_p2025_pp, 0) / 10,
    required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year = pmax(target_gap_p2026_pp, 0) / 10,
    growth_gap_vs_latest_obs_pp = required_growth_pp_per_year - latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    growth_gap_vs_model_pp = required_growth_pp_per_year - model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), projected_year_reach_40_p50, country)

ssa_gap_lastobs_plot <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    country = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
    region = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
    required_growth_pp_per_year = ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year
  ),
  ssa_goals_country_table %>%
    transmute(
      country,
      region,
      required_growth_pp_per_year = required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year
    )
) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = c(
        "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
        ssa_goals_country_table %>%
          arrange(desc(required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year), country) %>%
          pull(country)
      )
    )
  )

ssa_gap_p2025_plot <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    country = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
    region = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
    required_growth_pp_per_year = ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year
  ),
  ssa_goals_country_table %>%
    transmute(
      country,
      region,
      required_growth_pp_per_year = required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year
    )
 ) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = c(
        "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
        ssa_goals_country_table %>%
          arrange(desc(required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year), country) %>%
          pull(country)
      )
    )
  )

ssa_gap_p2026_intervention_plot <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    country = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
    region = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
    required_growth_pp_per_year = ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_pp_per_year
  ),
  ssa_goals_country_table %>%
    transmute(
      country,
      region,
      required_growth_pp_per_year = required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year,
      target40_status
    )
) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = c(
        "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
        ssa_goals_country_table %>%
          arrange(desc(required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year), country) %>%
          pull(country)
      )
    )
  )

ssa_goal_comparison_long <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  select(
    country, region, required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year,
    latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
      max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
      model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year
    ),
    names_to = "comparison_metric",
    values_to = "comparison_growth_pp_per_year"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    comparison_metric = factor(
      comparison_metric,
      levels = c(
        "latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year",
        "max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year",
        "model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Latest observed interval growth",
        "Max observed interval growth",
        "Model-implied current growth"
      )
    )
  )

ssa_priority_matrix_data <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  mutate(
    priority_label = crossing_label,
    weight_share_ssa_pct = 100 * weight_share_ssa,
    prob_reach_40_by_2035_pct = 100 * prob_reach_40_by_2035
  )

# Quantify which countries move the weighted SSA trend the most and which ones
# matter most for the 40% regional goal under counterfactual scenarios.
ssa_p2026_draws_country <- p_mat[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE]
ssa_p2035_draws <- logistic_draws_at_year(ssa_K_draws, ssa_t0_draws, ssa_s_draws, 2035)

ssa_contribution_2026_mat <- sweep(ssa_p2026_draws_country, 2, ssa_weight_vec_latest, `*`)
ssa_contribution_2035_mat <- sweep(ssa_p2035_draws, 2, ssa_weight_vec_latest, `*`)
ssa_contribution_delta_mat <- ssa_contribution_2035_mat - ssa_contribution_2026_mat

ssa_region_2035_draws <- rowSums(ssa_contribution_2035_mat)
```
