```{r ssa-goals-setup}
ssa_marginal_if40_mat <- sweep(matrix(target_mcpr_40, nrow = nrow(ssa_contribution_2035_mat), ncol = ncol(ssa_contribution_2035_mat)), 2, ssa_weight_vec_latest, `*`) - ssa_contribution_2035_mat

ssa_regional_influence_table <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  mutate(
    women_15_49_millions = women_15_49 / 1e6,
    contribution_2026_pp = 100 * colMeans(ssa_contribution_2026_mat),
    contribution_2035_pp = 100 * colMeans(ssa_contribution_2035_mat),
    delta_contribution_pp = 100 * colMeans(ssa_contribution_delta_mat),
    marginal_lift_if40_pp = 100 * colMeans(ssa_marginal_if40_mat),
    marginal_lift_if40_p05 = 100 * apply(ssa_marginal_if40_mat, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
    marginal_lift_if40_p95 = 100 * apply(ssa_marginal_if40_mat, 2, quantile, ci_upper),
    expected_goal_contribution_pp = marginal_lift_if40_pp * prob_reach_40_by_2035,
    corr_with_regional_2035 = vapply(
      seq_len(ncol(ssa_p2035_draws)),
      function(j) cor(ssa_p2035_draws[, j], ssa_region_2035_draws),
      numeric(1)
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(delta_contribution_pp), desc(expected_goal_contribution_pp), country)

ssa_regional_delta_plot <- ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
        arrange(delta_contribution_pp, country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ssa_regional_marginal_plot <- ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
        arrange(marginal_lift_if40_pp, country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ssa_regional_uncertainty_plot <- ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
        arrange(corr_with_regional_2035, country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

# Build a focused set of illustrative trajectories for the goals section. These
# are the countries we use to show retrospective crossings, prospective
# crossings, and no-crossing cases relative to the 40% target and the fitted K.
ssa_focus_candidates <- c("Rwanda", "Ethiopia", "Kenya", "Malawi", "Uganda", "Tanzania", "Nigeria")
ssa_focus_fallback <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  arrange(desc(weight_share_ssa), country) %>%
  pull(country)
ssa_focus_countries <- unique(c(
  ssa_focus_candidates[ssa_focus_candidates %in% ssa_goals_country_table$country],
  ssa_focus_fallback
))
ssa_focus_countries <- head(ssa_focus_countries, 7)

ssa_focus_meta <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  filter(country %in% ssa_focus_countries) %>%
  mutate(
    trajectory_status = case_when(
      target40_status == "Already crossed" ~ "Already crossed",
      target40_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ "Not projected to cross",
      target40_status == "Projected to cross by 2035" ~ "Projected to cross by 2035",
      TRUE ~ "Projected to cross after 2035"
    ),
    crossing_year_plot = case_when(
      target40_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ projected_year_reach_40_p50
    )
  )

ssa_focus_order <- c(
  ssa_focus_meta %>%
    filter(trajectory_status == "Already crossed") %>%
    arrange(projected_year_reach_40_p50, country) %>%
    pull(country),
  ssa_focus_meta %>%
    filter(trajectory_status == "Projected to cross by 2035") %>%
    arrange(projected_year_reach_40_p50, country) %>%
    pull(country),
  ssa_focus_meta %>%
    filter(trajectory_status == "Projected to cross after 2035") %>%
    arrange(projected_year_reach_40_p50, country) %>%
    pull(country),
  ssa_focus_meta %>%
    filter(trajectory_status == "Not projected to cross") %>%
    arrange(desc(weight_share_ssa), country) %>%
    pull(country)
)

ssa_focus_years <- seq(
  min(mcpr_logistic_data$year[mcpr_logistic_data$country %in% ssa_focus_countries], na.rm = TRUE),
  2045,
  by = 1
)

ssa_focus_projection <- bind_rows(
  lapply(ssa_focus_order, function(ctry) {
    j <- match(ctry, countries)
    p_draws_country <- sapply(ssa_focus_years, function(yy) {
      K_draws[, j] / (1 + exp(-(((yy - 2000) - t0_draws[, j]) / s_draws[, j])))
    })
    tibble(
      country = ctry,
      year = ssa_focus_years,
      p_med = apply(p_draws_country, 2, median),
      p_lo = apply(p_draws_country, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
      p_hi = apply(p_draws_country, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
    )
  })
) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_focus_meta %>%
      select(
        country, region, last_obs_year, last_obs_mcpr, K_mean,
        crossing_year_plot, trajectory_status
      ),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(country, levels = ssa_focus_order),
    segment = if_else(year <= last_obs_year, "Retrospective window", "Prospective window")
  )

ssa_focus_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(country %in% ssa_focus_countries, region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  mutate(country = factor(country, levels = ssa_focus_order))

ssa_focus_annotations <- ssa_focus_meta %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(country, levels = ssa_focus_order),
    annotation_year = pmin(if_else(is.na(crossing_year_plot), 2042, crossing_year_plot + 0.6), 2044),
    annotation_y = pmin(98, 100 * pmax(K_mean - 0.03, 0.16)),
    annotation_label = case_when(
      trajectory_status == "Already crossed" ~ "Already crossed",
      trajectory_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ "Not projected to cross",
      trajectory_status == "Projected to cross by 2035" ~ paste0("Projected by 2035 (~", round(crossing_year_plot), ")"),
      TRUE ~ paste0("Projected after 2035 (~", round(crossing_year_plot), ")")
    )
  )

ssa_asymptote_lastobs <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  filter(last_obs_mcpr < target_mcpr_40) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_goals_country_table %>%
        filter(last_obs_mcpr < target_mcpr_40) %>%
        arrange(desc(K_mean), country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ssa_asymptote_p2025 <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  filter(p2025_mean < target_mcpr_40) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_goals_country_table %>%
        filter(p2025_mean < target_mcpr_40) %>%
        arrange(desc(K_mean), country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )
```

## SSA regional target: weighted trajectory and uncertainty

This graph shows the population-weighted SSA trajectory implied by the fitted country model. Each country contributes to the SSA regional value in proportion to its WPP women age 15-49 population.

Interpretation:
a) black line is the posterior mean weighted SSA mCPR trajectory;
b) grey ribbon is the 95% credible interval;
c) red points are population-weighted observed SSA country-year mCPR values, using WPP women age 15-49 weights for the observed year;
d) the dashed horizontal line is the 40% target;
e) the darker vertical guides mark 2030 and 2035;
f) the annotation box states whether the weighted SSA trajectory reaches 40% under business as usual, when that crossing is projected to happen, and the posterior probability of reaching 40% by 2035.

```{r ssa-goals-region-trajectory}
ssa_region_crossing_note <- if (is.na(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50)) {
  paste0(
    "BAU weighted SSA trajectory does not reach 40%\n",
    "within the modeled window.\n",
    "P(reach 40% by 2035): ",
    round(100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$prob_reach_40_by_2035, 1),
    "%"
  )
} else if (ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2035) {
  paste0(
    "BAU weighted SSA trajectory reaches 40%\n",
    "around ",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50, 1),
    " (95% CrI ",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p05, 1),
    "-",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p95, 1),
    ").\n",
    "P(reach 40% by 2035): ",
    round(100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$prob_reach_40_by_2035, 1),
    "%"
  )
} else {
  paste0(
    "BAU weighted SSA trajectory reaches 40%\n",
    "after 2035, around ",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50, 1),
    " (95% CrI ",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p05, 1),
    "-",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p95, 1),
    ").\n",
    "P(reach 40% by 2035): ",
    round(100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$prob_reach_40_by_2035, 1),
    "%"
  )
}

ssa_region_crossing_point <- if (is.na(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50)) {
  tibble(year = numeric(0), p_mean = numeric(0))
} else {
  ssa_region_curve %>%
    filter(year == round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50)) %>%
    transmute(year, p_mean = target_mcpr_40)
}

ssa_region_x_breaks <- sort(unique(c(
  seq(
    1985,
    2035,
    by = 5
  ),
  2030,
  2035
)))

ssa_region_annotation_y <- max(
  c(
    ssa_region_curve$p_p95,
    ssa_observed_weighted$observed_weighted_mcpr,
    target_mcpr_40
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

ggplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = target_mcpr_40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#8B0000", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2030, linetype = "longdash", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.65) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2035, linetype = "longdash", color = "grey15", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = ssa_region_curve,
    aes(x = year, ymin = p_p05, ymax = p_p95),
    fill = "grey55",
    alpha = 0.22
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = ssa_region_curve,
    aes(x = year, y = p_mean),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1.05
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_observed_weighted,
    aes(x = year, y = observed_weighted_mcpr),
    color = "#B00020",
    alpha = 0.8,
    size = 2.2
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_region_crossing_point,
    aes(x = year, y = p_mean),
    shape = 21,
    size = 3.4,
    stroke = 0.95,
    fill = "white",
    color = "#8B0000"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = 2026.2,
    y = ssa_region_annotation_y,
    label = ssa_region_crossing_note,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 3.15,
    label.size = 0.2,
    fill = "white",
    color = "grey15"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = ssa_region_x_breaks,
    limits = range(ssa_region_curve$year, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(round(100 * x), "%")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Sub-Saharan Africa: population-weighted mCPR trajectory relative to 40%",
    subtitle = "Country posterior predictions are aggregated with WPP women age 15-49 weights; the 2030 and 2035 markers highlight the decision horizon for the regional 40% goal",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Population-weighted mCPR (%)"
  )
```

## SSA regional target summary

This table summarizes the regional goal quantities using the weighted SSA trajectory:
a) `last_obs_weighted_mean`: weighted SSA latest observed mCPR across countries;
b) `p2025_mean`: weighted SSA mCPR in 2025 under the fitted model;
c) `p2026_mean`: weighted SSA mCPR in 2026 under the fitted model;
d) `required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year`: annual increase needed to move from the weighted latest observed mCPR to 40% within 10 years;
e) `required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year`: annual increase needed to move from weighted `p(2025)` to 40% within 10 years;
f) `model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year`: weighted model-implied SSA growth rate in 2026;
g) `projected_year_reach_40_p50`: weighted SSA median projected year of reaching 40%;
h) `prob_reach_40_by_2035`: posterior probability that SSA reaches 40% by 2035.
i) `required_growth_pp_per_year`: annual increase needed to move from weighted `p(2026)` to 40% within 10 years, regardless of the fitted ceiling.

```{r ssa-goals-region-summary}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_region_goals_summary %>%
    select(
      geography, last_obs_weighted_mean, p2025_mean, p2026_mean,
      required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year, required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      required_growth_pp_per_year,
      model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year, observed_latest_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
      observed_max_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
      projected_year_reach_40_p50, prob_reach_40_by_2035
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "SSA regional target summary using WPP population weights"
)
```

## SSA regional annualized growth rate benchmark using population-weighted mCPR

This regional benchmark plot keeps the focus entirely at the weighted SSA level. It compares the annualized growth rate needed to hit 40% within 10 years against three weighted regional growth benchmarks:
a) the weighted latest observed inter-survey growth;
b) the weighted maximum observed inter-survey growth;
c) the weighted model-implied annual growth at 2026.

This is the cleanest single-region feasibility view in the report because it asks whether the weighted SSA region is already growing fast enough, would need to return to historically high growth, or would need growth beyond what the region has recently exhibited.

```{r ssa-goals-region-growth-benchmark}
ssa_region_growth_benchmark <- tibble(
  metric = c(
    "Required from weighted latest observed mCPR",
    "Required from weighted p(2025)",
    "Required from weighted p(2026)",
    "Weighted latest observed interval growth",
    "Weighted maximum observed interval growth",
    "Weighted model-implied growth at 2026"
  ),
  metric_group = c(
    "Required growth",
    "Required growth",
    "Required growth",
    "Observed / model benchmark",
    "Observed / model benchmark",
    "Observed / model benchmark"
  ),
  value = c(
    ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year,
    ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_pp_per_year,
    ssa_region_goals_summary$observed_latest_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    ssa_region_goals_summary$observed_max_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    ssa_region_goals_summary$model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    metric = factor(metric, levels = rev(metric))
  )

ggplot(ssa_region_growth_benchmark, aes(x = metric, y = value, fill = metric_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Required growth" = "#B91C1C",
      "Observed / model benchmark" = "#1D4ED8"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.8) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA regional annualized growth rate benchmark using population-weighted mCPR",
    subtitle = "Red bars are the weighted annual growth rates required to hit 40% within 10 years; blue bars are the weighted observed and model-implied growth benchmarks",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Annual growth (percentage points per year)"
  )
```

## SSA regional level and target milestones

This chart summarizes the weighted SSA regional level relative to the 40% target under three reference points:
a) the weighted latest observed mCPR;
b) the weighted model-estimated `p(2025)`;
c) the weighted model-estimated `p(2026)`.

The annotation box then reports the region's projected year of reaching 40% and the posterior probability of reaching 40% by 2035.

```{r ssa-goals-region-milestones}
ssa_region_level_benchmark <- tibble(
  metric = c(
    "Weighted latest observed mCPR",
    "Weighted p(2025)",
    "Weighted p(2026)",
    "40% target"
  ),
  value = c(
    100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$last_obs_weighted_mean,
    100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$p2025_mean,
    100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$p2026_mean,
    40
  )
) %>%
  mutate(metric = factor(metric, levels = metric))

ssa_region_milestone_note <- paste0(
  "Projected year reaching 40%: ",
  round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50, 1),
  "\nProbability of reaching 40% by 2035: ",
  round(100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$prob_reach_40_by_2035, 1),
  "%"
)

ggplot(ssa_region_level_benchmark, aes(x = metric, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#7F1D1D", linewidth = 0.45) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = 3.35,
    y = max(ssa_region_level_benchmark$value) + 6,
    label = ssa_region_milestone_note,
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 3.2,
    label.size = 0.2,
    fill = "white",
    color = "grey15"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Weighted latest observed mCPR" = "#111827",
      "Weighted p(2025)" = "#60A5FA",
      "Weighted p(2026)" = "#1D4ED8",
      "40% target" = "#F59E0B"
    )
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(ssa_region_level_benchmark$value) + 8)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.8) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA regional level milestones using population-weighted mCPR",
    subtitle = "Bars summarize the weighted regional level at the latest observed point, in 2025, and in 2026 relative to the 40% target",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Population-weighted mCPR (%)"
  )
```

## SSA gap analysis using the latest observed mCPR

This chart answers the 40% target question using the latest observed mCPR, even if the latest survey is older than 2025.

Interpretation:
a) each bar is the annual percentage-point growth needed to reach 40% within 10 years from the latest observed mCPR;
b) the first bar is the weighted SSA regional benchmark;
c) taller bars imply a more demanding target path.

```{r ssa-goals-gap-lastobs}
ssa_gap_lastobs_plot_data <- ssa_gap_lastobs_plot %>%
  mutate(
    fill_group = if_else(country == "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)", "SSA weighted", region),
    country = factor(country, levels = rev(levels(country)))
  )

ggplot(ssa_gap_lastobs_plot_data, aes(x = country, y = required_growth_pp_per_year, fill = fill_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "SSA weighted" = "#111827",
      "West Africa" = region_palette["West Africa"],
      "Central Africa" = region_palette["Central Africa"],
      "East/Southern Africa" = region_palette["East/Southern Africa"]
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA gap analysis: growth needed to reach 40% from the latest observed mCPR",
    subtitle = "Bars show annual percentage-point growth needed over the next 10 years; the first bar is the weighted SSA regional benchmark",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Required growth to reach 40% in 10 years (pp/year)"
  )
```

## SSA gap analysis using model-estimated p(2025)

This version shifts the baseline from the latest observed mCPR to the model-implied level in 2025.

Interpretation:
a) this puts all countries on the same calendar footing;
b) the first bar is again the weighted SSA benchmark;
c) the difference between this chart and the previous one shows how much the fitted model has already moved countries toward or away from the target by 2025.

```{r ssa-goals-gap-p2025}
ssa_gap_p2025_plot_data <- ssa_gap_p2025_plot %>%
  mutate(
    fill_group = if_else(country == "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)", "SSA weighted", region),
    country = factor(country, levels = rev(levels(country)))
  )

ggplot(ssa_gap_p2025_plot_data, aes(x = country, y = required_growth_pp_per_year, fill = fill_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "SSA weighted" = "#111827",
      "West Africa" = region_palette["West Africa"],
      "Central Africa" = region_palette["Central Africa"],
      "East/Southern Africa" = region_palette["East/Southern Africa"]
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA gap analysis: growth needed to reach 40% from model-estimated p(2025)",
    subtitle = "Bars show annual percentage-point growth needed over the next 10 years from the model-implied 2025 level",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Required growth to reach 40% in 10 years (pp/year)"
  )
```

## Intervention scenario: growth needed from p(2026) regardless of the fitted ceiling

This chart answers a different question. It ignores whether the fitted natural trajectory actually reaches 40% and instead asks what annual growth rate would be needed from model-estimated `p(2026)` to reach 40% by 2036.

Interpretation:
a) countries that do not naturally cross 40% are still included;
b) the first bar is the weighted SSA benchmark from `p(2026)`;
c) this is the right chart when the planning question is, “how much growth would an intervention have to generate from the current modeled level?”

```{r ssa-goals-gap-p2026-intervention}
ssa_gap_p2026_plot_data <- ssa_gap_p2026_intervention_plot %>%
  mutate(
    fill_group = case_when(
      country == "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)" ~ "SSA weighted",
      country %in% (ssa_goals_country_table %>% filter(target40_status == "Not projected to cross") %>% pull(country)) ~ "Needs intervention beyond natural trajectory",
      TRUE ~ as.character(region)
    ),
    country = factor(country, levels = rev(levels(country)))
  )

ggplot(ssa_gap_p2026_plot_data, aes(x = country, y = required_growth_pp_per_year, fill = fill_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "SSA weighted" = "#111827",
      "Needs intervention beyond natural trajectory" = "#B91C1C",
      "West Africa" = region_palette["West Africa"],
      "Central Africa" = region_palette["Central Africa"],
      "East/Southern Africa" = region_palette["East/Southern Africa"]
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Intervention scenario: growth needed from p(2026) to reach 40% by 2036",
    subtitle = "Red bars mark countries whose natural fitted trajectory does not reach 40%; the chart still reports the growth lift required if an intervention changes the path",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Required growth from p(2026) to 40% in 10 years (pp/year)"
  )
```

## Selected SSA trajectories relative to the 40% target and K ceiling

This is the anchor trajectory figure for the goals section. It shows how selected countries sit relative to the 40% target, their fitted ceiling, and their projected crossing year.

Interpretation:
a) black points are observed DHS mCPR values;
b) green lines are countries already above 40%;
c) blue lines are countries projected to cross by 2035;
d) orange lines are countries projected to cross after 2035;
e) red dashed lines indicate countries not projected to cross under the current fitted model;
f) ribbons are 95% credible intervals;
g) the dashed horizontal line is the 40% target;
h) the dotted brown line is the fitted ceiling \(K\);
i) vertical lines mark the median crossing year when a crossing is projected.

```{r ssa-goals-focus-trajectories}
ggplot(ssa_focus_projection, aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_med)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = 100 * p_lo, ymax = 100 * p_hi),
    fill = "#93C5FD",
    alpha = 0.22
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(color = trajectory_status, linetype = trajectory_status),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_focus_obs,
    aes(x = year, y = 100 * mCPR),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    size = 1.8,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#7F1D1D", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_hline(
    data = ssa_focus_meta,
    aes(yintercept = 100 * K_mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "#8A5A00",
    linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = ssa_focus_meta %>% filter(!is.na(crossing_year_plot)),
    aes(xintercept = crossing_year_plot),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "#1D4ED8",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = ssa_focus_annotations,
    aes(x = annotation_year, y = annotation_y, label = annotation_label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3,
    color = "grey15",
    segment.alpha = 0.3,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Already crossed" = "#047857",
      "Projected to cross by 2035" = "#2563EB",
      "Projected to cross after 2035" = "#D97706",
      "Not projected to cross" = "#B91C1C"
    )
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c(
      "Already crossed" = "solid",
      "Projected to cross by 2035" = "solid",
      "Projected to cross after 2035" = "solid",
      "Not projected to cross" = "dashed"
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ country, ncol = 4) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Selected SSA trajectories: retrospective and prospective paths to 40%",
    subtitle = "Panels combine observed DHS mCPR, fitted posterior median trajectories to 2045, 95% credible intervals, the 40% target, and each country's fitted K ceiling",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR (%)"
  )
```

## Asymptote question: latest observed mCPR versus fitted K

This graph focuses on countries still below 40% at their latest observed mCPR.

Interpretation:
a) black circles show the latest observed mCPR;
b) gold diamonds show the fitted ceiling \(K\);
c) gold whiskers show the 95% credible interval for \(K\);
d) grey connectors show the remaining headroom from the latest observed mCPR to \(K\);
e) the dashed red line marks the 40% target.

```{r ssa-goals-asymptote-lastobs}
ssa_asymptote_lastobs_legend <- bind_rows(
  ssa_asymptote_lastobs %>%
    transmute(country, region, y_value = 100 * last_obs_mcpr, point_type = "Latest observed mCPR"),
  ssa_asymptote_lastobs %>%
    transmute(country, region, y_value = 100 * K_mean, point_type = "Fitted K")
)

ggplot(ssa_asymptote_lastobs, aes(x = country)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#7F1D1D", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(xend = country, y = 100 * last_obs_mcpr, yend = 100 * K_mean),
    color = "grey65",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_linerange(
    aes(ymin = 100 * K_p05, ymax = 100 * K_p95, color = "Fitted K (95% CI)"),
    linewidth = 0.9,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_asymptote_lastobs_legend %>% filter(point_type == "Latest observed mCPR"),
    aes(y = y_value, fill = point_type),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.8,
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_asymptote_lastobs_legend %>% filter(point_type == "Fitted K"),
    aes(y = y_value, fill = point_type),
    shape = 23,
    size = 3,
    color = "#8A5A00",
    stroke = 0.55
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_grid(region ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Latest observed mCPR" = "#111827",
      "Fitted K" = "#F59E0B"
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Fitted K (95% CI)" = "#B45309")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA asymptote check: latest observed mCPR versus fitted K",
    subtitle = "Only countries still below 40% at the latest observed mCPR are shown; connectors highlight remaining headroom under the fitted natural trajectory",
    x = NULL,
    y = "mCPR or K (%)"
  )
```

## Asymptote question: model-estimated p(2025) versus fitted K

This version updates the current point from the latest observed mCPR to the model-implied level in 2025.

Interpretation:
a) blue circles show model-estimated `p(2025)`;
b) gold diamonds show the fitted ceiling \(K\);
c) gold whiskers show the 95% credible interval for \(K\);
d) grey connectors show the modeled remaining headroom from `p(2025)` to \(K\);
e) countries whose \(K\) remains close to or below 40% have limited modeled headroom even if their current level is still below target.

```{r ssa-goals-asymptote-p2025}
ssa_asymptote_p2025_legend <- bind_rows(
  ssa_asymptote_p2025 %>%
    transmute(country, region, y_value = 100 * p2025_mean, point_type = "Model-estimated p(2025)"),
  ssa_asymptote_p2025 %>%
    transmute(country, region, y_value = 100 * K_mean, point_type = "Fitted K")
)

ggplot(ssa_asymptote_p2025, aes(x = country)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#7F1D1D", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(xend = country, y = 100 * p2025_mean, yend = 100 * K_mean),
    color = "grey65",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_linerange(
    aes(ymin = 100 * K_p05, ymax = 100 * K_p95, color = "Fitted K (95% CI)"),
    linewidth = 0.9,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_asymptote_p2025_legend %>% filter(point_type == "Model-estimated p(2025)"),
    aes(y = y_value, fill = point_type),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.8,
    color = "#1D4ED8",
    stroke = 0.45
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_asymptote_p2025_legend %>% filter(point_type == "Fitted K"),
    aes(y = y_value, fill = point_type),
    shape = 23,
    size = 3,
    color = "#8A5A00",
    stroke = 0.55
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_grid(region ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Model-estimated p(2025)" = "#60A5FA",
      "Fitted K" = "#F59E0B"
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Fitted K (95% CI)" = "#B45309")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA asymptote check: model-estimated p(2025) versus fitted K",
    subtitle = "Only countries still below 40% at p(2025) are shown",
    x = NULL,
    y = "mCPR or K (%)"
  )
```

## SSA priority matrix: distance to 40, probability of success, and country size

This graph combines target difficulty, target feasibility, and population size in one view.

Interpretation:
a) the x-axis is the remaining gap from model-estimated `p(2025)` to 40%;
b) the y-axis is the posterior probability of reaching 40% by 2035;
c) bubble area is proportional to WPP women age 15-49 population;
d) bubble color is the fitted S-curve stage at 2026;
e) labels show whether a country already crossed, is projected to cross by 2035, is projected to cross later, or is not projected to cross.

```{r ssa-goals-priority-matrix}
ggplot(
  ssa_priority_matrix_data,
  aes(
    x = target_gap_p2025_pp,
    y = prob_reach_40_by_2035_pct,
    size = women_15_49,
    fill = s_curve_stage_2026
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "grey15", alpha = 0.88, stroke = 0.35) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = priority_label),
    size = 3,
    color = "grey15",
    segment.alpha = 0.35,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_size_area(
    max_size = 18,
    name = "Women 15-49"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Stage 1: initial" = "#D55E00",
      "Stage 2: diffusion" = "#0072B2",
      "Stage 3: plateau" = "#009E73"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA priority matrix: target gap, posterior success probability, and population size",
    subtitle = "Bubble labels show the median projected crossing year; larger bubbles correspond to larger women age 15-49 populations",
    x = "Gap from p(2025) to 40% target (percentage points)",
    y = "Posterior probability of reaching 40% by 2035 (%)"
  )
```

## Which countries move the weighted SSA regional trend the most?

This decomposition uses the weighted regional mean directly. Each country's contribution to the weighted SSA average is its mCPR multiplied by its women age 15-49 population share.

Interpretation:
a) bars show how many percentage points each country adds to the weighted SSA mean between 2026 and 2035 under business as usual;
b) larger positive bars mean that country is carrying more of the region's projected progress;
c) the same bar also measures how much the regional mean would fall short if that country stalled at its 2026 level while others followed business as usual.

```{r ssa-goals-regional-delta}
ggplot(ssa_regional_delta_plot, aes(x = country, y = delta_contribution_pp, fill = region)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.76) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = region_palette) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Country contribution to change in the weighted SSA mean, 2026 to 2035",
    subtitle = "Bars show each country's contribution to the projected increase in the weighted SSA regional mCPR under business as usual",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Change in weighted SSA mCPR contribution (percentage points)"
  )
```

## If one country reached 40% by 2035, how much would the weighted SSA mean move?

This is a counterfactual decomposition. For each country, we hold all other countries at their business-as-usual 2035 projection and set that one country to 40%.

Interpretation:
a) bars show the maximum regional lift if that country reaches 40% by 2035;
b) black points show the expected contribution after weighting that lift by the country's own posterior probability of reaching 40%;
c) large bars with low black points indicate countries that matter enormously for the regional goal but are unlikely to deliver under business as usual.

```{r ssa-goals-marginal-lift}
ggplot(ssa_regional_marginal_plot, aes(x = country, y = marginal_lift_if40_pp, fill = region)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.76, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_point(
    aes(y = expected_goal_contribution_pp),
    inherit.aes = TRUE,
    color = "black",
    size = 2
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = region_palette) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Counterfactual lift to the weighted SSA mean if one country reached 40% by 2035",
    subtitle = "Bars are the full marginal lift; black points are the expected contribution after weighting by each country's posterior probability of reaching 40%",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Lift to weighted SSA mean (percentage points)"
  )
```

## Which countries most influence the regional 2035 outcome?

This is a model-based influence diagnostic. It asks how strongly each country's posterior 2035 trajectory co-moves with the weighted SSA 2035 regional trajectory across posterior draws.

Interpretation:
a) higher values mean the country's uncertainty matters more for the regional 2035 outcome;
b) large-population countries with high correlation are the countries most able to swing the regional result;
c) this is not the same as having the fastest growth rate; it is an influence measure on the regional target outcome.

```{r ssa-goals-regional-uncertainty}
ggplot(ssa_regional_uncertainty_plot, aes(x = country, y = corr_with_regional_2035, color = region)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(xend = country, y = 0, yend = corr_with_regional_2035),
    linewidth = 0.7,
    alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(size = women_15_49_millions), alpha = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Women 15-49\n(millions)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior influence on the weighted SSA 2035 outcome",
    subtitle = "Correlation is computed between each country's 2035 posterior mCPR and the weighted SSA 2035 posterior mCPR",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Correlation with weighted SSA 2035 mCPR"
  )
```

## SSA regional influence summary table

This table brings the regional-contribution analysis into one place:
a) each country's weighted contribution to the SSA mean in 2026 and 2035;
b) the change in that contribution under business as usual;
c) the lift to the weighted SSA mean if that country reached 40% by 2035;
d) the expected goal contribution after weighting by the country's own probability of reaching 40%;
e) the posterior influence of that country on the regional 2035 outcome.

```{r ssa-goals-regional-influence-table}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_regional_influence_table %>%
    select(
      country, region, women_15_49_millions,
      contribution_2026_pp, contribution_2035_pp, delta_contribution_pp,
      marginal_lift_if40_pp, expected_goal_contribution_pp,
      prob_reach_40_by_2035, corr_with_regional_2035
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "SSA regional influence summary: weighted contribution, counterfactual lift, and posterior influence"
)
```

## SSA countries: projected year reaching 40% with uncertainty

This graph focuses on timing uncertainty.

Interpretation:
a) points are posterior mean years of reaching 40%;
b) horizontal bars are 95% credible intervals;
c) the dotted vertical line marks 2036;
d) `x` markers indicate countries not projected to cross by the fitted trajectory.

```{r ssa-goals-country-year40}
ssa_goals_year40_cap <- max(ssa_goals_country_table$projected_year_reach_40_p95, na.rm = TRUE) + 2

ssa_goals_year40_plot <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  mutate(
    year40_plot = case_when(
      target40_status == "Already crossed" ~ as.numeric(last_obs_year),
      target40_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ as.numeric(ssa_goals_year40_cap),
      TRUE ~ as.numeric(projected_year_reach_40_p50)
    ),
    year40_p05_plot = case_when(
      target40_status == "Already crossed" ~ as.numeric(last_obs_year),
      target40_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ as.numeric(projected_year_reach_40_p05)
    ),
    year40_p95_plot = case_when(
      target40_status == "Already crossed" ~ as.numeric(last_obs_year),
      target40_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ as.numeric(projected_year_reach_40_p95)
    ),
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_goals_country_table %>%
        arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), year40_plot, country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ggplot(ssa_goals_year40_plot, aes(x = country, color = region)) +
  geom_pointrange(
    data = ssa_goals_year40_plot %>% filter(target40_status != "Not projected to cross"),
    aes(y = year40_plot, ymin = year40_p05_plot, ymax = year40_p95_plot),
    linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_goals_year40_plot %>% filter(target40_status == "Not projected to cross"),
    aes(y = year40_plot),
    shape = 4,
    size = 2.8,
    stroke = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2036, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey45") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA countries: projected year reaching 40% mCPR",
    subtitle = "Points are posterior medians and horizontal bars are 95% credible intervals; x-marks indicate countries not projected to cross 40%",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Projected year reaching 40% mCPR"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank())
```

## SSA countries: is 40% in 10 years realistic?

This graph compares three benchmarks against the annual growth required to hit 40% within 10 years:
a) the latest observed interval growth;
b) the maximum observed interval growth;
d) the model-implied current annual growth rate.

The required growth on the x-axis comes from model-estimated `p(2026)` and ignores whether the fitted ceiling is below 40%. This makes the graph an intervention-feasibility benchmark rather than a natural-trajectory forecast.

Points below the 45-degree line imply the target requires faster growth than that comparison benchmark.

```{r ssa-goals-feasibility}
ggplot(
  ssa_goal_comparison_long,
  aes(
    x = required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year,
    y = comparison_growth_pp_per_year,
    color = region
  )
) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_point(size = 2.4, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~ comparison_metric, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = region_palette) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA countries: intervention growth requirement versus observed and model-implied growth",
    subtitle = "The x-axis uses the growth needed from p(2026) to reach 40% by 2036, regardless of the fitted ceiling; points below the diagonal would need faster growth than the comparison benchmark",
    x = "Required growth from p(2026) to 40% in 10 years (pp/year)",
    y = "Observed or model-implied growth (pp/year)"
  )
```

## SSA country goal summary table

This table brings the main goal-tracking quantities into one place:
a) current level relative to 40%;
b) model-estimated `p(2025)` and `p(2026)`;
c) projected year of reaching 40%;
d) required growth to hit 40% within 10 years;
e) latest observed and model-implied growth benchmarks;
f) investment phase and S-curve stage;
g) the country's population share within the modeled SSA set.

The first table below isolates the intervention scenario. It keeps countries that do not naturally cross 40% in view and reports the annual growth that would still be required from `p(2026)` if an intervention were assumed to change the path.

```{r ssa-goals-intervention-summary}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_goals_country_table %>%
    select(
      country, region, p2026_mean, required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year,
      latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year, model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year,
      target40_status, projected_year_reach_40, s_curve_stage_2026
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "SSA intervention scenario summary: growth needed from p(2026) to reach 40% by 2036"
)
```

```{r ssa-goals-country-summary}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_goals_country_table %>%
    select(
      country, region, weight_share_ssa, last_obs_year, last_obs_mcpr,
      p2025_mean, p2026_mean, target40_status, projected_year_reach_40,
      required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year, required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year,
      latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year, model_dpdt_lastobs_pp_per_year,
      prob_reach_40_by_2035, investment_phase, s_curve_stage_2026
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "SSA country goal summary relative to 40% mCPR"
)
```

## Dashboard preview: SSA static views

This section reorganizes the SSA goal-tracking analysis into a dashboard-style static preview. The point is not to introduce a new model. The point is to pull the main strategic questions into a compact sequence of figures and tables so we can see what the app-facing views should look like before building an interactive dashboard.

The preview focuses on these questions:
a) is the weighted SSA 40% goal realistic under business as usual;
b) what is the current gap to 40% at the regional and country levels;
c) what annualized growth rate would be needed to close that gap;
d) do historical and model-implied growth rates suggest that annualized growth rate is realistic;
e) where are the key outliers, structural ceiling issues, and leverage points for debate.

```{r ssa-dashboard-preview-setup}
goal_verdict <- function(prob_reach_40) {
  case_when(
    prob_reach_40 >= 0.75 ~ "Probable under BAU",
    prob_reach_40 >= 0.40 ~ "Plausible but uncertain under BAU",
    TRUE ~ "Aspirational under BAU"
  )
}

k_trafficlight_status <- function(k_lo, k_hi, target = target_mcpr_40) {
  case_when(
    k_lo >= target ~ "K above 40%",
    k_hi < target ~ "K below 40%",
    TRUE ~ "K straddles 40%"
  )
}

build_ssa_region_preview <- function(exclude_countries = character(), scenario_label = "All SSA") {
  idx_keep_rows <- which(!ssa_nd_region$country %in% exclude_countries)

  nd_keep <- ssa_nd_region[idx_keep_rows, , drop = FALSE] %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    mutate(weight_country_rebased = women_15_49 / sum(women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    ungroup()

  draws <- sapply(ssa_goal_years, function(yy) {
    idx_year <- which(nd_keep$year == yy)
    as.numeric(
      ep_ssa_region_country[, idx_keep_rows[idx_year], drop = FALSE] %*%
        nd_keep$weight_country_rebased[idx_year]
    )
  })

  if (is.vector(draws)) {
    draws <- matrix(draws, nrow = n_draws, ncol = length(ssa_goal_years))
  }

  keep_countries <- ssa_latest_obs$country[!ssa_latest_obs$country %in% exclude_countries]
  keep_cols <- match(keep_countries, countries)
  keep_weights <- ssa_weight_latest$women_15_49[match(keep_countries, ssa_weight_latest$country)]
  keep_weights <- keep_weights / sum(keep_weights, na.rm = TRUE)

  p2025_draws_keep <- as.numeric(draws[, match(2025, ssa_goal_years)])
  p2026_draws_keep <- as.numeric(p_mat[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE] %*% keep_weights)
  dpdt2026_draws_keep <- as.numeric(dpdt_mat[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE] %*% keep_weights)

  year40_draws_keep <- apply(draws, 1, function(x) {
    idx <- which(x >= target_mcpr_40)
    if (length(idx) == 0) NA_real_ else ssa_goal_years[min(idx)]
  })

  latest_keep <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
    filter(country %in% keep_countries) %>%
    mutate(weight_preview = keep_weights[match(country, keep_countries)])

  list(
    curve = tibble(
      scenario = scenario_label,
      year = ssa_goal_years,
      p_mean = colMeans(draws),
      p_p05 = apply(draws, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
      p_p95 = apply(draws, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
    ),
    summary = tibble(
      scenario = scenario_label,
      excluded_countries = if (length(exclude_countries) == 0) "None" else paste(exclude_countries, collapse = ", "),
      last_obs_weighted_mean = weighted.mean(latest_keep$last_obs_mcpr, latest_keep$weight_preview, na.rm = TRUE),
      p2025_mean = mean(p2025_draws_keep),
      p2026_mean = mean(p2026_draws_keep),
      required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year =
        100 * pmax(target_mcpr_40 - weighted.mean(latest_keep$last_obs_mcpr, latest_keep$weight_preview, na.rm = TRUE), 0) / 10,
      required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(pmax(target_mcpr_40 - p2025_draws_keep, 0) / 10),
      required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(pmax(target_mcpr_40 - p2026_draws_keep, 0) / 10),
      model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(dpdt2026_draws_keep),
      projected_year_reach_40_p50 = if (all(is.na(year40_draws_keep))) NA_real_ else median(year40_draws_keep, na.rm = TRUE),
      prob_reach_40_by_2035 = mean(!is.na(year40_draws_keep) & year40_draws_keep <= 2035)
    ) %>%
      mutate(verdict = goal_verdict(prob_reach_40_by_2035))
  )
}

ssa_dashboard_all <- build_ssa_region_preview(character(0), "All SSA")
ssa_dashboard_excl_ndrc <- build_ssa_region_preview(
  c("Nigeria", "Congo Democratic Republic"),
  "Excluding Nigeria and DRC"
)

ssa_dashboard_region_curve <- bind_rows(
  ssa_dashboard_all$curve,
  ssa_dashboard_excl_ndrc$curve
)

ssa_dashboard_region_summary <- bind_rows(
  ssa_dashboard_all$summary,
  ssa_dashboard_excl_ndrc$summary
)

ssa_dashboard_country <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  mutate(
    women_15_49_millions = women_15_49 / 1e6,
    weight_share_ssa_pct = 100 * weight_share_ssa,
    k_status = k_trafficlight_status(K_p05, K_p95),
    inflection_side_2026 = if_else(years_since_t0_2026 < 0, "Pre-inflection", "Post-inflection"),
    relative_to_ssa_p2025_pp = 100 * (p2025_mean - ssa_region_goals_summary$p2025_mean),
    feasibility_status = case_when(
      K_p95 < target_mcpr_40 ~ "Fitted K below 40%",
      required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year <= model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year ~ "Current annualized growth rate above required",
      required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year <= max_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year ~ "Needs return to historical peak",
      TRUE ~ "Needs growth above observed history"
    )
  )

ssa_dashboard_gap_compare <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    country = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
    region = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
    women_15_49_millions = sum(ssa_dashboard_country$women_15_49_millions, na.rm = TRUE),
    gap_from_lastobs_pp = 100 * pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_region_goals_summary$last_obs_weighted_mean, 0),
    gap_from_p2025_pp = 100 * pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_region_goals_summary$p2025_mean, 0)
  ),
  ssa_dashboard_country %>%
    transmute(
      country,
      region,
      women_15_49_millions,
      gap_from_lastobs_pp = pmax(target_gap_pp, 0),
      gap_from_p2025_pp = pmax(target_gap_p2025_pp, 0)
    )
) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = c(
        "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
        ssa_dashboard_country %>%
          arrange(desc(target_gap_p2025_pp), desc(target_gap_pp), country) %>%
          pull(country)
      )
    )
  )

ssa_dashboard_feasibility <- ssa_dashboard_country %>%
  mutate(
    label_flag = case_when(
      country %in% c("Nigeria", "Congo Democratic Republic") ~ TRUE,
      women_15_49 >= quantile(women_15_49, 0.85, na.rm = TRUE) ~ TRUE,
      target40_status == "Not projected to cross" ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE
    )
  )

ssa_dashboard_no_crossing <- ssa_dashboard_country %>%
  filter(target40_status == "Not projected to cross" | k_status == "K below 40%") %>%
  arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), desc(weight_share_ssa), country)

ssa_dashboard_stage2_window <- ssa_dashboard_country %>%
  filter(s_curve_stage_2026 == "Stage 2: diffusion") %>%
  mutate(
    crossing_window_flag = !is.na(projected_year_reach_40_p50) &
      projected_year_reach_40_p50 >= 2028 &
      projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2038,
    underperforming_vs_latest_obs = model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year < latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(crossing_window_flag), desc(weight_share_ssa), country)
```

### Dashboard preview 1: regional verdict under business as usual

The first dashboard panel should answer the main question directly: how likely is the weighted SSA region to reach 40% by 2035 under business as usual, and how much of that answer changes if Nigeria and DRC are temporarily removed from the weighted regional calculation.

```{r ssa-dashboard-region-verdict-plot}
ggplot(
  ssa_dashboard_region_curve %>% filter(year >= 2025),
  aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean, color = scenario, fill = scenario)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#8B0000", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2035, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 100 * p_p05, ymax = 100 * p_p95), alpha = 0.14, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.05) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "All SSA" = "#111827",
      "Excluding Nigeria and DRC" = "#1D4ED8"
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "All SSA" = "#111827",
      "Excluding Nigeria and DRC" = "#1D4ED8"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Dashboard preview: weighted SSA regional trajectory under BAU",
    subtitle = "The blue counterfactual curve shows what the weighted regional path looks like when Nigeria and DRC are excluded and remaining country weights are renormalized",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Population-weighted mCPR (%)"
  )
```

```{r ssa-dashboard-region-verdict-table}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_dashboard_region_summary %>%
    select(
      scenario, excluded_countries, last_obs_weighted_mean, p2025_mean, p2026_mean,
      required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year, model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year,
      projected_year_reach_40_p50, prob_reach_40_by_2035, verdict
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Dashboard preview: regional verdict under BAU, with and without Nigeria and DRC"
)
```

### Dashboard preview 2: current gap to 40% under two baselines

This preview compares the current gap to 40% using two baselines:
a) the latest observed DHS mCPR;
b) the model-estimated `p(2025)`.

That distinction matters because some countries have older latest surveys, while the `p(2025)` baseline places everyone on the same calendar footing.

```{r ssa-dashboard-gap-dumbbell}
ggplot(ssa_dashboard_gap_compare, aes(y = country)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = gap_from_lastobs_pp, xend = gap_from_p2025_pp, yend = country),
    color = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = gap_from_lastobs_pp, fill = "Gap from last observed", size = women_15_49_millions),
    shape = 21,
    color = "#111827",
    stroke = 0.35
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = gap_from_p2025_pp, fill = "Gap from model-estimated p(2025)", size = women_15_49_millions),
    shape = 21,
    color = "#1D4ED8",
    stroke = 0.35
  ) +
  scale_size_area(
    max_size = 10,
    name = "Women 15-49\n(millions)"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Gap from last observed" = "#111827",
      "Gap from model-estimated p(2025)" = "#60A5FA"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Dashboard preview: SSA gap to 40% under two baselines",
    subtitle = "The first row is the weighted SSA benchmark; grey connectors show how much the gap changes when the baseline shifts from the latest observed DHS mCPR to model-estimated p(2025)",
    x = "Gap to 40% target (percentage points)",
    y = NULL
  )
```

### Dashboard preview 3: is the required annualized growth rate realistic?

This preview compares the annual growth required from `p(2025)` to hit 40% by 2035 against the model-implied current annual growth rate at 2025.

Interpretation:
a) the 45-degree line separates countries already growing fast enough from countries needing acceleration;
b) fill color captures the feasibility framing;
c) point shape shows current stage in the contraceptive transition;
d) Nigeria and DRC are always labeled.

```{r ssa-dashboard-feasibility-scatter}
ggplot(
  ssa_dashboard_feasibility,
  aes(
    x = required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    y = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
    size = women_15_49_millions,
    fill = feasibility_status,
    shape = s_curve_stage_2026
  )
) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_point(color = "grey15", alpha = 0.88, stroke = 0.35) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = ssa_dashboard_feasibility %>% filter(label_flag),
    aes(label = country),
    size = 3,
    color = "grey15",
    segment.alpha = 0.35,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 16, name = "Women 15-49\n(millions)") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Current annualized growth rate above required" = "#0F766E",
      "Needs return to historical peak" = "#F59E0B",
      "Needs growth above observed history" = "#B91C1C",
      "Fitted K below 40%" = "#7F1D1D"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Dashboard preview: required annualized growth rate versus model-implied current annualized growth rate",
    subtitle = "Points above the 45-degree line are outperforming the annualized growth rate needed to reach 40% by 2035; points below the line need acceleration",
    x = "Required growth from p(2025) to 40% by 2035 (pp/year)",
    y = "Model-implied annual growth at 2025 (pp/year)"
  )
```

```{r ssa-dashboard-stage2-window-table}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_dashboard_stage2_window %>%
    select(
      country, region, weight_share_ssa, projected_year_reach_40_p50,
      prob_reach_40_by_2035, latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
      model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year, required_growth_from_p2026_pp_per_year,
      crossing_window_flag, underperforming_vs_latest_obs
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Dashboard preview: Stage 2 countries, crossing-window potential, and diagnostic flags"
)
```

### Dashboard preview 4: structural ceiling, stage, and no-crossing countries

This panel focuses on where the fitted ceiling sits relative to the 40% target.

Interpretation:
a) green countries have a fitted `K` fully above 40%;
b) amber countries have a `K` interval that straddles 40%;
c) red countries have a fitted `K` fully below 40%;
d) shape distinguishes countries that are pre-inflection from those already post-inflection at 2026.

```{r ssa-dashboard-k-trafficlight}
ssa_dashboard_k_plot <- ssa_dashboard_country %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(
      country,
      levels = ssa_dashboard_country %>%
        arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), K_mean, country) %>%
        pull(country)
    )
  )

ggplot(ssa_dashboard_k_plot, aes(x = country, y = 100 * K_mean)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40, linetype = "dashed", color = "#7F1D1D", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_linerange(
    aes(ymin = 100 * K_p05, ymax = 100 * K_p95, color = k_status),
    linewidth = 0.9,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = k_status, shape = inflection_side_2026, size = women_15_49_millions),
    color = "grey15",
    stroke = 0.35
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_grid(region ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "K above 40%" = "#059669",
      "K straddles 40%" = "#F59E0B",
      "K below 40%" = "#B91C1C"
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "K above 40%" = "#047857",
      "K straddles 40%" = "#D97706",
      "K below 40%" = "#991B1B"
    )
  ) +
  scale_size_area(
    max_size = 8,
    name = "Women 15-49\n(millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Dashboard preview: fitted K relative to the 40% target",
    subtitle = "Red countries are below 40% on the fitted ceiling itself; these should be framed as trajectory-implied ceiling issues rather than program failure",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Fitted K (%)"
  )
```

```{r ssa-dashboard-no-crossing-table}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_dashboard_no_crossing %>%
    select(
      country, region, women_15_49_millions, weight_share_ssa, p2025_mean, p2026_mean,
      K_mean, K_p05, K_p95, years_since_t0_2026, s_curve_stage_2026,
      prob_reach_40_by_2035, target40_status
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Dashboard preview: countries with no projected crossing or K below 40%"
)
```

### Dashboard preview 5: country heterogeneity relative to the SSA trajectory

This preview highlights outliers and heterogeneity directly.

Interpretation:
a) the x-axis compares each country's model-estimated `p(2025)` against the weighted SSA `p(2025)`;
b) points to the right are above the weighted SSA trajectory and points to the left are below it;
c) the y-axis is the posterior probability of reaching 40% by 2035;
d) larger points matter more for the regional conversation because of population size.

```{r ssa-dashboard-heterogeneity}
ggplot(
  ssa_dashboard_country,
  aes(
    x = relative_to_ssa_p2025_pp,
    y = 100 * prob_reach_40_by_2035,
    size = women_15_49_millions,
    fill = s_curve_stage_2026
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "grey15", alpha = 0.88, stroke = 0.35) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = ssa_dashboard_country %>%
      filter(
        country %in% c("Nigeria", "Congo Democratic Republic") |
          abs(relative_to_ssa_p2025_pp) >= quantile(abs(relative_to_ssa_p2025_pp), 0.85, na.rm = TRUE)
      ),
    aes(label = country),
    size = 3,
    color = "grey15",
    segment.alpha = 0.35,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 16, name = "Women 15-49\n(millions)") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Stage 1: initial" = "#D55E00",
      "Stage 2: diffusion" = "#0072B2",
      "Stage 3: plateau" = "#009E73"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Dashboard preview: country heterogeneity relative to weighted SSA p(2025)",
    subtitle = "This view is useful for flagging overperformers, underperformers, and high-population countries that sit below the regional path",
    x = "Difference from weighted SSA p(2025) (percentage points)",
    y = "Posterior probability of reaching 40% by 2035 (%)"
  )
```

### Dashboard preview note on equity

The dashboard should keep an equity perspective visible, but the current model has an important boundary: it is a country-level trajectory model, not a within-country subgroup model.

That means the current static preview can support:
a) keeping `Maintain / equity` visible as a meaningful planning category;
b) distinguishing countries near saturation from countries still in rapid diffusion;
c) showing where historical trajectories imply ceilings below 40%, without treating that as program failure.

What it cannot yet show directly is within-country inequality by wealth, geography, age, or other subgroups. That would require an explicit subgroup extension to the analysis rather than a relabeling of current country-level outputs.

## Goals - final

This section pulls the SSA goal work into one final set of static views for decision-making.

The logic is:
a) define the SSA "current level" two ways using population weights;
b) convert each gap to 40% into a required annualized growth rate over 10 years;
c) compare that required annualized growth rate against observed survey-based growth, current model-implied growth, and average model-projected growth over 2025 to 2035;
d) show the weighted SSA regional trajectory and every country trajectory in one place.

For the weighted SSA region, the "latest observed" baseline is the population-weighted average across each country's latest observed DHS mCPR, not a single same-year regional survey point.

```{r ssa-goals-final-setup}
# Final SSA goals block: combine the weighted regional benchmark and all SSA
# countries in one table so the same quantities drive all final charts.
ssa_forward_growth_country <- tibble(
  country = ssa_country_order_vec,
  projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year =
    100 * colMeans((ssa_p2035_draws - ssa_p2025_draws) / 10)
)

ssa_region_K_draws <- as.numeric(ssa_K_draws %*% ssa_weight_vec_latest)
ssa_region_p2035_draws_final <- as.numeric(ssa_p2035_draws %*% ssa_weight_vec_latest)
ssa_region_forward_growth_2025_2035_draws <-
  (ssa_region_p2035_draws_final - ssa_region_p2025_draws) / 10

ssa_region_k_horizon_years <- seq(
  min(mcpr_logistic_data$year[mcpr_logistic_data$region %in% ssa_regions], na.rm = TRUE),
  2100,
  by = 1
)

ssa_region_draws_k_horizon <- sapply(ssa_region_k_horizon_years, function(yy) {
  region_country_draws <- logistic_draws_at_year(ssa_K_draws, ssa_t0_draws, ssa_s_draws, yy)
  as.numeric(region_country_draws %*% ssa_weight_vec_latest)
})

ssa_region_year_reach_99pctK_draws <- apply(
  ssa_region_draws_k_horizon >=
    matrix(
      0.99 * ssa_region_K_draws,
      nrow = nrow(ssa_region_draws_k_horizon),
      ncol = ncol(ssa_region_draws_k_horizon)
    ),
  1,
  function(hit_vec) {
    idx <- which(hit_vec)
    if (length(idx) == 0) NA_real_ else ssa_region_k_horizon_years[min(idx)]
  }
)

ssa_region_goal_final_status <- case_when(
  ssa_region_goals_summary$last_obs_weighted_mean >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Already crossed",
  is.na(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50) ~ "Not projected to cross",
  ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2035 ~ "Projected to cross by 2035",
  TRUE ~ "Projected to cross after 2035"
)

ssa_region_goal_final_label <- case_when(
  ssa_region_goals_summary$last_obs_weighted_mean >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Already crossed",
  is.na(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50) ~ "Not projected to cross",
  ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2035 ~ paste0(
    "Projected by 2035 (~",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50),
    ")"
  ),
  TRUE ~ paste0(
    "Projected after 2035 (~",
    round(ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50)
    ,")"
  )
)

ssa_goal_final_country_rows <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_forward_growth_country,
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    time_to_k_table %>%
      transmute(country, year_reach_99pctK_mean),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    geography = country,
    geography_type = "Country",
    women_15_49_millions = women_15_49 / 1e6,
    current_latest_obs_pct = 100 * last_obs_mcpr,
    current_p2025_pct = 100 * p2025_mean,
    gap_from_lastobs_pp = pmax(target_gap_pp, 0),
    gap_from_p2025_pp = pmax(target_gap_p2025_pp, 0),
    survey_recency_group = case_when(
      last_obs_year >= 2015 ~ "Latest survey 2015 or later",
      TRUE ~ "Latest survey before 2015"
    ),
    trajectory_status_final = case_when(
      last_obs_mcpr >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Already crossed",
      is.na(projected_year_reach_40_p50) ~ "Not projected to cross",
      projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2035 ~ "Projected to cross by 2035",
      TRUE ~ "Projected to cross after 2035"
    ),
    crossing_label_final = case_when(
      last_obs_mcpr >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Already crossed",
      is.na(projected_year_reach_40_p50) ~ "Not projected to cross",
      projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2035 ~ paste0(
        "Projected by 2035 (~",
        round(projected_year_reach_40_p50),
        ")"
      ),
      TRUE ~ paste0("Projected after 2035 (~", round(projected_year_reach_40_p50), ")")
    ),
    geography_label = paste0(
      geography,
      " (",
      format(round(women_15_49_millions, 1), nsmall = 1),
      "m)"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    geography, geography_type, geography_label, region,
    women_15_49, women_15_49_millions, weight_share_ssa,
    last_obs_year, survey_recency_group,
    current_latest_obs_pct, current_p2025_pct,
    gap_from_lastobs_pp, gap_from_p2025_pp,
    required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year,
    required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
    model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year,
    projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year,
    K_mean, K_p05, K_p95, year_reach_99pctK_mean,
    projected_year_reach_40_p50, prob_reach_40_by_2035,
    trajectory_status_final, crossing_label_final
  )

ssa_goal_final_region_row <- tibble(
  geography = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
  geography_type = "Region",
  geography_label = paste0(
    "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted) (",
    format(round(sum(ssa_weight_latest$women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6, 1), nsmall = 1),
    "m)"
  ),
  region = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
  women_15_49 = sum(ssa_weight_latest$women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE),
  women_15_49_millions = sum(ssa_weight_latest$women_15_49, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6,
  weight_share_ssa = 1,
  last_obs_year = NA_real_,
  survey_recency_group = "Population-weighted region",
  current_latest_obs_pct = 100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$last_obs_weighted_mean,
  current_p2025_pct = 100 * ssa_region_goals_summary$p2025_mean,
  gap_from_lastobs_pp = 100 * pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_region_goals_summary$last_obs_weighted_mean, 0),
  gap_from_p2025_pp = 100 * pmax(target_mcpr_40 - ssa_region_goals_summary$p2025_mean, 0),
  required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year =
    ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year,
  required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year =
    ssa_region_goals_summary$required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
  latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year =
    ssa_region_goals_summary$observed_latest_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
  model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year =
    ssa_region_goals_summary$model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
  model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year =
    ssa_region_goals_summary$model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year,
  projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year =
    100 * mean(ssa_region_forward_growth_2025_2035_draws),
  K_mean = mean(ssa_region_K_draws),
  K_p05 = quantile(ssa_region_K_draws, ci_lower),
  K_p95 = quantile(ssa_region_K_draws, ci_upper),
  year_reach_99pctK_mean =
    if (all(is.na(ssa_region_year_reach_99pctK_draws))) {
      NA_real_
    } else {
      mean(ssa_region_year_reach_99pctK_draws, na.rm = TRUE)
    },
  projected_year_reach_40_p50 = ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50,
  prob_reach_40_by_2035 = ssa_region_goals_summary$prob_reach_40_by_2035,
  trajectory_status_final = ssa_region_goal_final_status,
  crossing_label_final = ssa_region_goal_final_label
)

ssa_goal_final_all <- bind_rows(
  ssa_goal_final_region_row,
  ssa_goal_final_country_rows
)

ssa_goal_final_current_summary <- tibble(
  measure = c(
    "Population-weighted latest observed mCPR across each country's last survey year",
    "Population-weighted model-estimated mCPR in 2025"
  ),
  mcpr_pct = c(
    ssa_goal_final_region_row$current_latest_obs_pct,
    ssa_goal_final_region_row$current_p2025_pct
  ),
  gap_to_40_pp = c(
    ssa_goal_final_region_row$gap_from_lastobs_pp,
    ssa_goal_final_region_row$gap_from_p2025_pp
  ),
  required_growth_pp_per_year = c(
    ssa_goal_final_region_row$required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year,
    ssa_goal_final_region_row$required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year
  )
)

ssa_goal_final_region_label <- ssa_goal_final_region_row$geography_label

ssa_goal_final_gap_lastobs <- ssa_goal_final_all %>%
  mutate(
    geography_label_plot = factor(
      geography_label,
      levels = rev(c(
        ssa_goal_final_region_label,
        ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
          arrange(desc(gap_from_lastobs_pp), geography) %>%
          pull(geography_label)
      )),
      ordered = TRUE
    ),
    growth_label = paste0(
      format(round(required_growth_from_lastobs_pp_per_year, 2), nsmall = 2),
      " pp/yr"
    )
  )

ssa_goal_final_gap_p2025 <- ssa_goal_final_all %>%
  mutate(
    geography_label_plot = factor(
      geography_label,
      levels = rev(c(
        ssa_goal_final_region_label,
        ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
          arrange(desc(gap_from_p2025_pp), geography) %>%
          pull(geography_label)
      )),
      ordered = TRUE
    ),
    growth_label = paste0(
      format(round(required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year, 2), nsmall = 2),
      " pp/yr"
    )
  )

ssa_goal_final_pace_long <- ssa_goal_final_all %>%
  select(
    geography, geography_label, region, women_15_49_millions,
    required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
    model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
    projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year,
      model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
      projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year
    ),
    names_to = "growth_metric",
    values_to = "growth_pp_per_year"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    growth_metric = factor(
      growth_metric,
      levels = c(
        "required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year",
        "latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year",
        "model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year",
        "projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Required to hit 40% from p(2025)",
        "Observed annualized growth from latest survey interval",
        "Model-implied annual growth at 2025",
        "Average model-projected growth, 2025-2035"
      )
    ),
    geography_label_plot = factor(
      geography_label,
      levels = levels(ssa_goal_final_gap_p2025$geography_label_plot),
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  )

ssa_goal_final_pace_range <- ssa_goal_final_pace_long %>%
  group_by(geography_label_plot) %>%
  summarise(
    x_min = min(growth_pp_per_year, na.rm = TRUE),
    x_max = max(growth_pp_per_year, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ssa_goal_final_feasibility <- ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
  mutate(
    pace_gap_pp_per_year = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year - required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    pace_status = case_when(
      K_p95 < target_mcpr_40 ~ "K below 40%",
      model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year >= required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year ~ "Current annualized growth rate above required",
      K_p05 >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Below required annualized growth rate, K above 40%",
      TRUE ~ "Below required annualized growth rate, K spans 40%"
    ),
    crossed_status = case_when(
      current_latest_obs_pct >= 40 ~ "Already at or above 40%",
      TRUE ~ "Below 40% at latest observed"
    ),
    label_flag = case_when(
      geography %in% c("Nigeria", "Congo Democratic Republic") ~ TRUE,
      women_15_49_millions >= quantile(women_15_49_millions, 0.85, na.rm = TRUE) ~ TRUE,
      abs(pace_gap_pp_per_year) >= quantile(abs(pace_gap_pp_per_year), 0.85, na.rm = TRUE) ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE
    )
  )

ssa_goal_final_region_feasibility <- ssa_goal_final_region_row %>%
  mutate(
    pace_gap_pp_per_year = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year - required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    pace_status = case_when(
      K_p95 < target_mcpr_40 ~ "K below 40%",
      model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year >= required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year ~ "Current annualized growth rate above required",
      K_p05 >= target_mcpr_40 ~ "Below required annualized growth rate, K above 40%",
      TRUE ~ "Below required annualized growth rate, K spans 40%"
    ),
    crossed_status = if_else(current_latest_obs_pct >= 40, "Already at or above 40%", "Below 40% at latest observed"),
    label_flag = TRUE
  )

ssa_goal_final_feasibility_cols <- c(
  "Current annualized growth rate above required" = "#0F766E",
  "Below required annualized growth rate, K above 40%" = "#2563EB",
  "Below required annualized growth rate, K spans 40%" = "#F59E0B",
  "K below 40%" = "#B91C1C"
)

ssa_goal_final_first_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarise(first_obs_year = min(year, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ssa_goal_final_panel_meta <- bind_rows(
  ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
    left_join(ssa_goal_final_first_obs, by = c("geography" = "country")) %>%
    mutate(
      plot_end_year = pmin(
        pmax(2035, 5 * ceiling(year_reach_99pctK_mean / 5)),
        2095
      ),
      annotation_x = first_obs_year - 1.4,
      annotation_y = pmin(96, pmax(52, 100 * K_mean - 4))
    ),
  ssa_goal_final_region_row %>%
    mutate(
      first_obs_year = min(ssa_observed_weighted$year, na.rm = TRUE),
      plot_end_year = pmin(
        pmax(2035, 5 * ceiling(year_reach_99pctK_mean / 5)),
        2095
      ),
      annotation_x = first_obs_year - 1.4,
      annotation_y = pmin(96, pmax(52, 100 * K_mean - 4))
    )
) %>%
  mutate(
    panel_label = geography_label,
    panel_label = factor(
      panel_label,
      levels = c(
        ssa_goal_final_region_label,
        ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
          arrange(factor(region, levels = ssa_regions), desc(women_15_49_millions), geography) %>%
          pull(geography_label)
      ),
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  )

ssa_goal_final_traj_country <- bind_rows(
  lapply(
    ssa_goal_final_country_rows$geography,
    function(ctry) {
      meta_i <- ssa_goal_final_panel_meta %>%
        filter(geography == ctry) %>%
        slice(1)
      j <- match(ctry, ssa_country_order_vec)
      years_i <- seq(meta_i$first_obs_year - 2, meta_i$plot_end_year, by = 1)
      p_draws_i <- sapply(years_i, function(yy) {
        ssa_K_draws[, j] / (1 + exp(-(((yy - 2000) - ssa_t0_draws[, j]) / ssa_s_draws[, j])))
      })
      tibble(
        geography = ctry,
        panel_label = meta_i$panel_label,
        year = years_i,
        p_mean = colMeans(p_draws_i),
        p_p05 = apply(p_draws_i, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
        p_p95 = apply(p_draws_i, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
      )
    }
  )
)

ssa_goal_final_traj_region <- {
  meta_i <- ssa_goal_final_panel_meta %>%
    filter(geography_type == "Region") %>%
    slice(1)
  years_i <- seq(meta_i$first_obs_year - 2, meta_i$plot_end_year, by = 1)
  p_draws_i <- sapply(years_i, function(yy) {
    region_country_draws <- logistic_draws_at_year(ssa_K_draws, ssa_t0_draws, ssa_s_draws, yy)
    as.numeric(region_country_draws %*% ssa_weight_vec_latest)
  })
  tibble(
    geography = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
    panel_label = meta_i$panel_label,
    year = years_i,
    p_mean = colMeans(p_draws_i),
    p_p05 = apply(p_draws_i, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
    p_p95 = apply(p_draws_i, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
  )
}

ssa_goal_final_traj_plot <- bind_rows(
  ssa_goal_final_traj_region,
  ssa_goal_final_traj_country
) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_goal_final_panel_meta %>%
      select(panel_label, trajectory_status_final),
    by = "panel_label"
  )

ssa_goal_final_obs_plot <- bind_rows(
  ssa_observed_weighted %>%
    transmute(
      geography = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
      year,
      mcp_pct = 100 * observed_weighted_mcpr
    ),
  mcpr_logistic_data %>%
    filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
    transmute(
      geography = country,
      year,
      mcp_pct = 100 * mCPR
    )
) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_goal_final_panel_meta %>%
      select(geography, panel_label),
    by = "geography"
  )

ssa_goal_final_status_cols <- c(
  "Already crossed" = "#047857",
  "Projected to cross by 2035" = "#2563EB",
  "Projected to cross after 2035" = "#D97706",
  "Not projected to cross" = "#B91C1C"
)

ssa_goal_final_already_crossed <- ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
  filter(current_latest_obs_pct >= 40) %>%
  arrange(projected_year_reach_40_p50, geography)

ssa_goal_final_already_colors <- setNames(
  grDevices::hcl.colors(
    max(nrow(ssa_goal_final_already_crossed), 1),
    palette = "Dark 3"
  )[seq_len(max(nrow(ssa_goal_final_already_crossed), 1))],
  if (nrow(ssa_goal_final_already_crossed) > 0) {
    ssa_goal_final_already_crossed$geography
  } else {
    "placeholder"
  }
)
if (nrow(ssa_goal_final_already_crossed) == 0) {
  ssa_goal_final_already_colors <- c()
}

ssa_goal_final_already_traj <- ssa_goal_final_traj_country %>%
  filter(
    geography %in% ssa_goal_final_already_crossed$geography,
    year <= 2045
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_goal_final_already_crossed %>%
      select(geography, geography_label),
    by = "geography"
  )

ssa_goal_final_already_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(
    region %in% ssa_regions,
    country %in% ssa_goal_final_already_crossed$geography
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    geography = country,
    year,
    mcp_pct = 100 * mCPR
  )

ssa_goal_final_already_cross_points <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(
    region %in% ssa_regions,
    country %in% ssa_goal_final_already_crossed$geography,
    mCPR >= target_mcpr_40
  ) %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  slice_min(year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  transmute(
    geography = country,
    year,
    mcp_pct = 100 * mCPR,
    cross_label = paste0(country, " crossed ", year)
  )

ssa_goal_final_notyet <- ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
  filter(current_latest_obs_pct < 40) %>%
  mutate(
    projection_label = case_when(
```
