```{r ssa-goals-final-setup}
      trajectory_status_final == "Not projected to cross" ~ paste0(geography, " (Not projected to cross)"),
      trajectory_status_final == "Projected to cross by 2035" ~ paste0(
        geography,
        " (by 2035: ~",
        round(projected_year_reach_40_p50),
        ")"
      ),
      TRUE ~ paste0(
        geography,
        " (after 2035: ~",
        round(projected_year_reach_40_p50),
        ")"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(
      trajectory_status_final,
      levels = c("Projected to cross by 2035", "Projected to cross after 2035", "Not projected to cross")
    ),
    projected_year_reach_40_p50,
    geography
  )

ssa_goal_final_notyet_traj <- ssa_goal_final_traj_country %>%
  filter(
    geography %in% ssa_goal_final_notyet$geography,
    year >= 2025,
    year <= 2060
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_goal_final_notyet %>%
      select(
        geography, geography_label, trajectory_status_final,
        projected_year_reach_40_p50, projection_label
      ),
    by = "geography"
  )

ssa_goal_final_notyet_label_points <- ssa_goal_final_notyet_traj %>%
  group_by(
    geography, geography_label, trajectory_status_final,
    projected_year_reach_40_p50, projection_label
  ) %>%
  slice_max(year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ssa_goal_final_notyet_cross_points <- ssa_goal_final_notyet %>%
  filter(!is.na(projected_year_reach_40_p50), projected_year_reach_40_p50 <= 2060) %>%
  transmute(
    geography,
    trajectory_status_final,
    year = projected_year_reach_40_p50,
    mcp_pct = 40
  )

ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025 <- ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
  filter(current_p2025_pct < 40) %>%
  mutate(
    K_mean_pct = 100 * K_mean,
    K_p05_pct = 100 * K_p05,
    K_p95_pct = 100 * K_p95
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(K_mean_pct), desc(current_p2025_pct), geography) %>%
  mutate(
    geography_plot = factor(
      geography,
      levels = rev(geography),
      ordered = TRUE
    ),
    k_label = paste0(format(round(K_mean_pct, 1), nsmall = 1), "%")
  )

ssa_goal_final_asymptote_right_x <- max(
  c(
    ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025$K_p95_pct,
    ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025$current_p2025_pct
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
) + 6

ssa_goal_final_asymptote_label_header <- tibble(
  geography_plot = factor(
    levels(ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025$geography_plot)[length(levels(ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025$geography_plot))],
    levels = levels(ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025$geography_plot),
    ordered = TRUE
  ),
  x = ssa_goal_final_asymptote_right_x,
  label = "Fitted K"
)
```

### Current weighted SSA level

These two rows define the regional "current level" that anchors the rest of the section.

```{r ssa-goals-final-current-summary}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_goal_final_current_summary %>%
    transmute(
      Baseline = measure,
      `Current mCPR (%)` = round(mcpr_pct, 2),
      `Gap to 40% (pp)` = round(gap_to_40_pp, 2),
      `Required annualized growth rate (pp/year)` = round(required_growth_pp_per_year, 2)
    ),
  caption = "SSA weighted current mCPR defined two ways for the 40% goal discussion"
)
```

`Current mCPR (%)` is the population-weighted SSA regional level under each baseline, `Gap to 40% (pp)` is the remaining distance to the 40% goal in percentage points, and `Required annualized growth rate (pp/year)` is the annual percentage-point increase needed to close that gap by 2035.

### Gap to 40% from the latest observed baseline

Bars show the gap to 40% in percentage points. The label at the end of each bar is the annualized growth rate that would be required to close that gap in 10 years.

```{r ssa-goals-final-gap-lastobs, fig.height=11}
ggplot(
  ssa_goal_final_gap_lastobs,
  aes(x = gap_from_lastobs_pp, y = geography_label_plot, fill = survey_recency_group)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = gap_from_lastobs_pp + 0.35,
      label = growth_label
    ),
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Population-weighted region" = "#111827",
      "Latest survey 2015 or later" = "#2563EB",
      "Latest survey before 2015" = "#F59E0B"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: gap to 40% from the latest observed baseline",
    subtitle = "The SSA row is the population-weighted regional benchmark; bar-end labels show the annualized growth rate required to close the gap in 10 years",
    x = "Gap to 40% target (percentage points)",
    y = NULL
  )
```

### Gap to 40% from the model-estimated 2025 baseline

This version puts every country and the weighted SSA region on the same calendar footing by starting from `p(2025)`.

```{r ssa-goals-final-gap-p2025, fig.height=11}
ggplot(
  ssa_goal_final_gap_p2025,
  aes(x = gap_from_p2025_pp, y = geography_label_plot, fill = survey_recency_group)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = gap_from_p2025_pp + 0.35,
      label = growth_label
    ),
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Population-weighted region" = "#111827",
      "Latest survey 2015 or later" = "#2563EB",
      "Latest survey before 2015" = "#F59E0B"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: gap to 40% from model-estimated p(2025)",
    subtitle = "This is the harmonized baseline for comparing required annualized growth rates across the weighted SSA region and all countries",
    x = "Gap to 40% target (percentage points)",
    y = NULL
  )
```

### Growth-rate benchmark tables

These tables put the three growth quantities used in the goals discussion in one place.

Interpretation:
a) `Observed annualized growth from survey data` uses the latest observed DHS interval for each country; for the weighted SSA region it is the population-weighted mean of each country's latest observed interval;
b) `Model instantaneous annualized growth in 2025` is the first derivative of the fitted trajectory at 2025;
c) `Model-projected average annualized growth, 2025-2035` is the average annualized change implied by the fitted model between `p(2025)` and `p(2035)`;
d) the detailed model table below shows the year-by-year model-implied instantaneous annual growth rate through 2025 and its 95% credible interval;
e) a value of `0.5` means `0.5 percentage points per year`, not `50%` growth.

```{r ssa-goals-final-growth-tables-setup}
ssa_goal_final_obs_interval_lookup <- ssa_obs_growth_intervals %>%
  group_by(country, region) %>%
  slice_max(year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  transmute(
    geography = country,
    region,
    observed_interval_label = paste0(prev_year, "-", year)
  )

ssa_goal_final_growth_benchmark_region <- tibble(
  Geography = ssa_goal_final_region_label,
  `Observed Survey Interval / Basis` = "Population-weighted mean of each country's latest observed DHS interval",
  `Observed Annualized Growth From Survey Data (pp/year)` =
    round(ssa_goal_final_region_row$latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year, 2),
  `Model Instantaneous Annualized Growth In 2025 (pp/year)` =
    round(ssa_goal_final_region_row$model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year, 2),
  `Model-Projected Average Annualized Growth, 2025-2035 (pp/year)` =
    round(ssa_goal_final_region_row$projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year, 2)
)

ssa_goal_final_growth_benchmark_country <- ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_goal_final_obs_interval_lookup,
    by = c("geography", "region")
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(region, levels = ssa_regions),
    desc(women_15_49_millions),
    geography
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Geography = geography_label,
    Region = region,
    `Observed Survey Interval` = observed_interval_label,
    `Observed Annualized Growth From Survey Data (pp/year)` =
      round(latest_obs_interval_growth_pp_per_year, 2),
    `Model Instantaneous Annualized Growth In 2025 (pp/year)` =
      round(model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year, 2),
    `Model-Projected Average Annualized Growth, 2025-2035 (pp/year)` =
      round(projected_growth_2025_2035_pp_per_year, 2)
  )

ssa_goal_final_survey_to_survey_growth <- ssa_obs_growth_intervals %>%
  arrange(country, prev_year, year) %>%
  transmute(
    Country = country,
    `Survey Interval` = paste0(prev_year, "-", year),
    `Interval Length (years)` = year - prev_year,
    `Start mCPR (%)` = round(100 * prev_mcpr, 2),
    `End mCPR (%)` = round(100 * mCPR, 2),
    `Observed Annualized Growth (pp/year)` = round(100 * obs_growth_annual, 2)
  )

ssa_goal_final_region_model_growth_history <- bind_rows(
  lapply(ssa_goal_years[ssa_goal_years <= 2025], function(yy) {
    weight_vec_yy <- ssa_nd_region %>%
      filter(year == yy) %>%
      distinct(country, weight_country) %>%
      mutate(country = factor(country, levels = ssa_country_order_vec)) %>%
      arrange(country) %>%
      pull(weight_country)

    if (length(weight_vec_yy) != length(ssa_country_order_vec) || any(is.na(weight_vec_yy))) {
      stop("Missing SSA regional growth weights at year ", yy)
    }

    z_yy <- exp(-(((yy - 2000) - ssa_t0_draws) / ssa_s_draws))
    dpdt_yy <- (ssa_K_draws / ssa_s_draws) * z_yy / (1 + z_yy)^2
    region_dpdt_draws_yy <- as.numeric(dpdt_yy %*% weight_vec_yy)

    tibble(
      geography = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
      geography_label = ssa_goal_final_region_label,
      region = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
      sort_population = ssa_goal_final_region_row$women_15_49_millions,
      year = yy,
      period = "Population-weighted regional model year",
      model_growth_pp_per_year = 100 * mean(region_dpdt_draws_yy),
      model_growth_p05 = 100 * quantile(region_dpdt_draws_yy, ci_lower),
      model_growth_p95 = 100 * quantile(region_dpdt_draws_yy, ci_upper)
    )
  })
)

ssa_goal_final_country_model_growth_history <- country_growth_rate_table %>%
  filter(country %in% ssa_country_order_vec, year <= 2025) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_goal_final_country_rows %>%
      select(geography, geography_label, last_obs_year, women_15_49_millions),
    by = c("country" = "geography")
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    geography = country,
    geography_label,
    region,
    sort_population = women_15_49_millions,
    year,
    period = if_else(
      year <= last_obs_year,
      "Within observed DHS window",
      "Model-only year before 2025"
    ),
    model_growth_pp_per_year = 100 * dpdt_mean,
    model_growth_p05 = 100 * dpdt_p05,
    model_growth_p95 = 100 * dpdt_p95
  )

ssa_goal_final_model_growth_history <- bind_rows(
  ssa_goal_final_region_model_growth_history,
  ssa_goal_final_country_model_growth_history
) %>%
  arrange(
    desc(geography == "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)"),
    factor(region, levels = c("Sub-Saharan Africa", ssa_regions)),
    desc(sort_population),
    geography,
    desc(year)
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Geography = geography_label,
    Region = region,
    Year = year,
    Period = period,
    `Model Instantaneous Annualized Growth (pp/year)` = round(model_growth_pp_per_year, 2),
    `95% CrI (pp/year)` = paste0(
      format(round(model_growth_p05, 2), nsmall = 2),
      " to ",
      format(round(model_growth_p95, 2), nsmall = 2)
    )
  )
```

```{r ssa-goals-final-growth-region-table}
knitr::kable(
  ssa_goal_final_growth_benchmark_region,
  caption = "Weighted SSA region: observed, current model-implied, and projected annualized growth benchmarks"
)
```

```{r ssa-goals-final-growth-country-table}
rmarkdown::paged_table(
  ssa_goal_final_growth_benchmark_country
)
```

```{r ssa-goals-final-survey-to-survey-growth-table}
rmarkdown::paged_table(
  ssa_goal_final_survey_to_survey_growth
)
```

```{r ssa-goals-final-model-growth-history-table}
rmarkdown::paged_table(
  ssa_goal_final_model_growth_history
)
```

### Is the required annualized growth rate realistic?

This comparison keeps the required annualized growth rate on a common 2025 to 2035 footing and places it beside three benchmarks:
a) the latest observed annualized inter-survey growth;
b) the model-implied annual growth at 2025;
c) the average model-projected annual growth from 2025 to 2035.

### p(2025) versus fitted K

This asymptote-style check keeps the focus on current modeled position versus the fitted ceiling.

Interpretation:
a) the solid dot is each country's model-estimated `p(2025)`;
b) the diamond is the fitted ceiling `K`;
c) the gold error bar is the 95% credible interval for `K`;
d) countries are ordered from highest fitted `K` to lowest fitted `K`;
e) the right-side labels show fitted `K`;
f) the vertical reference line is the 40% target;
g) countries whose fitted `K` sits near or below 40% have limited modeled headroom even if `p(2025)` is still below target.

```{r ssa-goals-final-asymptote-p2025, fig.height=9}
ggplot(ssa_goal_final_asymptote_p2025, aes(y = geography_plot)) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 40,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#7F1D1D",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(
      xmin = K_p05_pct,
      xmax = K_p95_pct
    ),
    height = 0.24,
    linewidth = 0.95,
    color = "#B45309"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = current_p2025_pct,
      fill = "Model-estimated p(2025)"
    ),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.9,
    color = "#1E3A8A",
    stroke = 0.45
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = K_mean_pct,
      fill = "Fitted K"
    ),
    shape = 23,
    size = 3.1,
    color = "#8A5A00",
    stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = ssa_goal_final_asymptote_right_x,
      y = geography_plot,
      label = k_label
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3.1,
    color = "#92400E",
    fontface = "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = ssa_goal_final_asymptote_label_header,
    aes(
      x = x,
      y = geography_plot,
      label = label
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = -1.1,
    size = 3.15,
    color = "#78350F",
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Model-estimated p(2025)" = "#60A5FA",
      "Fitted K" = "#F59E0B"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(
      0,
      ssa_goal_final_asymptote_right_x + 4
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.01))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8.8),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 75, 10, 10)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: model-estimated p(2025) versus fitted K",
    subtitle = "Only countries still below 40% at p(2025) are shown; countries are ordered from highest fitted K to lowest and right-side labels show fitted K",
    x = "mCPR or fitted K (%)",
    y = NULL
  )
```

```{r ssa-goals-final-pace-compare, fig.height=11}
ggplot(
  ssa_goal_final_pace_long,
  aes(
    x = growth_pp_per_year,
    y = geography_label_plot,
    color = growth_metric,
    shape = growth_metric
  )
) +
  geom_segment(
    data = ssa_goal_final_pace_range,
    aes(
      x = x_min,
      xend = x_max,
      y = geography_label_plot,
      yend = geography_label_plot
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, stroke = 0.35) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Required to hit 40% from p(2025)" = "#B91C1C",
      "Observed annualized growth from latest survey interval" = "#111827",
      "Model-implied annual growth at 2025" = "#2563EB",
      "Average model-projected growth, 2025-2035" = "#059669"
    )
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(
      "Required to hit 40% from p(2025)" = 15,
      "Observed annualized growth from latest survey interval" = 16,
      "Model-implied annual growth at 2025" = 17,
      "Average model-projected growth, 2025-2035" = 18
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.4) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: required annualized growth rate versus observed and model-based growth benchmarks",
    subtitle = "The red square is the annualized growth rate needed from p(2025) to reach 40% by 2035; countries to the right of their black, blue, and green markers need acceleration beyond those benchmarks",
    x = "Annual growth in mCPR (percentage points per year)",
    y = NULL
  )
```

### Required annualized growth rate versus model-implied current annualized growth rate

This is the cleanest single-view feasibility graph for the 40% target.

Interpretation:
a) the x-axis is the annual growth required to move from `p(2025)` to 40% by 2035;
b) the y-axis is the model-implied annual growth rate at `2025`;
c) the dotted 45-degree line is the diagonal reference line;
d) points above the dotted line are outperforming the annualized growth rate needed to reach 40% by 2035 under the current fitted trajectory;
e) points below the dotted line would need acceleration relative to the model-implied current annualized growth rate;
f) red points have fitted `K` mostly below 40%, so even fast current growth does not by itself solve the ceiling problem.

```{r ssa-goals-final-feasibility-scatter, fig.height=10}
ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits <- range(
  c(
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility$required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility$model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
    ssa_goal_final_region_feasibility$required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
    ssa_goal_final_region_feasibility$model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
)
ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1] <- min(0, ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1]) - 0.05
ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2] <- ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2] + 0.55

ssa_goal_final_feasibility_zone_above <- tibble(
  x = c(
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2]
  ),
  y = c(
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2]
  )
)

ssa_goal_final_feasibility_zone_below <- tibble(
  x = c(
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2]
  ),
  y = c(
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1],
    ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2]
  )
)

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_zone_above,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill = "#ECFDF5",
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_polygon(
    data = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_zone_below,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill = "#FFF7ED",
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "longdash",
    color = "#475569",
    linewidth = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_goal_final_feasibility,
    aes(
      x = required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      y = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
      size = women_15_49_millions,
      fill = pace_status,
      shape = crossed_status
    ),
    color = "white",
    alpha = 0.95,
    stroke = 0.45
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_goal_final_region_feasibility,
    aes(
      x = required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      y = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
      fill = pace_status
    ),
    shape = 23,
    size = 5,
    color = "grey15",
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = ssa_goal_final_feasibility,
    aes(
      x = required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      y = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
      label = geography
    ),
    seed = 2025,
    size = 3.0,
    color = "#111827",
    fontface = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey55",
    segment.alpha = 0.65,
    segment.size = 0.28,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    box.padding = 0.32,
    point.padding = 0.18,
    force = 1.2,
    force_pull = 0.12,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_label_repel(
    data = ssa_goal_final_region_feasibility,
    aes(
      x = required_growth_from_p2025_pp_per_year,
      y = model_dpdt_2025_pp_per_year,
      label = "SSA weighted"
    ),
    seed = 2025,
    size = 3.6,
    color = "#111827",
    fontface = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey40",
    segment.alpha = 0.75,
    segment.size = 0.34,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    box.padding = 0.42,
    point.padding = 0.26,
    force = 1.45,
    force_pull = 0.14,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    fill = "white",
    label.size = 0.22,
    label.r = unit(0.14, "lines"),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1] + 0.18,
    y = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2] - 0.08,
    label = "Above line:\ncurrent model annualized growth rate exceeds required annualized growth rate",
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 3.05,
    label.size = 0.2,
    fill = "#ECFDF5",
    color = "#14532D"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[2] - 0.15,
    y = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits[1] + 0.4,
    label = "Below line:\nacceleration is needed",
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 3.05,
    label.size = 0.2,
    fill = "#FFF7ED",
    color = "#9A3412"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_cols,
    breaks = c(
      "Current annualized growth rate above required",
      "Below required annualized growth rate, K above 40%",
      "Below required annualized growth rate, K spans 40%",
      "K below 40%"
    ),
    name = "Current annualized growth rate / K status"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(
      "Already at or above 40%" = 24,
      "Below 40% at latest observed" = 21
    ),
    name = "Latest observed status"
  ) +
  scale_size_area(
    max_size = 14,
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits,
    expand = expansion(mult = 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = ssa_goal_final_feasibility_limits,
    expand = expansion(mult = 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.8) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.35),
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.margin = margin(10, 90, 10, 10)
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      order = 1,
      override.aes = list(shape = 21, size = 4.5, color = "grey15", alpha = 1)
    ),
    shape = guide_legend(
      order = 2,
      override.aes = list(size = 4.5, fill = "grey55", color = "grey15", alpha = 1)
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: required annualized growth rate versus model-implied current annualized growth rate",
    subtitle = "Every country is labeled; the weighted SSA region is the diamond. Points above the diagonal are on a faster model-implied annualized growth rate than the annualized growth rate needed to reach 40% by 2035. Triangle markers indicate countries already at or above 40% at their latest observed survey.",
    x = "Required annual growth from p(2025) to reach 40% by 2035 (pp/year)",
    y = "Model-implied annual growth at 2025 (pp/year)"
  )
```

### Weighted SSA region and all country trajectories relative to 40%

The first panel is the population-weighted SSA region. Each country panel then shows:
a) observed DHS points or weighted regional observed points;
b) a solid fitted trajectory with 95% credible interval;
c) the 40% goal as a horizontal dashed line;
d) the black vertical guide line marks the goal year 2035;
e) the fitted ceiling `K` as a dotted horizontal line;
f) the year the trajectory reaches approximately 99% of fitted `K` as a dotted vertical line;
g) a crossing label stating whether the geography has already crossed 40%, is projected to cross by 2035, is projected to cross after 2035, or is not projected to cross.

```{r ssa-goals-final-all-trajectories, fig.height=24}
ggplot(ssa_goal_final_traj_plot, aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      ymin = 100 * p_p05,
      ymax = 100 * p_p95,
      fill = trajectory_status_final
    ),
    alpha = 0.16
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(color = trajectory_status_final),
    linewidth = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_goal_final_obs_plot,
    aes(x = year, y = mcp_pct),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    size = 1.4,
    alpha = 0.72
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 40,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#7F1D1D",
    linewidth = 0.42
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2035,
    linetype = "longdash",
    color = "grey20",
    linewidth = 0.48
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    data = ssa_goal_final_panel_meta,
    aes(yintercept = 100 * K_mean),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "#8A5A00",
    linewidth = 0.48
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = ssa_goal_final_panel_meta %>% filter(!is.na(year_reach_99pctK_mean)),
    aes(xintercept = year_reach_99pctK_mean),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "#8A5A00",
    linewidth = 0.48
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = ssa_goal_final_panel_meta,
    aes(
      x = annotation_x,
      y = annotation_y,
      label = crossing_label_final,
      color = trajectory_status_final
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0,
    size = 2.6
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel_label, ncol = 4, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_color_manual(values = ssa_goal_final_status_cols) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ssa_goal_final_status_cols) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9.6) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7.8, lineheight = 0.95),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6.6),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6.6)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: weighted SSA region first, then every country trajectory relative to 40%",
    subtitle = "Panel titles include women age 15-49 population size; the horizontal dashed line marks the 40% goal and the black vertical guide marks 2035. Line color encodes whether the geography already crossed, is projected to cross by 2035, is projected to cross later, or is not projected to cross",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR (%)"
  )
```

### Countries already above 40% at their latest observed survey

This graph isolates the countries whose latest observed DHS mCPR is already at or above 40%.

The crossing labels mark the first observed DHS survey year in which each country is at or above 40%, while the colored line shows the fitted trajectory around that observed crossing history.

```{r ssa-goals-final-already-crossed, fig.height=8}
ggplot(
  ssa_goal_final_already_traj,
  aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean, color = geography)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.95, alpha = 0.92) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_goal_final_already_obs,
    aes(x = year, y = mcp_pct, color = geography),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 40,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#7F1D1D",
    linewidth = 0.45
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_goal_final_already_cross_points,
    aes(x = year, y = mcp_pct, fill = geography),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 21,
    size = 3,
    stroke = 0.5,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = ssa_goal_final_already_cross_points,
    aes(x = year, y = mcp_pct, label = cross_label, color = geography),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3,
    segment.alpha = 0.35,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ssa_goal_final_already_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ssa_goal_final_already_colors) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 70)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: countries already above 40% at their latest observed survey",
    subtitle = "Crossing labels use the first observed DHS survey year at or above 40%; fitted lines show the broader trajectory around that threshold",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR (%)"
  )
```

### Countries still below 40% at their latest observed survey

This graph switches fully to the model projection. It starts in 2025 and shows whether countries below 40% at their latest observed survey are projected to cross 40%, whether that happens by 2035 or later, and roughly when that crossing is expected.

```{r ssa-goals-final-notyet-crossed, fig.height=11}
ggplot(
  ssa_goal_final_notyet_traj,
  aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean, group = geography, color = trajectory_status_final)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 40,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#7F1D1D",
    linewidth = 0.45
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2035,
    linetype = "longdash",
    color = "grey25",
    linewidth = 0.45
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_label_repel(
    data = ssa_goal_final_notyet_label_points,
    aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean, label = projection_label, color = trajectory_status_final),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    direction = "y",
    hjust = 0,
    nudge_x = 2.8,
    size = 2.7,
    segment.alpha = 0.38,
    segment.size = 0.28,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    box.padding = 0.28,
    point.padding = 0.2,
    label.size = 0.16,
    label.padding = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    label.r = unit(0.12, "lines"),
    fill = "white",
    fontface = "bold",
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = ssa_goal_final_status_cols[c(
      "Projected to cross by 2035",
      "Projected to cross after 2035",
      "Not projected to cross"
    )]
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = ssa_goal_final_status_cols[c(
      "Projected to cross by 2035",
      "Projected to cross after 2035",
      "Not projected to cross"
    )]
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(2025, 2066), ylim = c(0, 100), clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.3) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 80, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals - final: projected trajectories for countries still below 40% at last observation",
    subtitle = "Boxed labels distinguish countries projected to cross by 2035, projected to cross later, and countries not projected to cross under the fitted trajectory",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR (%)"
  )
```

## SSA outliers relative to the weighted regional benchmark

This section asks a simple comparative question: which countries sit above or below the weighted SSA benchmark on two core dimensions:
a) fitted ceiling `K`;
b) current model-implied annual growth at 2026.

The regional benchmark is always the population-weighted SSA benchmark derived from the main country model.

```{r ssa-outlier-stage-setup}
ssa_stage_cols <- c(
  "Stage 1: initial" = "#D55E00",
  "Stage 2: diffusion" = "#0072B2",
  "Stage 3: plateau" = "#009E73"
)

ssa_outlier_k_diff_draws <- sweep(
  ssa_K_draws,
  1,
  ssa_region_K_draws,
  FUN = "-"
)

ssa_outlier_growth_diff_draws <- sweep(
  dpdt_mat[, ssa_country_cols, drop = FALSE],
  1,
  ssa_region_dpdt_2026_draws,
  FUN = "-"
)

ssa_outlier_table <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    region,
    women_15_49,
    women_15_49_millions = women_15_49 / 1e6,
    K_mean,
    model_dpdt_2026_pp_per_year,
    t0_year_mean,
    years_since_t0_2026,
    s_curve_stage_2026
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    k_diff_vs_ssa_pp = 100 * colMeans(ssa_outlier_k_diff_draws),
    growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year = 100 * colMeans(ssa_outlier_growth_diff_draws),
    prob_k_above_ssa = colMeans(ssa_outlier_k_diff_draws > 0),
    prob_growth_above_ssa = colMeans(ssa_outlier_growth_diff_draws > 0)
  )

ssa_outlier_k_scale <- stats::sd(ssa_outlier_table$k_diff_vs_ssa_pp, na.rm = TRUE)
ssa_outlier_growth_scale <- stats::sd(ssa_outlier_table$growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year, na.rm = TRUE)
ssa_outlier_k_scale <- ifelse(is.finite(ssa_outlier_k_scale) && ssa_outlier_k_scale > 0, ssa_outlier_k_scale, 1)
ssa_outlier_growth_scale <- ifelse(is.finite(ssa_outlier_growth_scale) && ssa_outlier_growth_scale > 0, ssa_outlier_growth_scale, 1)

ssa_outlier_table <- ssa_outlier_table %>%
  mutate(
    outlier_quadrant = case_when(
      k_diff_vs_ssa_pp >= 0 & growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year >= 0 ~ "Higher K, higher growth than SSA",
      k_diff_vs_ssa_pp >= 0 & growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year < 0 ~ "Higher K, lower growth than SSA",
      k_diff_vs_ssa_pp < 0 & growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year >= 0 ~ "Lower K, higher growth than SSA",
      TRUE ~ "Lower K, lower growth than SSA"
    ),
    outlier_distance = sqrt(
      (k_diff_vs_ssa_pp / ssa_outlier_k_scale)^2 +
        (growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year / ssa_outlier_growth_scale)^2
    ),
    outlier_label_flag = case_when(
      country %in% c("Nigeria", "Congo Democratic Republic") ~ TRUE,
      outlier_distance >= quantile(outlier_distance, 0.86, na.rm = TRUE) ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE
    )
  )

ssa_outlier_quadrant_cols <- c(
  "Higher K, higher growth than SSA" = "#1D4ED8",
  "Higher K, lower growth than SSA" = "#D97706",
  "Lower K, higher growth than SSA" = "#059669",
  "Lower K, lower growth than SSA" = "#B91C1C"
)

ssa_region_rel_sat_2026_draws <- pmin(
  pmax(ssa_region_p2026_draws / ssa_region_K_draws, 1e-6),
  1 - 1e-6
)

ssa_region_stage_position_2026_draws <- qlogis(ssa_region_rel_sat_2026_draws)

ssa_region_inflection_year_draws <- apply(
  ssa_region_draws_k_horizon >=
    matrix(
      0.5 * ssa_region_K_draws,
      nrow = nrow(ssa_region_draws_k_horizon),
      ncol = ncol(ssa_region_draws_k_horizon)
    ),
  1,
  function(hit_vec) {
    idx <- which(hit_vec)
    if (length(idx) == 0) NA_real_ else ssa_region_k_horizon_years[min(idx)]
  }
)

ssa_region_stage1_2026 <- ssa_region_stage_position_2026_draws < -1.5
ssa_region_stage3_2026 <- ssa_region_stage_position_2026_draws > 1.5
ssa_region_stage2_2026 <- !(ssa_region_stage1_2026 | ssa_region_stage3_2026)

ssa_region_stage_summary <- tibble(
  geography = "Sub-Saharan Africa (weighted)",
  decision_year = 2026,
  p_2026_mean = mean(ssa_region_p2026_draws),
  K_mean = mean(ssa_region_K_draws),
  rel_sat_2026_mean = mean(ssa_region_rel_sat_2026_draws),
  stage_position_2026_mean = mean(ssa_region_stage_position_2026_draws),
  inflection_year_p50 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws))) NA_real_ else median(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws, na.rm = TRUE),
  inflection_year_p05 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws))) NA_real_ else quantile(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws, ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
  inflection_year_p95 = if (all(is.na(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws))) NA_real_ else quantile(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws, ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
  prob_stage1_initial_2026 = mean(ssa_region_stage1_2026),
  prob_stage2_diffusion_2026 = mean(ssa_region_stage2_2026),
  prob_stage3_plateau_2026 = mean(ssa_region_stage3_2026)
) %>%
  mutate(
    s_curve_stage_2026 = case_when(
      prob_stage1_initial_2026 >= prob_stage2_diffusion_2026 &
        prob_stage1_initial_2026 >= prob_stage3_plateau_2026 ~ "Stage 1: initial",
      prob_stage3_plateau_2026 >= prob_stage1_initial_2026 &
        prob_stage3_plateau_2026 >= prob_stage2_diffusion_2026 ~ "Stage 3: plateau",
      TRUE ~ "Stage 2: diffusion"
    )
  )

ssa_region_stage_end_year <- pmin(
  2060,
  pmax(
    2045,
    5 * ceiling(
      ifelse(
        all(is.na(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws)),
        2045,
        quantile(ssa_region_inflection_year_draws, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
      ) / 5
    )
  )
)

ssa_region_stage_plot <- ssa_goal_final_traj_region %>%
  filter(year <= ssa_region_stage_end_year)

ssa_region_stage_note <- paste0(
  "Stage at 2026: ", ssa_region_stage_summary$s_curve_stage_2026,
  "\nWeighted K: ", round(100 * ssa_region_stage_summary$K_mean, 1), "%",
  "\nWeighted p(2026): ", round(100 * ssa_region_stage_summary$p_2026_mean, 1), "%",
  "\nRelative saturation p/K: ", round(100 * ssa_region_stage_summary$rel_sat_2026_mean, 1), "%",
  "\nInflection year: ", round(ssa_region_stage_summary$inflection_year_p50, 1),
  " (95% CrI ", round(ssa_region_stage_summary$inflection_year_p05, 1),
  "-", round(ssa_region_stage_summary$inflection_year_p95, 1), ")"
)

ssa_stage_country_meta <- ssa_goals_country_table %>%
  left_join(
    s_curve_stage_2026_table %>%
      select(
        country,
        stage_position_2026_mean,
        rel_sat_2026_mean
      ),
    by = "country"
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    country,
    region,
    women_15_49_millions = women_15_49 / 1e6,
    K_mean,
    t0_year_mean,
    p2026_mean,
    years_since_t0_2026,
    stage_position_2026_mean,
    rel_sat_2026_mean,
    s_curve_stage_2026,
    stage_panel_label = paste0(
      country,
      "\n",
      s_curve_stage_2026,
      " | t0~",
      round(t0_year_mean),
      " | K ",
      round(100 * K_mean),
      "%"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(
      s_curve_stage_2026,
      levels = c("Stage 1: initial", "Stage 2: diffusion", "Stage 3: plateau")
    ),
    years_since_t0_2026,
    desc(women_15_49_millions),
    country
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    stage_panel_label = factor(
      stage_panel_label,
      levels = stage_panel_label,
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  )

ssa_stage_country_plot <- ssa_goal_final_traj_country %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_stage_country_meta,
    by = c("geography" = "country")
  )

ssa_stage_country_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(region %in% ssa_regions) %>%
  left_join(
    ssa_stage_country_meta %>% select(country, stage_panel_label),
    by = c("country" = "country")
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    stage_panel_label,
    year,
    mcp_pct = 100 * mCPR
  )
```

### Outlier map: K and growth relative to weighted SSA

This map is the quickest visual way to see over- and underperformance relative to the weighted SSA benchmark.

Interpretation:
a) points to the right have a higher fitted `K` than the weighted SSA region;
b) points above zero have faster current model-implied growth than the weighted SSA region;
c) point size shows women age 15-49 population;
d) countries far from the origin are the clearest outliers relative to the weighted SSA benchmark.

```{r ssa-outlier-map}
ggplot(
  ssa_outlier_table,
  aes(
    x = k_diff_vs_ssa_pp,
    y = growth_diff_vs_ssa_pp_per_year,
    size = women_15_49_millions,
    fill = outlier_quadrant
  )
) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "grey15", alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0.35) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = ssa_outlier_table %>% filter(outlier_label_flag),
    aes(label = country),
    size = 3,
    color = "grey15",
    segment.alpha = 0.35,
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ssa_outlier_quadrant_cols) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 16, name = "Women 15-49\n(millions)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10.5) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA outlier map: fitted K and current growth relative to the weighted regional benchmark",
    subtitle = "Right of zero means a country's fitted ceiling is above weighted SSA K; above zero means current model-implied growth at 2026 is above weighted SSA growth",
    x = "Country K minus weighted SSA K (percentage points)",
    y = "Country growth minus weighted SSA growth at 2026 (pp/year)"
  )
```

## S-curve stage positioning for the weighted SSA region and all SSA countries

This section uses the same 2026 logistic stage logic as the country stage classification above. For countries, the stage rule is exactly the one already used in the report. For the weighted SSA region, we apply the same logic to the population-weighted regional `p/K` position implied by the aggregated posterior curve.

### Weighted SSA region on the S curve

The weighted SSA curve below shows the region's current position on the S curve, using the **population-weighted SSA regional mCPR implied by the fitted country model**. The black dots are the **population-weighted observed SSA regional mCPR points** by calendar year, built from observed country mCPR values and WPP women age 15-49 population weights for that year. The colored horizontal lines mark the **40% goal** and the **weighted fitted `K`**, and the dotted vertical line marks the weighted region's implied inflection year.

```{r ssa-stage-region-plot}
ssa_region_stage_goal_y <- 40
ssa_region_stage_k_y <- 100 * ssa_region_stage_summary$K_mean
ssa_region_stage_label_x <- max(ssa_region_stage_plot$year, na.rm = TRUE) - 1.2
ssa_region_stage_note_x <- min(ssa_region_stage_plot$year, na.rm = TRUE) + 3
ssa_region_stage_goal_col <- "#C2410C"
ssa_region_stage_k_col <- "#8A5A00"
ssa_region_stage_curve_col <- ssa_stage_cols[[ssa_region_stage_summary$s_curve_stage_2026]]
ssa_region_stage_cross_col <- "#7C3AED"
ssa_region_stage_cross_x <- ssa_region_goals_summary$projected_year_reach_40_p50
ssa_region_stage_cross_label <- if (is.na(ssa_region_stage_cross_x)) {
  "No weighted regional crossing projected"
} else {
  paste0("Weighted crossing year: ~", round(ssa_region_stage_cross_x, 1))
}

ggplot(ssa_region_stage_plot, aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = 100 * p_p05, ymax = 100 * p_p95),
    fill = ssa_region_stage_curve_col,
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = ssa_region_stage_curve_col,
    linewidth = 1.25
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_observed_weighted,
    aes(x = year, y = 100 * observed_weighted_mcpr),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "#111827",
    fill = "#111827",
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.5,
    stroke = 0.25,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = 2026, y = 100 * ssa_region_stage_summary$p_2026_mean),
    shape = 21,
    size = 4.2,
    stroke = 0.7,
    fill = "white",
    color = ssa_region_stage_curve_col
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = ssa_region_stage_goal_y,
    linetype = "longdash",
    color = ssa_region_stage_goal_col,
    linewidth = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = ssa_region_stage_k_y,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = ssa_region_stage_k_col,
    linewidth = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = ssa_region_stage_summary$inflection_year_p50,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "#1D4ED8",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2035,
    linetype = "longdash",
    color = "grey45",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  {
    if (!is.na(ssa_region_stage_cross_x)) {
      geom_vline(
        xintercept = ssa_region_stage_cross_x,
        linetype = "dotdash",
        color = ssa_region_stage_cross_col,
        linewidth = 0.95
      )
    }
  } +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = ssa_region_stage_label_x,
    y = ssa_region_stage_goal_y + 1.4,
    label = "40% goal",
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 4.1,
    fontface = "bold",
    label.size = 0.25,
    fill = "#FFF7ED",
    color = ssa_region_stage_goal_col
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = ssa_region_stage_label_x,
    y = ssa_region_stage_k_y + 1.6,
    label = paste0("Weighted K: ", round(ssa_region_stage_k_y, 1), "%"),
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 4.1,
    fontface = "bold",
    label.size = 0.25,
    fill = "#FFFBEB",
    color = ssa_region_stage_k_col
  ) +
  {
    if (!is.na(ssa_region_stage_cross_x)) {
      annotate(
        "label",
        x = ssa_region_stage_cross_x,
        y = 8,
        label = ssa_region_stage_cross_label,
        angle = 90,
        hjust = 0,
        vjust = -0.15,
        size = 3.6,
        fontface = "bold",
        label.size = 0.25,
        fill = "#F5F3FF",
        color = ssa_region_stage_cross_col
      )
    }
  } +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = ssa_region_stage_note_x,
    y = min(98, 100 * ssa_region_stage_summary$K_mean + 8),
    label = ssa_region_stage_note,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 3.6,
    label.size = 0.2,
    fill = "white",
    color = "grey15"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(c(
      seq(
        floor(min(ssa_region_stage_plot$year, na.rm = TRUE) / 5) * 5,
        ceiling(max(ssa_region_stage_plot$year, na.rm = TRUE) / 5) * 5,
        by = 5
      ),
      2035
    )))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.10))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12.5) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey88", linewidth = 0.35),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.35),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.65),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.55),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11.5, color = "grey15"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11.4, lineheight = 1.08)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weighted SSA region on the S curve at 2026",
    subtitle = "This is the population-weighted SSA regional mCPR implied by the fitted country model. Black dots are population-weighted observed SSA regional mCPR values; the colored horizontal lines mark the 40% goal and weighted K, and the vertical lines mark the inflection year, 2035 horizon, and weighted regional crossing year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Population-weighted model mCPR (%)"
  )
```

### Every SSA country on the S curve

Each panel uses the 2026 S-curve stage classification and shows the country trajectory, fitted `K`, and inflection year directly.

How the 3-stage classification is calculated here:
a) within each posterior draw, we place the country at 2026 on the logistic curve using the standardized position \(x_i(2026) = (2026 - t_{0,i}) / s_i\), where \(t_{0,i}\) is the inflection year and \(s_i\) is the country speed parameter;
b) equivalently, this is the logit-scale position implied by the model-estimated ratio \(p_i(2026) / K_i\);
c) `Stage 1: initial` if \(x_i(2026) < -1.5\), which corresponds to \(p_i(2026) / K_i < \operatorname{logit}^{-1}(-1.5) \approx 0.18\);
d) `Stage 2: diffusion` if \(-1.5 \le x_i(2026) \le 1.5\), which corresponds to roughly \(0.18 \le p_i(2026) / K_i \le 0.82\);
e) `Stage 3: plateau` if \(x_i(2026) > 1.5\), which corresponds to \(p_i(2026) / K_i > \operatorname{logit}^{-1}(1.5) \approx 0.82\);
f) the plotted stage label is the stage with the highest posterior probability across draws.

For the weighted SSA region, we apply the same cutpoints to the population-weighted regional posterior position on the S curve, using the aggregated posterior ratio \(p_{\mathrm{SSA}}(2026) / K_{\mathrm{SSA}}\).

Line guide for the figure:
a) the colored line and ribbon show the posterior mean trajectory and 95% credible interval;
b) the ochre dotted horizontal line marks fitted `K`;
c) the blue dotted vertical line marks the inflection year `t0`;
d) the dark dashed vertical line marks the goal year `2035`.

```{r ssa-stage-country-trajectories, fig.height=24}
ggplot(ssa_stage_country_plot, aes(x = year, y = 100 * p_mean)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      ymin = 100 * p_p05,
      ymax = 100 * p_p95,
      fill = s_curve_stage_2026
    ),
    alpha = 0.15
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(color = s_curve_stage_2026),
    linewidth = 0.88
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_stage_country_obs,
    aes(x = year, y = mcp_pct),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    size = 1.3,
    alpha = 0.68
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ssa_stage_country_meta,
    aes(x = 2026, y = 100 * p2026_mean, fill = s_curve_stage_2026),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 21,
    size = 1.9,
    stroke = 0.25,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    data = ssa_stage_country_meta,
    aes(yintercept = 100 * K_mean, linetype = "Fitted K"),
    color = "#8A5A00",
    linewidth = 0.72,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = ssa_stage_country_meta,
    aes(xintercept = t0_year_mean, linetype = "Inflection year"),
    color = "#1D4ED8",
    linewidth = 0.74,
    alpha = 0.98
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = tibble(goal_year = 2035, line_key = "Goal year (2035)"),
    aes(xintercept = goal_year, linetype = line_key),
    color = "grey15",
    linewidth = 0.8,
    alpha = 0.98
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ stage_panel_label, ncol = 4, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_color_manual(values = ssa_stage_cols, name = "Stage at 2026") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ssa_stage_cols, name = "Stage at 2026") +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    name = "Reference lines",
    values = c(
      "Fitted K" = "dotted",
      "Inflection year" = "dotted",
      "Goal year (2035)" = "longdash"
    ),
    breaks = c("Fitted K", "Inflection year", "Goal year (2035)")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = function(x) {
      brks <- pretty(x, n = 4)
      brks[brks != 2035]
    },
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100), clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9.4) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey88", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey25", linewidth = 0.45),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey25", linewidth = 0.4),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7.5, lineheight = 0.95),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6.8, color = "grey15"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6.5, color = "grey15"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9.6, margin = margin(t = 7)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9.4),
    plot.margin = margin(6, 8, 14, 8)
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      order = 1,
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.05, alpha = 1)
    ),
    fill = "none",
    linetype = guide_legend(
      order = 2,
      override.aes = list(
        color = c("#8A5A00", "#1D4ED8", "grey15"),
        linewidth = c(0.72, 0.74, 0.8)
      )
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SSA country S-curve stages at 2026",
    subtitle = "Panels are ordered by stage. The shaded ribbon is the 95% credible interval around the fitted trajectory, stage colors reflect the modal posterior stage at 2026, and the reference-line legend distinguishes fitted K, the inflection year, and the 2035 goal year.",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR (%)"
  )
```

## Global posterior predicted trajectory

Technical note:
a) A recurring failure mode in this project was `predictor_fe(...)` in global prediction when region-level `newdata` was built from external templates that did not perfectly align with the fitted factor structure.
b) This chunk builds region rows from the fitted DHS data itself and checks `region_prior`, `K_region_fixed`, and region weights before prediction.

```{r global-posterior-predicted}
# Compute and plot global posterior trajectory with 95% credible interval.
year_grid_global <- seq(min(mcpr_logistic_data$year, na.rm = TRUE), 2036, by = 1)

# Build a population-level grid by region and year, then aggregate with region weights.
# IMPORTANT:
# For nonlinear brms prediction, `newdata` must carry predictor columns with the same
# classes/levels as the fitted model design matrix. We build from mcpr_logistic_data
# directly to avoid `predictor_fe` errors from mismatched factor levels or missing joins.
region_profile <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
  filter(!is.na(region_prior), !is.na(K_region_fixed)) %>%
  mutate(region_prior = as.character(region_prior)) %>%
  arrange(region_prior)

region_weights <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  distinct(country, region_prior) %>%
  mutate(region_prior = as.character(region_prior)) %>%
  count(region_prior, name = "n_country") %>%
  inner_join(region_profile %>% select(region_prior), by = "region_prior") %>%
  mutate(weight = n_country / sum(n_country))

nd_global <- tidyr::crossing(
  region_profile,
  year = year_grid_global
) %>%
  mutate(
    region_prior = as.character(region_prior),
    country = as.character(mcpr_logistic_data$country[1]),
    year_center = year - 2000,
    n = 1L,
    y = 0L
  ) %>%
  left_join(region_weights %>% select(region_prior, weight), by = "region_prior")

if (any(is.na(nd_global$region_prior))) {
  stop("nd_global contains NA region_prior; cannot run posterior prediction.")
}
if (any(is.na(nd_global$K_region_fixed))) {
  stop("nd_global contains NA K_region_fixed; cannot run posterior prediction.")
}
if (any(is.na(nd_global$weight))) {
  stop("nd_global contains NA region weights; cannot run global aggregation.")
}

ep_global_region <- posterior_epred(fit_logistic, newdata = nd_global, re_formula = NA)

# Weighted average over region-specific fitted curves for each posterior draw and year.
ep_global <- sapply(year_grid_global, function(yy) {
  idx <- which(nd_global$year == yy)
  ep_global_region[, idx, drop = FALSE] %*% nd_global$weight[idx]
})

global_curve <- tibble(
  year = year_grid_global,
  p_mean = colMeans(ep_global),
  p_lo = apply(ep_global, 2, quantile, ci_lower),
  p_hi = apply(ep_global, 2, quantile, ci_upper)
)

obs_all <- mcpr_logistic_data %>% select(year, mCPR)

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = obs_all, aes(x = year, y = mCPR), alpha = 0.20, size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(data = global_curve, aes(x = year, ymin = p_lo, ymax = p_hi), alpha = 0.20) +
  geom_line(data = global_curve, aes(x = year, y = p_mean), linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Global posterior predicted mCPR trajectory",
    subtitle = "Region-weighted population-level fit (excluding country random effects), with 95% credible interval",
    x = "Year",
    y = "mCPR"
  )
```

## Implied growth and acceleration (global posterior)

This section reports derivatives of the posterior mean global trajectory:

a) First derivative \(dp/dt\): implied growth rate.
b) Second derivative \(d^2p/dt^2\): implied acceleration or deceleration.

```{r global-posterior-derivatives}
# Derive and plot first/second derivatives with posterior uncertainty bands.
global_curve_deriv <- global_curve %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  mutate(
    dp_dt = (lead(p_mean) - lag(p_mean)) / (lead(year) - lag(year)),
    d2p_dt2 = (lead(p_mean) - 2 * p_mean + lag(p_mean)) / (((lead(year) - lag(year)) / 2)^2)
  )

# ep_global has dimensions: draws x years (created in global-posterior-predicted chunk).
n_year <- ncol(ep_global)
if (n_year >= 3) {
  # Central-difference derivatives by posterior draw.
  dp_draws <- sapply(2:(n_year - 1), function(j) {
    (ep_global[, j + 1] - ep_global[, j - 1]) / (year_grid_global[j + 1] - year_grid_global[j - 1])
  })

  d2p_draws <- sapply(2:(n_year - 1), function(j) {
    (ep_global[, j + 1] - 2 * ep_global[, j] + ep_global[, j - 1]) /
      (((year_grid_global[j + 1] - year_grid_global[j - 1]) / 2)^2)
  })

  deriv_ci <- tibble(
    year = year_grid_global[2:(n_year - 1)],
    dp_p05 = apply(dp_draws, 2, quantile, probs = ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    dp_p50 = apply(dp_draws, 2, quantile, probs = 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
    dp_p95 = apply(dp_draws, 2, quantile, probs = ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
    d2p_p05 = apply(d2p_draws, 2, quantile, probs = ci_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    d2p_p50 = apply(d2p_draws, 2, quantile, probs = 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
    d2p_p95 = apply(d2p_draws, 2, quantile, probs = ci_upper, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

  ggplot(deriv_ci, aes(x = year, y = dp_p50)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = dp_p05, ymax = dp_p95), alpha = 0.20, fill = "#6BAED6") +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#2171B5") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    labs(
      title = "Global implied growth rate (first derivative)",
      subtitle = "Median with 95% credible interval",
      x = "Year",
      y = "dp/dt"
    )

  ggplot(deriv_ci, aes(x = year, y = d2p_p50)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = d2p_p05, ymax = d2p_p95), alpha = 0.20, fill = "#F4A582") +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#CA0020") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    labs(
      title = "Global implied acceleration (second derivative)",
      subtitle = "Median with 95% credible interval",
      x = "Year",
      y = "d2p/dt2"
    )
}
```

# Exploratory regional check: latest observed mCPR by region

This exploratory section aggregates each country's most recent observed mCPR and compares the distribution across the eight analytic regions.

```{r exploratory-latest-mcpr-region}
# Keep one row per country: the latest non-missing observed survey value.
latest_country_obs <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(mCPR), !is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(country, region) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(country, region, last_year = year, last_mcpr = mCPR)

# Summarize latest country values within each region.
latest_region_summary <- latest_country_obs %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries = n(),
    mean_last_mcpr = mean(last_mcpr, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_last_mcpr = median(last_mcpr, na.rm = TRUE),
    p25_last_mcpr = quantile(last_mcpr, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    p75_last_mcpr = quantile(last_mcpr, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_last_mcpr = min(last_mcpr, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_last_mcpr = max(last_mcpr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(region)

knitr::kable(
  latest_region_summary %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))),
  caption = "Latest observed country mCPR by region (country-last observation summary)"
)
```

```{r exploratory-latest-mcpr-region-plot}
# Visualize region-level spread of latest observed country values.
ggplot(latest_country_obs, aes(x = factor(region, levels = sort(unique(region))), y = last_mcpr, color = region)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.12, width = 0.55) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.75, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Latest observed country mCPR by region",
    subtitle = "Each point is a country's most recent observed mCPR",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Latest observed mCPR"
  )
```

# Appendix

## Appendix A: Modeling options tried and identifiability

We tested six candidate specifications before settling on the final model.

Options tried to date:
a) **Option A:** fully country-varying \(K_i\), \(t_{0,i}\), and \(s_i\). This was the most flexible form, but it was typically too slow and did not converge reliably.
b) **Option B:** region-fixed \(K_r\), country-varying \(s_i\), and region-plus-country \(t_0\). This is the current alternative final model because it removes one major source of \(K\)-\(s\) confounding while preserving country-level speed variation.
c) **Option C:** country-varying \(K_i\), region-fixed \(s_r\), and region-plus-country \(t_0\). This is the production comparator because it preserves country headroom while breaking the same \(K\)-\(s\) confounding from the opposite direction.
d) **Option D:** region-fixed effects for both \(K\) and \(s\) with country random effects around each. This improved shrinkage but still left weakly identified speed terms in several regions.
e) **Option E:** stronger shrinkage on the country-level variation in both \(K\) and \(s\) under region anchoring. This was more stable than Options A and D, but still showed convergence problems in weak-data settings.
f) **Option F:** reparameterized peak-growth timing with stronger shrinkage on speed dispersion and region anchoring. This still failed to mix adequately in the country-specific speed parameters.

The core issue across the rejected variants was the **identifiability tradeoff between \(K\) and \(s\)**: with sparse or noisy country series, higher ceilings with slower speeds can fit almost as well as lower ceilings with faster speeds.

In practical terms:
a) Fixing \(K\) at region medians stabilizes estimation by removing one of the two most weakly identified nonlinear components.
b) Allowing \(s\) to vary by country preserves the country-level variation in transition speed that is central to this alternative specification.

## Appendix B: Phase Classification Sensitivity to Fixed-\(s\) Values

This appendix checks whether phase rankings are robust when re-fitting fixed-speed models at several \(s\) values.

```{r appendix-fixeds-settings}
run_appendix_fixeds_sensitivity <- TRUE
fixed_s_grid <- c(6, 8, 10, 12)

summarise_fit_diag_appendix <- function(fit_obj, model_name, td_max = 15) {
  dsum <- posterior::summarise_draws(
    posterior::as_draws_df(fit_obj),
    "rhat", "ess_bulk", "ess_tail"
  )
  nuts <- nuts_params(fit_obj)
  td_vals <- nuts$Value[nuts$Parameter == "treedepth__"]
  tibble(
    model = model_name,
    n_rhat_gt_101 = sum(dsum$rhat > 1.01, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_rhat_gt_105 = sum(dsum$rhat > 1.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_ess_bulk = min(dsum$ess_bulk, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_ess_tail = min(dsum$ess_tail, na.rm = TRUE),
    divergences = sum(nuts$Parameter == "divergent__" & nuts$Value == 1),
    max_treedepth = max(td_vals),
    n_treedepth_at_max = sum(td_vals >= td_max)
  )
}

classify_investment_from_summary <- function(country_summary_df, pct_scaleup = 0.20, pct_maintain = 0.20) {
  df <- country_summary_df %>%
    mutate(
      room_abs = head_mean,
      room_rel = 1 - rel_mean,
      r_growth = dplyr::percent_rank(dpdt_mean),
      r_head = dplyr::percent_rank(room_abs),
      r_room = dplyr::percent_rank(room_rel),
      leverage_head = r_growth * r_head,
      leverage_rel = r_growth * r_room,
      leverage = 0.4 * leverage_head + 0.6 * leverage_rel
    )

  n_total <- nrow(df)
  n_scaleup <- max(5, round(pct_scaleup * n_total))
  n_maintain <- max(5, round(pct_maintain * n_total))

  scaleup_set <- df %>% arrange(desc(leverage)) %>% slice_head(n = n_scaleup) %>% pull(country)
  maintain_set <- df %>% arrange(leverage) %>% slice_head(n = n_maintain) %>% pull(country)

  df %>%
    mutate(
      phase = case_when(
        country %in% scaleup_set ~ "Scale-up",
        country %in% maintain_set ~ "Maintain / equity",
        TRUE ~ "Build / monitor"
      )
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(leverage))
}
```

```{r appendix-fixeds-fit}
logistic_fixeds_grid <- bf(
  y | trials(n) ~ K_region_fixed *
    inv_logit((year_center - (-34 + 69 * inv_logit(t0raw))) / s_fixed),
  t0raw ~ 0 + region_prior + (1 | country),
  nl = TRUE
)

priors_fixeds_grid <- c(
  t0_region_priors_internal,
  prior(exponential(0.5), nlpar = "t0raw",   class = "sd", group = "country")
)

fixeds_fit_results <- list()
fixeds_diag_rows <- list()

if (run_appendix_fixeds_sensitivity) {
  for (s_val in fixed_s_grid) {
    fit_path_s <- if (s_val == 8) {
      paste0("fit_logistic_regionFixedK_countryS_compare_fixedS8_", run_profile_label, ".rds")
    } else {
      paste0("fit_logistic_regionFixedK_countryS_compare_fixedS", s_val, "_", run_profile_label, ".rds")
    }

    model_data_s <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
      mutate(s_fixed = s_val)

    if (file.exists(fit_path_s)) {
      fit_s <- readRDS(fit_path_s)
    } else {
      fit_s <- brm(
        formula = logistic_fixeds_grid,
        data    = model_data_s,
        family  = binomial(link = "identity"),
        prior   = priors_fixeds_grid,
        chains  = 4,
        cores   = 4,
        iter    = appendix_fixeds_iter,
        warmup  = appendix_fixeds_warmup,
        seed    = 8000 + s_val,
        control = list(adapt_delta = 0.995, max_treedepth = 15)
      )
      saveRDS(fit_s, fit_path_s)
    }

    nd_country_s <- model_data_s %>%
      distinct(country, region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
      arrange(country) %>%
      mutate(year_center = 0, n = 1, y = 0, s_fixed = s_val)

    t0_raw_draws_s <- posterior_linpred(
      fit_s, nlpar = "t0raw", newdata = nd_country_s,
      re_formula = NULL, transform = FALSE, summary = FALSE
    )
    t0_draws_s <- t0_lower + t0_span * plogis(t0_raw_draws_s)

    n_draws_s <- nrow(t0_draws_s)
    n_countries_s <- ncol(t0_draws_s)
    K_draws_s <- matrix(
      rep(nd_country_s$K_region_fixed, each = n_draws_s),
      nrow = n_draws_s,
      ncol = n_countries_s
    )
    t_dec <- 2026 - 2000
    t_mat_s <- matrix(t_dec, nrow = n_draws_s, ncol = n_countries_s)
    s_mat_s <- matrix(s_val, nrow = n_draws_s, ncol = n_countries_s)

    z_mat_s <- exp(-(t_mat_s - t0_draws_s) / s_mat_s)
    p_mat_s <- K_draws_s / (1 + z_mat_s)
    dpdt_mat_s <- (K_draws_s / s_mat_s) * z_mat_s / (1 + z_mat_s)^2

    qcol <- function(x, p) apply(x, 2, quantile, probs = p)

    country_summary_s <- tibble(
      country = nd_country_s$country,
      decision_year = 2026,
      s_assumed = s_val,
      K_mean = colMeans(K_draws_s),
      p_mean = colMeans(p_mat_s),
      head_mean = colMeans(K_draws_s - p_mat_s),
      rel_mean = colMeans(p_mat_s / K_draws_s),
      dpdt_mean = colMeans(dpdt_mat_s),
      dpdt_p05 = qcol(dpdt_mat_s, ci_lower),
      dpdt_p95 = qcol(dpdt_mat_s, ci_upper)
    )

    investment_s <- classify_investment_from_summary(country_summary_s)

    fixeds_fit_results[[as.character(s_val)]] <- list(
      fit = fit_s,
      summary = country_summary_s,
      investment = investment_s
    )

    fixeds_diag_rows[[as.character(s_val)]] <- summarise_fit_diag_appendix(
      fit_s, paste0("Fixed s = ", s_val)
    )
  }

  fixeds_diag_tbl <- bind_rows(fixeds_diag_rows)
  knitr::kable(
    fixeds_diag_tbl %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
    caption = "Appendix: convergence diagnostics across fixed-speed sensitivity fits"
  )
}
```

```{r appendix-fixeds-stability}
if (run_appendix_fixeds_sensitivity && length(fixeds_fit_results) > 0) {
  final_rank <- investment_table %>%
    arrange(desc(leverage)) %>%
    mutate(rank_final = row_number()) %>%
    select(country, phase_final = phase, rank_final)

  fixeds_assignments <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(fixeds_fit_results), function(s_chr) {
      fixeds_fit_results[[s_chr]]$investment %>%
        mutate(
          scenario = paste0("Fixed s=", s_chr),
          rank_s = row_number()
        ) %>%
        select(country, scenario, phase, rank_s, leverage, dpdt_mean, head_mean, room_rel)
    })
  )

  fixeds_stability_tbl <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(fixeds_fit_results), function(s_chr) {
      inv <- fixeds_fit_results[[s_chr]]$investment %>%
        mutate(rank_s = row_number()) %>%
        select(country, phase_s = phase, rank_s)

      cmp <- final_rank %>%
        inner_join(inv, by = "country") %>%
        mutate(phase_changed = phase_final != phase_s)

      final_scale <- final_rank %>% filter(phase_final == "Scale-up") %>% pull(country)
      s_scale <- inv %>% filter(phase_s == "Scale-up") %>% pull(country)
      final_main <- final_rank %>% filter(phase_final == "Maintain / equity") %>% pull(country)
      s_main <- inv %>% filter(phase_s == "Maintain / equity") %>% pull(country)

      tibble(
        scenario = paste0("Fixed s=", s_chr),
        spearman_rank_corr = cor(cmp$rank_final, cmp$rank_s, method = "spearman"),
        mean_abs_rank_shift = mean(abs(cmp$rank_final - cmp$rank_s)),
        pct_phase_changed = mean(cmp$phase_changed) * 100,
        scaleup_jaccard = length(intersect(final_scale, s_scale)) / length(union(final_scale, s_scale)),
        maintain_jaccard = length(intersect(final_main, s_main)) / length(union(final_main, s_main))
      )
    })
  )

  knitr::kable(
    fixeds_stability_tbl %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
    caption = "Appendix: phase and ranking stability of fixed-s models vs the final model"
  )

  phase_grid_plot <- bind_rows(
    investment_table %>% transmute(country, scenario = "Final model", phase),
    fixeds_assignments %>% transmute(country, scenario, phase)
  )

  country_order <- investment_table %>% arrange(desc(leverage)) %>% pull(country)

  ggplot(
    phase_grid_plot %>%
      mutate(
        country = factor(country, levels = rev(country_order)),
        phase = factor(phase, levels = c("Scale-up", "Build / monitor", "Maintain / equity")),
        scenario = factor(scenario, levels = c("Final model", paste0("Fixed s=", fixed_s_grid)))
      ),
    aes(x = scenario, y = country, fill = phase)
  ) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "Scale-up" = "#4A80E8",
      "Build / monitor" = "#F06A6A",
      "Maintain / equity" = "#2DA44E"
    )) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    labs(
      title = "Appendix: phase assignment stability across speed assumptions",
      subtitle = "Country order follows final-model leverage ranking",
      x = "Scenario",
      y = "Country"
    )
}
```

## Appendix C: Internal Check on the Region-fixed \(K\) Implementation

This check re-imposes the same region-fixed ceilings used in the alternative final model while keeping final-model \(t_0\) and \(s\) posteriors unchanged. The expected result is near-identity with the main decision outputs, which confirms that the report is treating \(K\) as a fixed regional input throughout.

```{r appendix-kfixed-check}
k_region_lookup <- wb_region_prior_inputs %>%
  transmute(
    region_prior,
    K_region_fixed = pmin(pmax(median_K, 0.05), 0.98)
  ) %>%
  select(region_prior, K_region_fixed)

country_region_tbl <- nd_country %>%
  select(country, region_prior) %>%
  left_join(k_region_lookup, by = "region_prior") %>%
  arrange(country)

if (!all(country_region_tbl$country == countries)) {
  stop("Country order mismatch in appendix K-fixed check.")
}

if (any(is.na(country_region_tbl$K_region_fixed))) {
  stop("Missing regional fixed-K values for one or more countries in appendix K-fixed check.")
}

# Re-apply the same region-fixed K values used in the main model while keeping final-model t0 and s.
K_draws_region_fixed <- matrix(
  rep(country_region_tbl$K_region_fixed, each = nrow(t0_draws)),
  nrow = nrow(t0_draws)
)

t_dec <- 2026 - 2000
t_mat_final <- matrix(t_dec, nrow = nrow(t0_draws), ncol = ncol(t0_draws))
z_mat_kfixed <- exp(-(t_mat_final - t0_draws) / s_draws)
p_mat_kfixed <- K_draws_region_fixed / (1 + z_mat_kfixed)
dpdt_mat_kfixed <- (K_draws_region_fixed / s_draws) * z_mat_kfixed / (1 + z_mat_kfixed)^2

country_summary_kfixed <- tibble(
  country = country_region_tbl$country,
  decision_year = 2026,
  K_mean = colMeans(K_draws_region_fixed),
  p_mean = colMeans(p_mat_kfixed),
  head_mean = colMeans(K_draws_region_fixed - p_mat_kfixed),
  rel_mean = colMeans(p_mat_kfixed / K_draws_region_fixed),
  dpdt_mean = colMeans(dpdt_mat_kfixed)
)

investment_kfixed <- classify_investment_from_summary(country_summary_kfixed) %>%
  mutate(rank_kfixed = row_number()) %>%
  select(country, phase_kfixed = phase, rank_kfixed)

final_rank <- investment_table %>%
  arrange(desc(leverage)) %>%
  mutate(rank_final = row_number()) %>%
  select(country, phase_final = phase, rank_final)

cmp_kfixed <- final_rank %>%
  inner_join(investment_kfixed, by = "country") %>%
  mutate(phase_changed = phase_final != phase_kfixed)

final_scale <- final_rank %>% filter(phase_final == "Scale-up") %>% pull(country)
kfix_scale <- investment_kfixed %>% filter(phase_kfixed == "Scale-up") %>% pull(country)

kfixed_stability <- tibble(
  scenario = "Re-applied region-fixed K (holding final-model t0 and s)",
  spearman_rank_corr = cor(cmp_kfixed$rank_final, cmp_kfixed$rank_kfixed, method = "spearman"),
  mean_abs_rank_shift = mean(abs(cmp_kfixed$rank_final - cmp_kfixed$rank_kfixed)),
  pct_phase_changed = mean(cmp_kfixed$phase_changed) * 100,
  scaleup_jaccard = length(intersect(final_scale, kfix_scale)) / length(union(final_scale, kfix_scale))
)

knitr::kable(
  kfixed_stability %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
  caption = "Appendix: sensitivity to fixing K by region"
)
```

```{r appendix-sensitivity-summary}
if (exists("fixeds_stability_tbl") && exists("kfixed_stability")) {
  fixeds_min_corr <- min(fixeds_stability_tbl$spearman_rank_corr, na.rm = TRUE)
  fixeds_max_phase_change <- max(fixeds_stability_tbl$pct_phase_changed, na.rm = TRUE)
  kfixed_corr <- kfixed_stability$spearman_rank_corr[1]
  kfixed_phase_change <- kfixed_stability$pct_phase_changed[1]

  cat(
    "Fixed-s sensitivity summary: minimum Spearman rank correlation vs the final model =",
    round(fixeds_min_corr, 3),
    "and maximum phase change rate =",
    round(fixeds_max_phase_change, 1), "%.\n"
  )
  cat(
    "K-fixed sensitivity summary: Spearman rank correlation vs the final model =",
    round(kfixed_corr, 3),
    "and phase change rate =",
    round(kfixed_phase_change, 1), "%.\n"
  )
}
```

Interpretation:
a) If fixed-\(s\) scenarios remain close to the final model (high rank correlation, low phase switching), phase rules are not overly sensitive to the country-varying speed component.
b) The region-fixed \(K\) re-application check should be nearly identical to the final model, because the main specification already treats \(K\) as a fixed regional input.

## Appendix D: Bayesian Power (Assurance) for a New Survey

This appendix uses **assurance**: the probability that a future survey will meet a pre-specified posterior decision rule under the current model.

Decision rule in this check:
a) target year: 2028
b) meaningful increase threshold: \(\Delta = 0.03\) (3 percentage points)
c) success rule: \(\Pr(p_{2028} - p_{\text{last obs}} \ge \Delta \mid \text{new survey}) \ge 0.80\)

Scope:
a) this is a fast design-stage approximation using Beta-Binomial updating for the *new* survey proportion;
b) a full operating-characteristic study would re-fit the full hierarchical model for each simulated dataset.

What this appendix is doing:
a) For each selected country, it first takes the fitted final model and extracts the posterior distribution of that country's projected mCPR in 2028.
b) It treats those posterior draws as plausible "true" 2028 mCPR values under the current model.
c) For each candidate new survey sample size (\(n=500,1000,\dots,3000\)), it repeatedly simulates what a new survey could observe if the true 2028 mCPR were one of those plausible values.
d) After each simulated survey, it updates only the new-survey proportion with a simple Beta-Binomial posterior, rather than re-fitting the full hierarchical model.
e) It then checks whether that simulated survey would be strong enough to conclude, with at least 80% posterior probability, that 2028 mCPR is at least 3 percentage points above the country's latest observed mCPR.
f) The reported assurance is the share of simulated surveys that meet that decision rule, so it answers: "if the current model is roughly right, how often would a new survey of size \(n\) be persuasive enough?"

How to read the outputs:
a) In the table, `assurance` is the main quantity: values near 1 mean the planned survey size is very likely to support the decision rule; values near 0 mean it usually will not.
b) `threshold_mcpr` is the country-specific target level, defined as the latest observed mCPR plus 3 percentage points.
c) `median_post_prob` is the median posterior probability, across simulations, that the threshold is exceeded after seeing the new survey.
d) In the line plot, the dashed horizontal line at 0.80 is a practical design benchmark: sample sizes above that line are more likely to deliver a convincing result under this rule.

```{r appendix-bayesian-assurance}
# Toggle this section if you want to skip assurance calculations in fast reruns.
run_bayesian_assurance <- TRUE

if (run_bayesian_assurance) {
  set.seed(20260225)

  # Settings for the assurance scenario.
  assurance_year <- 2028
  delta_increase <- 0.03
  posterior_cut <- 0.80
  n_sim <- 2000
  n_grid <- c(500, 1000, 1500, 2000, 3000)
  assurance_countries <- c("Nigeria", "India", "Ethiopia", "Pakistan", "Kenya")

  available_countries <- sort(unique(mcpr_logistic_data$country))
  assurance_countries <- assurance_countries[assurance_countries %in% available_countries]
  if (length(assurance_countries) == 0) {
    stop("No requested assurance countries are present in mcpr_logistic_data.")
  }

  # Build prediction frame for country-specific p(year) at the assurance year.
  nd_assurance <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
    distinct(country, region_prior, K_region_fixed) %>%
    filter(country %in% assurance_countries) %>%
    arrange(country) %>%
    mutate(
      country = as.character(country),
      region_prior = as.character(region_prior),
      year = assurance_year,
      year_center = assurance_year - 2000,
      n = 1L,
      y = 0L
    )

  # Posterior draws of country-specific p(assurance_year) from the fitted model.
  p_assurance_draws <- posterior_epred(
    fit_logistic,
    newdata = nd_assurance,
    re_formula = NULL
  )
  # `posterior_epred()` can return:
  # a) a vector for one newdata row,
  # b) a draws x observations matrix for one-response models,
  # c) a draws x observations x categories array in some families.
  # This block standardizes everything to a draws x observations matrix
  # without relying on dimension-specific slicing.
  assurance_dims <- dim(p_assurance_draws)
  if (is.null(assurance_dims)) {
    if (nrow(nd_assurance) != 1L) {
      stop("Unexpected one-dimensional posterior_epred output for multiple assurance rows.")
    }
    p_assurance_draws <- matrix(p_assurance_draws, ncol = 1L)
  } else {
    p_assurance_draws <- matrix(
      p_assurance_draws,
      nrow = assurance_dims[1],
      ncol = prod(assurance_dims[-1])
    )
  }

  if (ncol(p_assurance_draws) != nrow(nd_assurance)) {
    stop("Assurance prediction columns do not match assurance newdata rows.")
  }

  # Baseline is each country's latest observed mCPR.
  latest_obs_assurance <- mcpr_logistic_data %>%
    group_by(country) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    transmute(country, last_year = year, last_mcpr = mCPR) %>%
    filter(country %in% assurance_countries) %>%
    arrange(country)

  # Align baseline to prediction column order.
  latest_obs_assurance <- latest_obs_assurance %>%
    right_join(nd_assurance %>% select(country) %>% distinct() %>% arrange(country), by = "country")

  # Simulation-based assurance calculation.
  assurance_results <- bind_rows(
    lapply(seq_len(nrow(nd_assurance)), function(j) {
      country_j <- nd_assurance$country[j]
      p_draw_j <- as.numeric(p_assurance_draws[, j])
      p_draw_j <- pmin(pmax(p_draw_j, 0.001), 0.999)

      base_j <- latest_obs_assurance$last_mcpr[latest_obs_assurance$country == country_j][1]
      thresh_j <- pmin(base_j + delta_increase, 0.999)

      bind_rows(lapply(n_grid, function(n_new) {
        # Sample plausible truths from the current model and simulate a new survey.
        # This approximates many future surveys we might observe at sample size n_new.
        p_true <- sample(p_draw_j, size = n_sim, replace = TRUE)
        y_new <- rbinom(n_sim, size = n_new, prob = p_true)

        # Approximate posterior for new survey proportion: Beta(1,1) prior + Binomial likelihood.
        # This step asks: after seeing y_new out of n_new, how much posterior support
        # is there that true 2028 mCPR exceeds the target threshold?
        post_prob_success <- 1 - pbeta(thresh_j, y_new + 1, n_new - y_new + 1)
        decision_success <- post_prob_success >= posterior_cut

        tibble(
          country = country_j,
          n_new = n_new,
          last_year = latest_obs_assurance$last_year[latest_obs_assurance$country == country_j][1],
          last_mcpr = base_j,
          threshold_mcpr = thresh_j,
          assurance = mean(decision_success),
          median_post_prob = median(post_prob_success),
          p2028_model_median = median(p_draw_j)
        )
      }))
    })
  ) %>%
    arrange(country, n_new)

  knitr::kable(
    assurance_results %>%
      mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))),
    caption = "Appendix: Bayesian assurance for detecting a >=3pp increase by 2028"
  )

  ggplot(assurance_results, aes(x = n_new, y = assurance, color = country)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    labs(
      title = "Bayesian assurance vs new-survey sample size",
      subtitle = "Success rule: posterior Pr(>=3pp increase by 2028) >= 0.80",
      x = "New survey sample size (n)",
      y = "Assurance (probability decision rule is met)"
    )
}
```

## Export

```{r export}
write.csv(investment_table, "mcpr_investment_table_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(investment_table_lastobs, "mcpr_investment_table_lastobs_headroom_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(latest_projection_table, "mcpr_latest_observed_vs_projection_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(time_to_k_table, "mcpr_time_to_99pct_k_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(country_growth_rate_table, "mcpr_country_growth_rates_over_time_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(acceleration_table, "mcpr_acceleration_status_2026_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(s_curve_stage_table, "mcpr_s_curve_stage_classification_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(s_curve_stage_2026_table, "mcpr_s_curve_stage_classification_2026_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ssa_target40_table, "mcpr_ssa_target40_summary_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ssa_summary_table, "mcpr_ssa_planning_summary_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ssa_obs_growth_intervals, "mcpr_ssa_observed_interval_growth_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ssa_region_goals_summary, "mcpr_ssa_region_goals_summary_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ssa_goals_country_table, "mcpr_ssa_country_goals_summary_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ssa_regional_influence_table, "mcpr_ssa_regional_influence_summary_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)

  write.csv(fixeds_stability_tbl, "mcpr_final_model_fixeds_phase_stability_region_fixedK_countryS.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}

}
```
